[[Ukanomitama no Mikoto]] |established={{ill|Ōei era|ja|応永}} (circa 1400) |shrine_rank=Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |annual_festival=See [[#Festivals]] |main_hall= |map={{location map|Japan Ibaraki|label=Wakamiya Hachimangū|coordinates= {{coord2|36|32|34.8|N|140|31|14.6|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|32|34.8|N|140|31|14.6|E}}}} }} [[File:Torii of Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|thumb|right|245px|Torii]] [[File:Shinmon of Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|thumb|right|245px|Shinmon (gate)]] '''Wakamiya Hachimangū''' (若宮八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Hitachiōta|ja|常陸太田市}}, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県}}, Japan. The shrine's rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度}} was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == History == The shrine was established during the {{ill|Ōei era|ja|応永}} (circa 1400) by {{ill|Yoshihito Satake|ja|佐竹義人}}, who invited its deity from {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|ja|鶴岡八幡宮}} in {{ill|Kamakura|ja|鎌倉市}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}. It was originally enshrined within {{ill|Ōta Castle (Hitachi Province)|ja|太田城 (常陸国)}} as a guardian deity and served as the prayer site for the {{ill|Satake clan|ja|佐竹氏}}. Research suggests that the Satake clan initially revered a {{ill|Hachiman shrine|ja|八幡社}} derived from {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮}}, which was the guardian shrine of the Minamoto clan in Hitachi. However, Yoshihito Satake, who had ties with the {{ill|Uesugi clan|ja|上杉氏}}, instead adopted the enshrinement of Tsurugaoka Hachimangū, possibly against opposition from the Satake family.Teiko Tsutsumi, "Satake Clan and Hachiman Worship," {{ill|Ibaraki Prefectural History Museum Bulletin|ja|茨城県立歴史館報}} No. 28, 2001. Compiled in {{ill|Research Series on Medieval Kanto Warriors Vol. 30: Hitachi Satake Clan|ja|シリーズ・中世関東武士の研究 第三十巻 常陸佐竹氏}} (Kōshōshō Publishing, 2021), pp. 192–227. Although the Satake clan was later relocated to {{ill|Akita|ja|秋田}} after the {{ill|Battle of Sekigahara|ja|関ヶ原の戦い}}, they took the shrine's deity with them to {{ill|Ōdate Castle|ja|大館城}} as its guardian spirit. The deity was subsequently enshrined at {{ill|Ōdate Hachiman Shrine|ja|大館八幡神社}}, continuing its role as a protector.{{Cite web|title=八幡神社 |url=http://akita-jinjacho.sakura.ne.jp/tatsujin_etc/kennsaku/oodate/67_hachiman_hachiman.html |publisher=Akita Prefecture Shrine Association |accessdate=2015-10-12}} In {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} 14 (1609), the first {{ill|daimyō|ja|大名}} of the {{ill|Mito Domain|ja|水戸藩}}, {{ill|Tokugawa Yorifusa|ja|徳川頼房}}, was healed of an illness after praying at the shrine. Thereafter, the {{ill|Mito Tokugawa clan|ja|水戸徳川家}} held the shrine in high reverence. In {{ill|Genroku|ja|元禄}} 5 (1692), the second daimyō, {{ill|Tokugawa Mitsukuni|ja|徳川光圀}} (popularly known as Mito Kōmon), designated the shrine as a guardian of the Ōta district. In {{ill|Hōei|ja|宝永}} 5 (1708), the shrine was relocated to its current location alongside the {{ill|Ōta Inari Shrine|ja|太田稲荷神社}}, which had been worshiped together with it. In 1940 (Showa 15), based on the tradition that a woman named Tsuruko accompanied the deity’s transfer from Tsurugaoka Hachimangū and oversaw its rituals, the shrine's parishioner priestesses revived the "Tsuruko Dance." Recently, this {{ill|kagura|ja|神楽}} offering has been expanded to include performances such as {{ill|Urayasu no Mai|ja|浦安の舞}}, {{ill|Toyo no Sakai no Mai|ja|豊栄の舞}}, and {{ill|Yaotome no Mai|ja|八乙女舞}}, which are performed with grace during major festivals. [[File:Kaguraden of Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|thumb|right|245px|Kaguraden]] == Enshrined Deities == * '''[[Ōsasagi no Mikoto]]''' (Emperor Nintoku) * '''[[Ukanomitama no Mikoto]]''' == Festivals == * January: {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|元旦祭|Saitan-sai=Saitan-sai|lt=Saitan-sai (New Year's Festival)}} * February: {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分追儺祭|lt=Setsubun Tsuina-sai}} * May: festival * June: {{ill|Nagoshi no Harae|ja|夏越の祓}} * September: Autumn Festival * December: {{ill|Ōharae|ja|大祓}} == Cultural Properties == * Large {{ill|zelkova|ja|ケヤキ}} tree (designated a natural monument of Ibaraki Prefecture) * Hanging scroll of Hachiman as a monk * Stone sword artifacts * Military fan (attributed to {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|ja|源義光}}) == Auxiliary Shrines == * {{ill|Kitano Tenjin Shrine|ja|北野天神社}} (Plum Tenjin) – Established in {{ill|Kōhei 6|ja|康平}} (1062) by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|ja|源義家}} to pray for victory in the {{ill|Earlier Nine Years' War|ja|前九年の役}}. * {{ill|Ichihime Shrine|ja|市比売神社}} * {{ill|Ashio Shrine|ja|足尾神社}} * {{ill|Atago Shrine|ja|愛宕神社}} * {{ill|Wakazumi Shrine|ja|別雷神社}} * {{ill|Fuji Shrine|ja|富士神社}} * {{ill|Awashima Shrine|ja|淡島神社}} File:Ota-tenmangu in Wakamiya-hachimanhu (Hitachiota).jpg|Kitano Tenjin Shrine File:Keidaisha1 in Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|Ichihime Shrine, Wakazumi Shrine, Atago Shrine File:Ashio-jinja in Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|Ashio Shrine File:Fuji-jinja in Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|Fuji Shrine == Access == The shrine is approximately 1.5 km north of {{ill|Hitachi-Ōta Station|ja|常陸太田駅}} on the {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|ja|東日本旅客鉄道}} (JR). It is a 20-minute walk. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Shinto shrines|ja|神社一覧}} * {{ill|List of Japanese deities|ja|日本の神の一覧}} == External Links == * {{Instagram|wakamiyah}} * {{Twitter|wakamiya_h}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Ibaraki Prefecture Shrine Index}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Former Gōsha]] [[Category:Satake clan]] [[Category:Architecture in Hitachiōta]] [[Category:History of Hitachiōta]] [[Category:Emperor Nintoku]] [[zh:若宮八幡宮]] [[ja:%E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E5%AE%AE_(%E5%B8%B8%E9%99%B8%E5%A4%AA%E7%94%B0%E5%B8%82)]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)-qq]] [[Category:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]][[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)-qq]] [[Category:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]][[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:Subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]][[Category:qq]]

Recently, this {{ill|kagura|ja|神楽}} offering has been expanded to include performances such as {{ill|Urayasu no Mai|ja|浦安の舞}}, {{ill|Toyo no Sakai no Mai|ja|豊栄の舞}}, and {{ill|Yaotome no Mai|ja|八乙女舞}}, which are performed with grace during major festivals. [[File:Kaguraden of Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|thumb|right|245px|Kaguraden]] == Enshrined Deities == * '''[[Ōsasagi no Mikoto]]''' (Emperor Nintoku) * '''[[Ukanomitama no Mikoto]]''' == Festivals == * January: {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|元旦祭|Saitan-sai=Saitan-sai|lt=Saitan-sai (New Year's Festival)}} * February: {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分追儺祭|lt=Setsubun Tsuina-sai}} * May: festival * June: {{ill|Nagoshi no Harae|ja|夏越の祓}} * September: Autumn Festival * December: {{ill|Ōharae|ja|大祓}} == Cultural Properties == * Large {{ill|zelkova|ja|ケヤキ}} tree (designated a natural monument of Ibaraki Prefecture) * Hanging scroll of Hachiman as a monk * Stone sword artifacts * Military fan (attributed to {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|ja|源義光}}) == Auxiliary Shrines == * {{ill|Kitano Tenjin Shrine|ja|北野天神社}} (Plum Tenjin) – Established in {{ill|Kōhei 6|ja|康平}} (1062) by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|ja|源義家}} to pray for victory in the {{ill|Earlier Nine Years' War|ja|前九年の役}}. * {{ill|Ichihime Shrine|ja|市比売神社}} * {{ill|Ashio Shrine|ja|足尾神社}} * {{ill|Atago Shrine|ja|愛宕神社}} * {{ill|Wakazumi Shrine|ja|別雷神社}} * {{ill|Fuji Shrine|ja|富士神社}} * {{ill|Awashima Shrine|ja|淡島神社}} File:Ota-tenmangu in Wakamiya-hachimanhu (Hitachiota).jpg|Kitano Tenjin Shrine File:Keidaisha1 in Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|Ichihime Shrine, Wakazumi Shrine, Atago Shrine File:Ashio-jinja in Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|Ashio Shrine File:Fuji-jinja in Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|Fuji Shrine == Access == The shrine is approximately 1.5 km north of {{ill|Hitachi-Ōta Station|ja|常陸太田駅}} on the {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|ja|東日本旅客鉄道}} (JR). It is a 20-minute walk. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Shinto shrines|ja|神社一覧}} * {{ill|List of Japanese deities|ja|日本の神の一覧}} == External Links == * {{Instagram|wakamiyah}} * {{Twitter|wakamiya_h}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Ibaraki Prefecture Shrine Index}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Former Gōsha]] [[Category:Satake clan]] [[Category:Architecture in Hitachiōta]] [[Category:History of Hitachiōta]] [[Category:Emperor Nintoku]] [[zh:若宮八幡宮]] [[ja:%E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E5%AE%AE_(%E5%B8%B8%E9%99%B8%E5%A4%AA%E7%94%B0%E5%B8%82)]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)-qq]] [[Category:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]][[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)-qq]] [[Category:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]][[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:Sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:trimming off this bad category]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]][[Category:trimming off this bad category]]

{{Cite sources|date=2019-03-12}} {{Shrine |name=Wakamiya Hachimangū (若宮八幡宮) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Wakamiyahachimaｎgu.JPG|250px]]
Main hall (September 2005) |location={{ill|Hitachiōta|ja|常陸太田市}}, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県}} Miyamoto-chō 2344 |coordinates={{coord2|36|32|34.8|N|140|31|14.6|E|type:landmark_region:JP-08|display=inline,title|name=Wakamiya Hachimangū}} |enshrined_deities=[[Ōsasagi no Mikoto]] ({{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}})
[[Ukanomitama no Mikoto]] |established={{ill|Ōei era|ja|応永}} (circa 1400) |shrine_rank=Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |annual_festival=See [[#Festivals]] |main_hall= |map={{location map|Japan Ibaraki|label=Wakamiya Hachimangū|coordinates= {{coord2|36|32|34.8|N|140|31|14.6|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|32|34.8|N|140|31|14.6|E}}}} }} [[File:Torii of Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|thumb|right|245px|Torii]] [[File:Shinmon of Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|thumb|right|245px|Shinmon (gate)]] '''Wakamiya Hachimangū''' (若宮八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Hitachiōta|ja|常陸太田市}}, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県}}, Japan. The shrine's rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度}} was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == History == The shrine was established during the {{ill|Ōei era|ja|応永}} (circa 1400) by {{ill|Yoshihito Satake|ja|佐竹義人}}, who invited its deity from {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|ja|鶴岡八幡宮}} in {{ill|Kamakura|ja|鎌倉市}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}. It was originally enshrined within {{ill|Ōta Castle (Hitachi Province)|ja|太田城 (常陸国)}} as a guardian deity and served as the prayer site for the {{ill|Satake clan|ja|佐竹氏}}. Research suggests that the Satake clan initially revered a {{ill|Hachiman shrine|ja|八幡社}} derived from {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮}}, which was the guardian shrine of the Minamoto clan in Hitachi. However, Yoshihito Satake, who had ties with the {{ill|Uesugi clan|ja|上杉氏}}, instead adopted the enshrinement of Tsurugaoka Hachimangū, possibly against opposition from the Satake family.Teiko Tsutsumi, "Satake Clan and Hachiman Worship," {{ill|Ibaraki Prefectural History Museum Bulletin|ja|茨城県立歴史館報}} No. 28, 2001. Compiled in {{ill|Research Series on Medieval Kanto Warriors Vol. 30: Hitachi Satake Clan|ja|シリーズ・中世関東武士の研究 第三十巻 常陸佐竹氏}} (Kōshōshō Publishing, 2021), pp. 192–227. Although the Satake clan was later relocated to {{ill|Akita|ja|秋田}} after the {{ill|Battle of Sekigahara|ja|関ヶ原の戦い}}, they took the shrine's deity with them to {{ill|Ōdate Castle|ja|大館城}} as its guardian spirit. The deity was subsequently enshrined at {{ill|Ōdate Hachiman Shrine|ja|大館八幡神社}}, continuing its role as a protector.{{Cite web|title=八幡神社 |url=http://akita-jinjacho.sakura.ne.jp/tatsujin_etc/kennsaku/oodate/67_hachiman_hachiman.html |publisher=Akita Prefecture Shrine Association |accessdate=2015-10-12}} In {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} 14 (1609), the first {{ill|daimyō|ja|大名}} of the {{ill|Mito Domain|ja|水戸藩}}, {{ill|Tokugawa Yorifusa|ja|徳川頼房}}, was healed of an illness after praying at the shrine. Thereafter, the {{ill|Mito Tokugawa clan|ja|水戸徳川家}} held the shrine in high reverence. In {{ill|Genroku|ja|元禄}} 5 (1692), the second daimyō, {{ill|Tokugawa Mitsukuni|ja|徳川光圀}} (popularly known as Mito Kōmon), designated the shrine as a guardian of the Ōta district. In {{ill|Hōei|ja|宝永}} 5 (1708), the shrine was relocated to its current location alongside the {{ill|Ōta Inari Shrine|ja|太田稲荷神社}}, which had been worshiped together with it. In 1940 (Showa 15), based on the tradition that a woman named Tsuruko accompanied the deity’s transfer from Tsurugaoka Hachimangū and oversaw its rituals, the shrine's parishioner priestesses revived the "Tsuruko Dance." Recently, this {{ill|kagura|ja|神楽}} offering has been expanded to include performances such as {{ill|Urayasu no Mai|ja|浦安の舞}}, {{ill|Toyo no Sakai no Mai|ja|豊栄の舞}}, and {{ill|Yaotome no Mai|ja|八乙女舞}}, which are performed with grace during major festivals. [[File:Kaguraden of Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|thumb|right|245px|Kaguraden]] == Enshrined Deities == * '''[[Ōsasagi no Mikoto]]''' (Emperor Nintoku) * '''[[Ukanomitama no Mikoto]]''' == Festivals == * January: {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|元旦祭|Saitan-sai=Saitan-sai|lt=Saitan-sai (New Year's Festival)}} * February: {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分追儺祭|lt=Setsubun Tsuina-sai}} * May: festival * June: {{ill|Nagoshi no Harae|ja|夏越の祓}} * September: Autumn Festival * December: {{ill|Ōharae|ja|大祓}} == Cultural Properties == * Large {{ill|zelkova|ja|ケヤキ}} tree (designated a natural monument of Ibaraki Prefecture) * Hanging scroll of Hachiman as a monk * Stone sword artifacts * Military fan (attributed to {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|ja|源義光}}) == Auxiliary Shrines == * {{ill|Kitano Tenjin Shrine|ja|北野天神社}} (Plum Tenjin) – Established in {{ill|Kōhei 6|ja|康平}} (1062) by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|ja|源義家}} to pray for victory in the {{ill|Earlier Nine Years' War|ja|前九年の役}}. * {{ill|Ichihime Shrine|ja|市比売神社}} * {{ill|Ashio Shrine|ja|足尾神社}} * {{ill|Atago Shrine|ja|愛宕神社}} * {{ill|Wakazumi Shrine|ja|別雷神社}} * {{ill|Fuji Shrine|ja|富士神社}} * {{ill|Awashima Shrine|ja|淡島神社}} File:Ota-tenmangu in Wakamiya-hachimanhu (Hitachiota).jpg|Kitano Tenjin Shrine File:Keidaisha1 in Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|Ichihime Shrine, Wakazumi Shrine, Atago Shrine File:Ashio-jinja in Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|Ashio Shrine File:Fuji-jinja in Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|Fuji Shrine == Access == The shrine is approximately 1.5 km north of {{ill|Hitachi-Ōta Station|ja|常陸太田駅}} on the {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|ja|東日本旅客鉄道}} (JR). It is a 20-minute walk. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Shinto shrines|ja|神社一覧}} * {{ill|List of Japanese deities|ja|日本の神の一覧}} == External Links == * {{Instagram|wakamiyah}} * {{Twitter|wakamiya_h}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Ibaraki Prefecture Shrine Index}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Former Gōsha]] [[Category:Satake clan]] [[Category:Architecture in Hitachiōta]] [[Category:History of Hitachiōta]] [[Category:Emperor Nintoku]] [[zh:若宮八幡宮]] [[ja:%E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E5%AE%AE_(%E5%B8%B8%E9%99%B8%E5%A4%AA%E7%94%B0%E5%B8%82)]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)-qq]] [[Category:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]][[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)-qq]] [[Category:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]][[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:Sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:trimming off this bad category]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]][[Category:trimming off this bad category]] [[Category:qqqq]]

{{Cite sources|date=2019-03-12}} {{Shrine |name=Wakamiya Hachimangū (若宮八幡宮) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Wakamiyahachimaｎgu.JPG|250px]]
Main hall (September 2005) |location={{ill|Hitachiōta|ja|常陸太田市}}, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県}} Miyamoto-chō 2344 |coordinates={{coord2|36|32|34.8|N|140|31|14.6|E|type:landmark_region:JP-08|display=inline,title|name=Wakamiya Hachimangū}} |enshrined_deities=[[Ōsasagi no Mikoto]] ({{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}})
[[Ukanomitama no Mikoto]] |established={{ill|Ōei era|ja|応永}} (circa 1400) |shrine_rank=Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |annual_festival=See [[#Festivals]] |main_hall= |map={{location map|Japan Ibaraki|label=Wakamiya Hachimangū|coordinates= {{coord2|36|32|34.8|N|140|31|14.6|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|32|34.8|N|140|31|14.6|E}}}} }} [[File:Torii of Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|thumb|right|245px|Torii]] [[File:Shinmon of Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|thumb|right|245px|Shinmon (gate)]] '''Wakamiya Hachimangū''' (若宮八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Hitachiōta|ja|常陸太田市}}, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県}}, Japan. The shrine's rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度}} was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == History == The shrine was established during the {{ill|Ōei era|ja|応永}} (circa 1400) by {{ill|Yoshihito Satake|ja|佐竹義人}}, who invited its deity from {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|ja|鶴岡八幡宮}} in {{ill|Kamakura|ja|鎌倉市}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}. It was originally enshrined within {{ill|Ōta Castle (Hitachi Province)|ja|太田城 (常陸国)}} as a guardian deity and served as the prayer site for the {{ill|Satake clan|ja|佐竹氏}}. Research suggests that the Satake clan initially revered a {{ill|Hachiman shrine|ja|八幡社}} derived from {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮}}, which was the guardian shrine of the Minamoto clan in Hitachi. However, Yoshihito Satake, who had ties with the {{ill|Uesugi clan|ja|上杉氏}}, instead adopted the enshrinement of Tsurugaoka Hachimangū, possibly against opposition from the Satake family.Teiko Tsutsumi, "Satake Clan and Hachiman Worship," {{ill|Ibaraki Prefectural History Museum Bulletin|ja|茨城県立歴史館報}} No. 28, 2001. Compiled in {{ill|Research Series on Medieval Kanto Warriors Vol. 30: Hitachi Satake Clan|ja|シリーズ・中世関東武士の研究 第三十巻 常陸佐竹氏}} (Kōshōshō Publishing, 2021), pp. 192–227. Although the Satake clan was later relocated to {{ill|Akita|ja|秋田}} after the {{ill|Battle of Sekigahara|ja|関ヶ原の戦い}}, they took the shrine's deity with them to {{ill|Ōdate Castle|ja|大館城}} as its guardian spirit. The deity was subsequently enshrined at {{ill|Ōdate Hachiman Shrine|ja|大館八幡神社}}, continuing its role as a protector.{{Cite web|title=八幡神社 |url=http://akita-jinjacho.sakura.ne.jp/tatsujin_etc/kennsaku/oodate/67_hachiman_hachiman.html |publisher=Akita Prefecture Shrine Association |accessdate=2015-10-12}} In {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} 14 (1609), the first {{ill|daimyō|ja|大名}} of the {{ill|Mito Domain|ja|水戸藩}}, {{ill|Tokugawa Yorifusa|ja|徳川頼房}}, was healed of an illness after praying at the shrine. Thereafter, the {{ill|Mito Tokugawa clan|ja|水戸徳川家}} held the shrine in high reverence. In {{ill|Genroku|ja|元禄}} 5 (1692), the second daimyō, {{ill|Tokugawa Mitsukuni|ja|徳川光圀}} (popularly known as Mito Kōmon), designated the shrine as a guardian of the Ōta district. In {{ill|Hōei|ja|宝永}} 5 (1708), the shrine was relocated to its current location alongside the {{ill|Ōta Inari Shrine|ja|太田稲荷神社}}, which had been worshiped together with it. In 1940 (Showa 15), based on the tradition that a woman named Tsuruko accompanied the deity’s transfer from Tsurugaoka Hachimangū and oversaw its rituals, the shrine's parishioner priestesses revived the "Tsuruko Dance." Recently, this {{ill|kagura|ja|神楽}} offering has been expanded to include performances such as {{ill|Urayasu no Mai|ja|浦安の舞}}, {{ill|Toyo no Sakai no Mai|ja|豊栄の舞}}, and {{ill|Yaotome no Mai|ja|八乙女舞}}, which are performed with grace during major festivals. [[File:Kaguraden of Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|thumb|right|245px|Kaguraden]] == Enshrined Deities == * '''[[Ōsasagi no Mikoto]]''' (Emperor Nintoku) * '''[[Ukanomitama no Mikoto]]''' == Festivals == * January: {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|元旦祭|Saitan-sai=Saitan-sai|lt=Saitan-sai (New Year's Festival)}} * February: {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分追儺祭|lt=Setsubun Tsuina-sai}} * May: festival * June: {{ill|Nagoshi no Harae|ja|夏越の祓}} * September: Autumn Festival * December: {{ill|Ōharae|ja|大祓}} == Cultural Properties == * Large {{ill|zelkova|ja|ケヤキ}} tree (designated a natural monument of Ibaraki Prefecture) * Hanging scroll of Hachiman as a monk * Stone sword artifacts * Military fan (attributed to {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|ja|源義光}}) == Auxiliary Shrines == * {{ill|Kitano Tenjin Shrine|ja|北野天神社}} (Plum Tenjin) – Established in {{ill|Kōhei 6|ja|康平}} (1062) by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|ja|源義家}} to pray for victory in the {{ill|Earlier Nine Years' War|ja|前九年の役}}. * {{ill|Ichihime Shrine|ja|市比売神社}} * {{ill|Ashio Shrine|ja|足尾神社}} * {{ill|Atago Shrine|ja|愛宕神社}} * {{ill|Wakazumi Shrine|ja|別雷神社}} * {{ill|Fuji Shrine|ja|富士神社}} * {{ill|Awashima Shrine|ja|淡島神社}} File:Ota-tenmangu in Wakamiya-hachimanhu (Hitachiota).jpg|Kitano Tenjin Shrine File:Keidaisha1 in Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|Ichihime Shrine, Wakazumi Shrine, Atago Shrine File:Ashio-jinja in Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|Ashio Shrine File:Fuji-jinja in Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|Fuji Shrine == Access == The shrine is approximately 1.5 km north of {{ill|Hitachi-Ōta Station|ja|常陸太田駅}} on the {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|ja|東日本旅客鉄道}} (JR). It is a 20-minute walk. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Shinto shrines|ja|神社一覧}} * {{ill|List of Japanese deities|ja|日本の神の一覧}} == External Links == * {{Instagram|wakamiyah}} * {{Twitter|wakamiya_h}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Ibaraki Prefecture Shrine Index}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Former Gōsha]] [[Category:Satake clan]] [[Category:Architecture in Hitachiōta]] [[Category:History of Hitachiōta]] [[Category:Emperor Nintoku]] [[zh:若宮八幡宮]] [[ja:%E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E5%AE%AE_(%E5%B8%B8%E9%99%B8%E5%A4%AA%E7%94%B0%E5%B8%82)]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)}} [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)-qq]] [[Category:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]][[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)}} [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)-qq]] [[Category:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]][[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:Sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:trimming off this bad category]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]][[Category:trimming off this bad category]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:if these are dates then move them to completion tier 5]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]][[Category:if these are dates then move them to completion tier 5]]

[[File:Kaguraden of Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|thumb|right|245px|Kaguraden]] == Enshrined Deities == * '''[[Ōsasagi no Mikoto]]''' (Emperor Nintoku) * '''[[Ukanomitama no Mikoto]]''' == Festivals == * January: {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|元旦祭|Saitan-sai=Saitan-sai|lt=Saitan-sai (New Year's Festival)}} * February: {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分追儺祭|lt=Setsubun Tsuina-sai}} * May: festival * June: {{ill|Nagoshi no Harae|ja|夏越の祓}} * September: Autumn Festival * December: {{ill|Ōharae|ja|大祓}} == Cultural Properties == * Large {{ill|zelkova|ja|ケヤキ}} tree (designated a natural monument of Ibaraki Prefecture) * Hanging scroll of Hachiman as a monk * Stone sword artifacts * Military fan (attributed to {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|ja|源義光}}) == Auxiliary Shrines == * {{ill|Kitano Tenjin Shrine|ja|北野天神社}} (Plum Tenjin) – Established in {{ill|Kōhei 6|ja|康平}} (1062) by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|ja|源義家}} to pray for victory in the {{ill|Earlier Nine Years' War|ja|前九年の役}}. * {{ill|Ichihime Shrine|ja|市比売神社}} * {{ill|Ashio Shrine|ja|足尾神社}} * {{ill|Atago Shrine|ja|愛宕神社}} * {{ill|Wakazumi Shrine|ja|別雷神社}} * {{ill|Fuji Shrine|ja|富士神社}} * {{ill|Awashima Shrine|ja|淡島神社}} File:Ota-tenmangu in Wakamiya-hachimanhu (Hitachiota).jpg|Kitano Tenjin Shrine File:Keidaisha1 in Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|Ichihime Shrine, Wakazumi Shrine, Atago Shrine File:Ashio-jinja in Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|Ashio Shrine File:Fuji-jinja in Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|Fuji Shrine == Access == The shrine is approximately 1.5 km north of {{ill|Hitachi-Ōta Station|ja|常陸太田駅}} on the {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|ja|東日本旅客鉄道}} (JR). It is a 20-minute walk. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Shinto shrines|ja|神社一覧}} * {{ill|List of Japanese deities|ja|日本の神の一覧}} == External Links == * {{Instagram|wakamiyah}} * {{Twitter|wakamiya_h}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Ibaraki Prefecture Shrine Index}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Former Gōsha]] [[Category:Satake clan]] [[Category:Architecture in Hitachiōta]] [[Category:History of Hitachiōta]] [[Category:Emperor Nintoku]] [[zh:若宮八幡宮]] [[ja:%E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E5%AE%AE_(%E5%B8%B8%E9%99%B8%E5%A4%AA%E7%94%B0%E5%B8%82)]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)}} [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:qq-Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)—>[[category:qq close]] [[Category:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]][[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)}} [[Category:qq-Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)—>[[category:qq close]] [[Category:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]][[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:Sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:trimming off this bad category]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]][[Category:trimming off this bad category]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:if these are dates then move them to completion tier 5]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]][[Category:if these are dates then move them to completion tier 5]]

{{Cite sources|date=2019-03-12}} {{Shrine |name=Wakamiya Hachimangū (若宮八幡宮) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Wakamiyahachimaｎgu.JPG|250px]]
Main hall (September 2005) |location={{ill|Hitachiōta|ja|常陸太田市}}, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県}} Miyamoto-chō 2344 |coordinates={{coord2|36|32|34.8|N|140|31|14.6|E|type:landmark_region:JP-08|display=inline,title|name=Wakamiya Hachimangū}} |enshrined_deities=[[Ōsasagi no Mikoto]] ({{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}})
[[Ukanomitama no Mikoto]] |established={{ill|Ōei era|ja|応永}} (circa 1400) |shrine_rank=Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |annual_festival=See [[#Festivals]] |main_hall= |map={{location map|Japan Ibaraki|label=Wakamiya Hachimangū|coordinates= {{coord2|36|32|34.8|N|140|31|14.6|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|32|34.8|N|140|31|14.6|E}}}} }} [[File:Torii of Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|thumb|right|245px|Torii]] [[File:Shinmon of Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|thumb|right|245px|Shinmon (gate)]] '''Wakamiya Hachimangū''' (若宮八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Hitachiōta|ja|常陸太田市}}, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県}}, Japan. The shrine's rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度}} was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == History == The shrine was established during the {{ill|Ōei era|ja|応永}} (circa 1400) by {{ill|Yoshihito Satake|ja|佐竹義人}}, who invited its deity from {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|ja|鶴岡八幡宮}} in {{ill|Kamakura|ja|鎌倉市}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}. It was originally enshrined within {{ill|Ōta Castle (Hitachi Province)|ja|太田城 (常陸国)}} as a guardian deity and served as the prayer site for the {{ill|Satake clan|ja|佐竹氏}}. Research suggests that the Satake clan initially revered a {{ill|Hachiman shrine|ja|八幡社}} derived from {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮}}, which was the guardian shrine of the Minamoto clan in Hitachi. However, Yoshihito Satake, who had ties with the {{ill|Uesugi clan|ja|上杉氏}}, instead adopted the enshrinement of Tsurugaoka Hachimangū, possibly against opposition from the Satake family.Teiko Tsutsumi, "Satake Clan and Hachiman Worship," {{ill|Ibaraki Prefectural History Museum Bulletin|ja|茨城県立歴史館報}} No. 28, 2001. Compiled in {{ill|Research Series on Medieval Kanto Warriors Vol. 30: Hitachi Satake Clan|ja|シリーズ・中世関東武士の研究 第三十巻 常陸佐竹氏}} (Kōshōshō Publishing, 2021), pp. 192–227. Although the Satake clan was later relocated to {{ill|Akita|ja|秋田}} after the {{ill|Battle of Sekigahara|ja|関ヶ原の戦い}}, they took the shrine's deity with them to {{ill|Ōdate Castle|ja|大館城}} as its guardian spirit. The deity was subsequently enshrined at {{ill|Ōdate Hachiman Shrine|ja|大館八幡神社}}, continuing its role as a protector.{{Cite web|title=八幡神社 |url=http://akita-jinjacho.sakura.ne.jp/tatsujin_etc/kennsaku/oodate/67_hachiman_hachiman.html |publisher=Akita Prefecture Shrine Association |accessdate=2015-10-12}} In {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} 14 (1609), the first {{ill|daimyō|ja|大名}} of the {{ill|Mito Domain|ja|水戸藩}}, {{ill|Tokugawa Yorifusa|ja|徳川頼房}}, was healed of an illness after praying at the shrine. Thereafter, the {{ill|Mito Tokugawa clan|ja|水戸徳川家}} held the shrine in high reverence. In {{ill|Genroku|ja|元禄}} 5 (1692), the second daimyō, {{ill|Tokugawa Mitsukuni|ja|徳川光圀}} (popularly known as Mito Kōmon), designated the shrine as a guardian of the Ōta district. In {{ill|Hōei|ja|宝永}} 5 (1708), the shrine was relocated to its current location alongside the {{ill|Ōta Inari Shrine|ja|太田稲荷神社}}, which had been worshiped together with it. In 1940 (Showa 15), based on the tradition that a woman named Tsuruko accompanied the deity’s transfer from Tsurugaoka Hachimangū and oversaw its rituals, the shrine's parishioner priestesses revived the "Tsuruko Dance." Recently, this {{ill|kagura|ja|神楽}} offering has been expanded to include performances such as {{ill|Urayasu no Mai|ja|浦安の舞}}, {{ill|Toyo no Sakai no Mai|ja|豊栄の舞}}, and {{ill|Yaotome no Mai|ja|八乙女舞}}, which are performed with grace during major festivals. [[File:Kaguraden of Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|thumb|right|245px|Kaguraden]] == Enshrined Deities == * '''[[Ōsasagi no Mikoto]]''' (Emperor Nintoku) * '''[[Ukanomitama no Mikoto]]''' == Festivals == * January: {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|元旦祭|Saitan-sai=Saitan-sai|lt=Saitan-sai (New Year's Festival)}} * February: {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分追儺祭|lt=Setsubun Tsuina-sai}} * May: festival * June: {{ill|Nagoshi no Harae|ja|夏越の祓}} * September: Autumn Festival * December: {{ill|Ōharae|ja|大祓}} == Cultural Properties == * Large {{ill|zelkova|ja|ケヤキ}} tree (designated a natural monument of Ibaraki Prefecture) * Hanging scroll of Hachiman as a monk * Stone sword artifacts * Military fan (attributed to {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|ja|源義光}}) == Auxiliary Shrines == * {{ill|Kitano Tenjin Shrine|ja|北野天神社}} (Plum Tenjin) – Established in {{ill|Kōhei 6|ja|康平}} (1062) by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|ja|源義家}} to pray for victory in the {{ill|Earlier Nine Years' War|ja|前九年の役}}. * {{ill|Ichihime Shrine|ja|市比売神社}} * {{ill|Ashio Shrine|ja|足尾神社}} * {{ill|Atago Shrine|ja|愛宕神社}} * {{ill|Wakazumi Shrine|ja|別雷神社}} * {{ill|Fuji Shrine|ja|富士神社}} * {{ill|Awashima Shrine|ja|淡島神社}} File:Ota-tenmangu in Wakamiya-hachimanhu (Hitachiota).jpg|Kitano Tenjin Shrine File:Keidaisha1 in Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|Ichihime Shrine, Wakazumi Shrine, Atago Shrine File:Ashio-jinja in Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|Ashio Shrine File:Fuji-jinja in Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|Fuji Shrine == Access == The shrine is approximately 1.5 km north of {{ill|Hitachi-Ōta Station|ja|常陸太田駅}} on the {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|ja|東日本旅客鉄道}} (JR). It is a 20-minute walk. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Shinto shrines|ja|神社一覧}} * {{ill|List of Japanese deities|ja|日本の神の一覧}} == External Links == * {{Instagram|wakamiyah}} * {{Twitter|wakamiya_h}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Ibaraki Prefecture Shrine Index}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Former Gōsha]] [[Category:Satake clan]] [[Category:Architecture in Hitachiōta]] [[Category:History of Hitachiōta]] [[Category:Emperor Nintoku]] [[zh:若宮八幡宮]] [[ja:%E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E5%AE%AE_(%E5%B8%B8%E9%99%B8%E5%A4%AA%E7%94%B0%E5%B8%82)]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)}} [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:deprecated qq open]]

[[Ukanomitama no Mikoto]] |established={{ill|Ōei era|ja|応永}} (circa 1400) |shrine_rank=Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |annual_festival=See [[#Festivals]] |main_hall= |map={{location map|Japan Ibaraki|label=Wakamiya Hachimangū|coordinates= {{coord2|36|32|34.8|N|140|31|14.6|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|32|34.8|N|140|31|14.6|E}}}} }} [[File:Torii of Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|thumb|right|245px|Torii]] [[File:Shinmon of Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|thumb|right|245px|Shinmon (gate)]] '''Wakamiya Hachimangū''' (若宮八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Hitachiōta|ja|常陸太田市}}, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県}}, Japan. The shrine's rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度}} was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == History == The shrine was established during the {{ill|Ōei era|ja|応永}} (circa 1400) by {{ill|Yoshihito Satake|ja|佐竹義人}}, who invited its deity from {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|ja|鶴岡八幡宮}} in {{ill|Kamakura|ja|鎌倉市}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}. It was originally enshrined within {{ill|Ōta Castle (Hitachi Province)|ja|太田城 (常陸国)}} as a guardian deity and served as the prayer site for the {{ill|Satake clan|ja|佐竹氏}}. Research suggests that the Satake clan initially revered a {{ill|Hachiman shrine|ja|八幡社}} derived from {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮}}, which was the guardian shrine of the Minamoto clan in Hitachi. However, Yoshihito Satake, who had ties with the {{ill|Uesugi clan|ja|上杉氏}}, instead adopted the enshrinement of Tsurugaoka Hachimangū, possibly against opposition from the Satake family.Teiko Tsutsumi, "Satake Clan and Hachiman Worship," {{ill|Ibaraki Prefectural History Museum Bulletin|ja|茨城県立歴史館報}} No. 28, 2001. Compiled in {{ill|Research Series on Medieval Kanto Warriors Vol. 30: Hitachi Satake Clan|ja|シリーズ・中世関東武士の研究 第三十巻 常陸佐竹氏}} (Kōshōshō Publishing, 2021), pp. 192–227. Although the Satake clan was later relocated to {{ill|Akita|ja|秋田}} after the {{ill|Battle of Sekigahara|ja|関ヶ原の戦い}}, they took the shrine's deity with them to {{ill|Ōdate Castle|ja|大館城}} as its guardian spirit. The deity was subsequently enshrined at {{ill|Ōdate Hachiman Shrine|ja|大館八幡神社}}, continuing its role as a protector.{{Cite web|title=八幡神社 |url=http://akita-jinjacho.sakura.ne.jp/tatsujin_etc/kennsaku/oodate/67_hachiman_hachiman.html |publisher=Akita Prefecture Shrine Association |accessdate=2015-10-12}} In {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} 14 (1609), the first {{ill|daimyō|ja|大名}} of the {{ill|Mito Domain|ja|水戸藩}}, {{ill|Tokugawa Yorifusa|ja|徳川頼房}}, was healed of an illness after praying at the shrine. Thereafter, the {{ill|Mito Tokugawa clan|ja|水戸徳川家}} held the shrine in high reverence. In {{ill|Genroku|ja|元禄}} 5 (1692), the second daimyō, {{ill|Tokugawa Mitsukuni|ja|徳川光圀}} (popularly known as Mito Kōmon), designated the shrine as a guardian of the Ōta district. In {{ill|Hōei|ja|宝永}} 5 (1708), the shrine was relocated to its current location alongside the {{ill|Ōta Inari Shrine|ja|太田稲荷神社}}, which had been worshiped together with it. In 1940 (Showa 15), based on the tradition that a woman named Tsuruko accompanied the deity’s transfer from Tsurugaoka Hachimangū and oversaw its rituals, the shrine's parishioner priestesses revived the "Tsuruko Dance." Recently, this {{ill|kagura|ja|神楽}} offering has been expanded to include performances such as {{ill|Urayasu no Mai|ja|浦安の舞}}, {{ill|Toyo no Sakai no Mai|ja|豊栄の舞}}, and {{ill|Yaotome no Mai|ja|八乙女舞}}, which are performed with grace during major festivals. [[File:Kaguraden of Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|thumb|right|245px|Kaguraden]] == Enshrined Deities == * '''[[Ōsasagi no Mikoto]]''' (Emperor Nintoku) * '''[[Ukanomitama no Mikoto]]''' == Festivals == * January: {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|元旦祭|Saitan-sai=Saitan-sai|lt=Saitan-sai (New Year's Festival)}} * February: {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分追儺祭|lt=Setsubun Tsuina-sai}} * May: festival * June: {{ill|Nagoshi no Harae|ja|夏越の祓}} * September: Autumn Festival * December: {{ill|Ōharae|ja|大祓}} == Cultural Properties == * Large {{ill|zelkova|ja|ケヤキ}} tree (designated a natural monument of Ibaraki Prefecture) * Hanging scroll of Hachiman as a monk * Stone sword artifacts * Military fan (attributed to {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|ja|源義光}}) == Auxiliary Shrines == * {{ill|Kitano Tenjin Shrine|ja|北野天神社}} (Plum Tenjin) – Established in {{ill|Kōhei 6|ja|康平}} (1062) by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|ja|源義家}} to pray for victory in the {{ill|Earlier Nine Years' War|ja|前九年の役}}. * {{ill|Ichihime Shrine|ja|市比売神社}} * {{ill|Ashio Shrine|ja|足尾神社}} * {{ill|Atago Shrine|ja|愛宕神社}} * {{ill|Wakazumi Shrine|ja|別雷神社}} * {{ill|Fuji Shrine|ja|富士神社}} * {{ill|Awashima Shrine|ja|淡島神社}} File:Ota-tenmangu in Wakamiya-hachimanhu (Hitachiota).jpg|Kitano Tenjin Shrine File:Keidaisha1 in Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|Ichihime Shrine, Wakazumi Shrine, Atago Shrine File:Ashio-jinja in Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|Ashio Shrine File:Fuji-jinja in Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|Fuji Shrine == Access == The shrine is approximately 1.5 km north of {{ill|Hitachi-Ōta Station|ja|常陸太田駅}} on the {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|ja|東日本旅客鉄道}} (JR). It is a 20-minute walk. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Shinto shrines|ja|神社一覧}} * {{ill|List of Japanese deities|ja|日本の神の一覧}} == External Links == * {{Instagram|wakamiyah}} * {{Twitter|wakamiya_h}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Ibaraki Prefecture Shrine Index}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Former Gōsha]] [[Category:Satake clan]] [[Category:Architecture in Hitachiōta]] [[Category:History of Hitachiōta]] [[Category:Emperor Nintoku]] [[zh:若宮八幡宮]] [[ja:%E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E5%AE%AE_(%E5%B8%B8%E9%99%B8%E5%A4%AA%E7%94%B0%E5%B8%82)]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)}} [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:deprecated qq open]][[category:qq close]] [[Category:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]][[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)}} [[Category:deprecated qq open]][[category:qq close]] [[Category:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]][[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:Sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:trimming off this bad category]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]][[Category:trimming off this bad category]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:if these are dates then move them to completion tier 5]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]][[Category:if these are dates then move them to completion tier 5]] [[Category:New]]

In 1940 (Showa 15), based on the tradition that a woman named Tsuruko accompanied the deity’s transfer from Tsurugaoka Hachimangū and oversaw its rituals, the shrine's parishioner priestesses revived the "Tsuruko Dance." Recently, this {{ill|kagura|ja|神楽}} offering has been expanded to include performances such as {{ill|Urayasu no Mai|ja|浦安の舞}}, {{ill|Toyo no Sakai no Mai|ja|豊栄の舞}}, and {{ill|Yaotome no Mai|ja|八乙女舞}}, which are performed with grace during major festivals. [[File:Kaguraden of Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|thumb|right|245px|Kaguraden]] == Enshrined Deities == * '''[[Ōsasagi no Mikoto]]''' (Emperor Nintoku) * '''[[Ukanomitama no Mikoto]]''' == Festivals == * January: {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|元旦祭|Saitan-sai=Saitan-sai|lt=Saitan-sai (New Year's Festival)}} * February: {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分追儺祭|lt=Setsubun Tsuina-sai}} * May: festival * June: {{ill|Nagoshi no Harae|ja|夏越の祓}} * September: Autumn Festival * December: {{ill|Ōharae|ja|大祓}} == Cultural Properties == * Large {{ill|zelkova|ja|ケヤキ}} tree (designated a natural monument of Ibaraki Prefecture) * Hanging scroll of Hachiman as a monk * Stone sword artifacts * Military fan (attributed to {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|ja|源義光}}) == Auxiliary Shrines == * {{ill|Kitano Tenjin Shrine|ja|北野天神社}} (Plum Tenjin) – Established in {{ill|Kōhei 6|ja|康平}} (1062) by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|ja|源義家}} to pray for victory in the {{ill|Earlier Nine Years' War|ja|前九年の役}}. * {{ill|Ichihime Shrine|ja|市比売神社}} * {{ill|Ashio Shrine|ja|足尾神社}} * {{ill|Atago Shrine|ja|愛宕神社}} * {{ill|Wakazumi Shrine|ja|別雷神社}} * {{ill|Fuji Shrine|ja|富士神社}} * {{ill|Awashima Shrine|ja|淡島神社}} File:Ota-tenmangu in Wakamiya-hachimanhu (Hitachiota).jpg|Kitano Tenjin Shrine File:Keidaisha1 in Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|Ichihime Shrine, Wakazumi Shrine, Atago Shrine File:Ashio-jinja in Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|Ashio Shrine File:Fuji-jinja in Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|Fuji Shrine == Access == The shrine is approximately 1.5 km north of {{ill|Hitachi-Ōta Station|ja|常陸太田駅}} on the {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|ja|東日本旅客鉄道}} (JR). It is a 20-minute walk. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Shinto shrines|ja|神社一覧}} * {{ill|List of Japanese deities|ja|日本の神の一覧}} == External Links == * {{Instagram|wakamiyah}} * {{Twitter|wakamiya_h}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Ibaraki Prefecture Shrine Index}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Former Gōsha]] [[Category:Satake clan]] [[Category:Architecture in Hitachiōta]] [[Category:History of Hitachiōta]] [[Category:Emperor Nintoku]] [[zh:若宮八幡宮]] [[ja:%E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E5%AE%AE_(%E5%B8%B8%E9%99%B8%E5%A4%AA%E7%94%B0%E5%B8%82)]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)}} [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:deprecated qq open]][[category:qq close]] [[Category:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]][[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)}} [[Category:deprecated qq open]][[category:qq close]] [[Category:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]][[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]][[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:if these are dates then move them to completion tier 5]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]][[Category:if these are dates then move them to completion tier 5]] [[Category:New]]

{{Cite sources|date=2019-03-12}} {{Shrine |name=Wakamiya Hachimangū (若宮八幡宮) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Wakamiyahachimaｎgu.JPG|250px]]
Main hall (September 2005) |location={{ill|Hitachiōta|ja|常陸太田市}}, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県}} Miyamoto-chō 2344 |coordinates={{coord2|36|32|34.8|N|140|31|14.6|E|type:landmark_region:JP-08|display=inline,title|name=Wakamiya Hachimangū}} |enshrined_deities=[[Ōsasagi no Mikoto]] ({{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}})
[[Ukanomitama no Mikoto]] |established={{ill|Ōei era|ja|応永}} (circa 1400) |shrine_rank=Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |annual_festival=See [[#Festivals]] |main_hall= |map={{location map|Japan Ibaraki|label=Wakamiya Hachimangū|coordinates= {{coord2|36|32|34.8|N|140|31|14.6|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|32|34.8|N|140|31|14.6|E}}}} }} [[File:Torii of Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|thumb|right|245px|Torii]] [[File:Shinmon of Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|thumb|right|245px|Shinmon (gate)]] '''Wakamiya Hachimangū''' (若宮八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Hitachiōta|ja|常陸太田市}}, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県}}, Japan. The shrine's rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度}} was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == History == The shrine was established during the {{ill|Ōei era|ja|応永}} (circa 1400) by {{ill|Yoshihito Satake|ja|佐竹義人}}, who invited its deity from {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|ja|鶴岡八幡宮}} in {{ill|Kamakura|ja|鎌倉市}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}. It was originally enshrined within {{ill|Ōta Castle (Hitachi Province)|ja|太田城 (常陸国)}} as a guardian deity and served as the prayer site for the {{ill|Satake clan|ja|佐竹氏}}. Research suggests that the Satake clan initially revered a {{ill|Hachiman shrine|ja|八幡社}} derived from {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮}}, which was the guardian shrine of the Minamoto clan in Hitachi. However, Yoshihito Satake, who had ties with the {{ill|Uesugi clan|ja|上杉氏}}, instead adopted the enshrinement of Tsurugaoka Hachimangū, possibly against opposition from the Satake family.Teiko Tsutsumi, "Satake Clan and Hachiman Worship," {{ill|Ibaraki Prefectural History Museum Bulletin|ja|茨城県立歴史館報}} No. 28, 2001. Compiled in {{ill|Research Series on Medieval Kanto Warriors Vol. 30: Hitachi Satake Clan|ja|シリーズ・中世関東武士の研究 第三十巻 常陸佐竹氏}} (Kōshōshō Publishing, 2021), pp. 192–227. Although the Satake clan was later relocated to {{ill|Akita|ja|秋田}} after the {{ill|Battle of Sekigahara|ja|関ヶ原の戦い}}, they took the shrine's deity with them to {{ill|Ōdate Castle|ja|大館城}} as its guardian spirit. The deity was subsequently enshrined at {{ill|Ōdate Hachiman Shrine|ja|大館八幡神社}}, continuing its role as a protector.{{Cite web|title=八幡神社 |url=http://akita-jinjacho.sakura.ne.jp/tatsujin_etc/kennsaku/oodate/67_hachiman_hachiman.html |publisher=Akita Prefecture Shrine Association |accessdate=2015-10-12}} In {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} 14 (1609), the first {{ill|daimyō|ja|大名}} of the {{ill|Mito Domain|ja|水戸藩}}, {{ill|Tokugawa Yorifusa|ja|徳川頼房}}, was healed of an illness after praying at the shrine. Thereafter, the {{ill|Mito Tokugawa clan|ja|水戸徳川家}} held the shrine in high reverence. In {{ill|Genroku|ja|元禄}} 5 (1692), the second daimyō, {{ill|Tokugawa Mitsukuni|ja|徳川光圀}} (popularly known as Mito Kōmon), designated the shrine as a guardian of the Ōta district. In {{ill|Hōei|ja|宝永}} 5 (1708), the shrine was relocated to its current location alongside the {{ill|Ōta Inari Shrine|ja|太田稲荷神社}}, which had been worshiped together with it. In 1940 (Showa 15), based on the tradition that a woman named Tsuruko accompanied the deity’s transfer from Tsurugaoka Hachimangū and oversaw its rituals, the shrine's parishioner priestesses revived the "Tsuruko Dance." Recently, this {{ill|kagura|ja|神楽}} offering has been expanded to include performances such as {{ill|Urayasu no Mai|ja|浦安の舞}}, {{ill|Toyo no Sakai no Mai|ja|豊栄の舞}}, and {{ill|Yaotome no Mai|ja|八乙女舞}}, which are performed with grace during major festivals. [[File:Kaguraden of Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|thumb|right|245px|Kaguraden]] == Enshrined Deities == * '''[[Ōsasagi no Mikoto]]''' (Emperor Nintoku) * '''[[Ukanomitama no Mikoto]]''' == Festivals == * January: {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|元旦祭|Saitan-sai=Saitan-sai|lt=Saitan-sai (New Year's Festival)}} * February: {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分追儺祭|lt=Setsubun Tsuina-sai}} * May: festival * June: {{ill|Nagoshi no Harae|ja|夏越の祓}} * September: Autumn Festival * December: {{ill|Ōharae|ja|大祓}} == Cultural Properties == * Large {{ill|zelkova|ja|ケヤキ}} tree (designated a natural monument of Ibaraki Prefecture) * Hanging scroll of Hachiman as a monk * Stone sword artifacts * Military fan (attributed to {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|ja|源義光}}) == Auxiliary Shrines == * {{ill|Kitano Tenjin Shrine|ja|北野天神社}} (Plum Tenjin) – Established in {{ill|Kōhei 6|ja|康平}} (1062) by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|ja|源義家}} to pray for victory in the {{ill|Earlier Nine Years' War|ja|前九年の役}}. * {{ill|Ichihime Shrine|ja|市比売神社}} * {{ill|Ashio Shrine|ja|足尾神社}} * {{ill|Atago Shrine|ja|愛宕神社}} * {{ill|Wakazumi Shrine|ja|別雷神社}} * {{ill|Fuji Shrine|ja|富士神社}} * {{ill|Awashima Shrine|ja|淡島神社}} File:Ota-tenmangu in Wakamiya-hachimanhu (Hitachiota).jpg|Kitano Tenjin Shrine File:Keidaisha1 in Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|Ichihime Shrine, Wakazumi Shrine, Atago Shrine File:Ashio-jinja in Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|Ashio Shrine File:Fuji-jinja in Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|Fuji Shrine == Access == The shrine is approximately 1.5 km north of {{ill|Hitachi-Ōta Station|ja|常陸太田駅}} on the {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|ja|東日本旅客鉄道}} (JR). It is a 20-minute walk. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Shinto shrines|ja|神社一覧}} * {{ill|List of Japanese deities|ja|日本の神の一覧}} == External Links == * {{Instagram|wakamiyah}} * {{Twitter|wakamiya_h}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Ibaraki Prefecture Shrine Index}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Former Gōsha]] [[Category:Satake clan]] [[Category:Architecture in Hitachiōta]] [[Category:History of Hitachiōta]] [[Category:Emperor Nintoku]] [[zh:若宮八幡宮]] [[ja:%E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E5%AE%AE_(%E5%B8%B8%E9%99%B8%E5%A4%AA%E7%94%B0%E5%B8%82)]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)}} [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[category:qq close]] [[Category:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]][[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)}} [[category:qq close]] [[Category:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]][[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]][[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:if these are dates then move them to completion tier 5]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]][[Category:if these are dates then move them to completion tier 5]] [[Category:New]]

{{Cite sources|date=2019-03-12}} {{Shrine |name=Wakamiya Hachimangū (若宮八幡宮) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Wakamiyahachimaｎgu.JPG|250px]]
Main hall (September 2005) |location={{ill|Hitachiōta|ja|常陸太田市}}, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県}} Miyamoto-chō 2344 |coordinates={{coord2|36|32|34.8|N|140|31|14.6|E|type:landmark_region:JP-08|display=inline,title|name=Wakamiya Hachimangū}} |enshrined_deities=[[Ōsasagi no Mikoto]] ({{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}})
[[Ukanomitama no Mikoto]] |established={{ill|Ōei era|ja|応永}} (circa 1400) |shrine_rank=Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |annual_festival=See [[#Festivals]] |main_hall= |map={{location map|Japan Ibaraki|label=Wakamiya Hachimangū|coordinates= {{coord2|36|32|34.8|N|140|31|14.6|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|32|34.8|N|140|31|14.6|E}}}} }} [[File:Torii of Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|thumb|right|245px|Torii]] [[File:Shinmon of Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|thumb|right|245px|Shinmon (gate)]] '''Wakamiya Hachimangū''' (若宮八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Hitachiōta|ja|常陸太田市}}, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県}}, Japan. The shrine's rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度}} was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == History == The shrine was established during the {{ill|Ōei era|ja|応永}} (circa 1400) by {{ill|Yoshihito Satake|ja|佐竹義人}}, who invited its deity from {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|ja|鶴岡八幡宮}} in {{ill|Kamakura|ja|鎌倉市}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}. It was originally enshrined within {{ill|Ōta Castle (Hitachi Province)|ja|太田城 (常陸国)}} as a guardian deity and served as the prayer site for the {{ill|Satake clan|ja|佐竹氏}}. Research suggests that the Satake clan initially revered a {{ill|Hachiman shrine|ja|八幡社}} derived from {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮}}, which was the guardian shrine of the Minamoto clan in Hitachi. However, Yoshihito Satake, who had ties with the {{ill|Uesugi clan|ja|上杉氏}}, instead adopted the enshrinement of Tsurugaoka Hachimangū, possibly against opposition from the Satake family.Teiko Tsutsumi, "Satake Clan and Hachiman Worship," {{ill|Ibaraki Prefectural History Museum Bulletin|ja|茨城県立歴史館報}} No. 28, 2001. Compiled in {{ill|Research Series on Medieval Kanto Warriors Vol. 30: Hitachi Satake Clan|ja|シリーズ・中世関東武士の研究 第三十巻 常陸佐竹氏}} (Kōshōshō Publishing, 2021), pp. 192–227. Although the Satake clan was later relocated to {{ill|Akita|ja|秋田}} after the {{ill|Battle of Sekigahara|ja|関ヶ原の戦い}}, they took the shrine's deity with them to {{ill|Ōdate Castle|ja|大館城}} as its guardian spirit. The deity was subsequently enshrined at {{ill|Ōdate Hachiman Shrine|ja|大館八幡神社}}, continuing its role as a protector.{{Cite web|title=八幡神社 |url=http://akita-jinjacho.sakura.ne.jp/tatsujin_etc/kennsaku/oodate/67_hachiman_hachiman.html |publisher=Akita Prefecture Shrine Association |accessdate=2015-10-12}} In {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} 14 (1609), the first {{ill|daimyō|ja|大名}} of the {{ill|Mito Domain|ja|水戸藩}}, {{ill|Tokugawa Yorifusa|ja|徳川頼房}}, was healed of an illness after praying at the shrine. Thereafter, the {{ill|Mito Tokugawa clan|ja|水戸徳川家}} held the shrine in high reverence. In {{ill|Genroku|ja|元禄}} 5 (1692), the second daimyō, {{ill|Tokugawa Mitsukuni|ja|徳川光圀}} (popularly known as Mito Kōmon), designated the shrine as a guardian of the Ōta district. In {{ill|Hōei|ja|宝永}} 5 (1708), the shrine was relocated to its current location alongside the {{ill|Ōta Inari Shrine|ja|太田稲荷神社}}, which had been worshiped together with it. In 1940 (Showa 15), based on the tradition that a woman named Tsuruko accompanied the deity’s transfer from Tsurugaoka Hachimangū and oversaw its rituals, the shrine's parishioner priestesses revived the "Tsuruko Dance." Recently, this {{ill|kagura|ja|神楽}} offering has been expanded to include performances such as {{ill|Urayasu no Mai|ja|浦安の舞}}, {{ill|Toyo no Sakai no Mai|ja|豊栄の舞}}, and {{ill|Yaotome no Mai|ja|八乙女舞}}, which are performed with grace during major festivals. [[File:Kaguraden of Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|thumb|right|245px|Kaguraden]] == Enshrined Deities == * '''[[Ōsasagi no Mikoto]]''' (Emperor Nintoku) * '''[[Ukanomitama no Mikoto]]''' == Festivals == * January: {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|元旦祭|Saitan-sai=Saitan-sai|lt=Saitan-sai (New Year's Festival)}} * February: {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分追儺祭|lt=Setsubun Tsuina-sai}} * May: festival * June: {{ill|Nagoshi no Harae|ja|夏越の祓}} * September: Autumn Festival * December: {{ill|Ōharae|ja|大祓}} == Cultural Properties == * Large {{ill|zelkova|ja|ケヤキ}} tree (designated a natural monument of Ibaraki Prefecture) * Hanging scroll of Hachiman as a monk * Stone sword artifacts * Military fan (attributed to {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|ja|源義光}}) == Auxiliary Shrines == * {{ill|Kitano Tenjin Shrine|ja|北野天神社}} (Plum Tenjin) – Established in {{ill|Kōhei 6|ja|康平}} (1062) by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|ja|源義家}} to pray for victory in the {{ill|Earlier Nine Years' War|ja|前九年の役}}. * {{ill|Ichihime Shrine|ja|市比売神社}} * {{ill|Ashio Shrine|ja|足尾神社}} * {{ill|Atago Shrine|ja|愛宕神社}} * {{ill|Wakazumi Shrine|ja|別雷神社}} * {{ill|Fuji Shrine|ja|富士神社}} * {{ill|Awashima Shrine|ja|淡島神社}} File:Ota-tenmangu in Wakamiya-hachimanhu (Hitachiota).jpg|Kitano Tenjin Shrine File:Keidaisha1 in Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|Ichihime Shrine, Wakazumi Shrine, Atago Shrine File:Ashio-jinja in Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|Ashio Shrine File:Fuji-jinja in Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|Fuji Shrine == Access == The shrine is approximately 1.5 km north of {{ill|Hitachi-Ōta Station|ja|常陸太田駅}} on the {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|ja|東日本旅客鉄道}} (JR). It is a 20-minute walk. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Shinto shrines|ja|神社一覧}} * {{ill|List of Japanese deities|ja|日本の神の一覧}} == External Links == * {{Instagram|wakamiyah}} * {{Twitter|wakamiya_h}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Ibaraki Prefecture Shrine Index}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Former Gōsha]] [[Category:Satake clan]] [[Category:Architecture in Hitachiōta]] [[Category:History of Hitachiōta]] [[Category:Emperor Nintoku]] [[zh:若宮八幡宮]] [[ja:%E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E5%AE%AE_(%E5%B8%B8%E9%99%B8%E5%A4%AA%E7%94%B0%E5%B8%82)]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)}} [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:New uncategorized original categories]] [[Category:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]][[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)}} [[Category:New uncategorized original categories]] [[Category:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]][[Category:q--q]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]][[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:if these are dates then move them to completion tier 5]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]][[Category:if these are dates then move them to completion tier 5]] [[Category:New]]

[[File:Kaguraden of Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|thumb|right|245px|Kaguraden]] == Enshrined Deities == * '''[[Ōsasagi no Mikoto]]''' (Emperor Nintoku) * '''[[Ukanomitama no Mikoto]]''' == Festivals == * January: {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|元旦祭|Saitan-sai=Saitan-sai|lt=Saitan-sai (New Year's Festival)}} * February: {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分追儺祭|lt=Setsubun Tsuina-sai}} * May: festival * June: {{ill|Nagoshi no Harae|ja|夏越の祓}} * September: Autumn Festival * December: {{ill|Ōharae|ja|大祓}} == Cultural Properties == * Large {{ill|zelkova|ja|ケヤキ}} tree (designated a natural monument of Ibaraki Prefecture) * Hanging scroll of Hachiman as a monk * Stone sword artifacts * Military fan (attributed to {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|ja|源義光}}) == Auxiliary Shrines == * {{ill|Kitano Tenjin Shrine|ja|北野天神社}} (Plum Tenjin) – Established in {{ill|Kōhei 6|ja|康平}} (1062) by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|ja|源義家}} to pray for victory in the {{ill|Earlier Nine Years' War|ja|前九年の役}}. * {{ill|Ichihime Shrine|ja|市比売神社}} * {{ill|Ashio Shrine|ja|足尾神社}} * {{ill|Atago Shrine|ja|愛宕神社}} * {{ill|Wakazumi Shrine|ja|別雷神社}} * {{ill|Fuji Shrine|ja|富士神社}} * {{ill|Awashima Shrine|ja|淡島神社}} File:Ota-tenmangu in Wakamiya-hachimanhu (Hitachiota).jpg|Kitano Tenjin Shrine File:Keidaisha1 in Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|Ichihime Shrine, Wakazumi Shrine, Atago Shrine File:Ashio-jinja in Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|Ashio Shrine File:Fuji-jinja in Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|Fuji Shrine == Access == The shrine is approximately 1.5 km north of {{ill|Hitachi-Ōta Station|ja|常陸太田駅}} on the {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|ja|東日本旅客鉄道}} (JR). It is a 20-minute walk. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Shinto shrines|ja|神社一覧}} * {{ill|List of Japanese deities|ja|日本の神の一覧}} == External Links == * {{Instagram|wakamiyah}} * {{Twitter|wakamiya_h}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Ibaraki Prefecture Shrine Index}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Former Gōsha]] [[Category:Satake clan]] [[Category:Architecture in Hitachiōta]] [[Category:History of Hitachiōta]] [[Category:Emperor Nintoku]] [[zh:若宮八幡宮]] [[ja:%E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E5%AE%AE_(%E5%B8%B8%E9%99%B8%E5%A4%AA%E7%94%B0%E5%B8%82)]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)}} [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:New uncategorized original categories]] [[Category:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]][[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)}} [[Category:New uncategorized original categories]] [[Category:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]][[Category:qq]][[en:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]][[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:if these are dates then move them to completion tier 5]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]][[Category:if these are dates then move them to completion tier 5]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]][[Category:qq]]

{{Cite sources|date=2019-03-12}} {{Shrine |name=Wakamiya Hachimangū (若宮八幡宮) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Wakamiyahachimaｎgu.JPG|250px]]
Main hall (September 2005) |location={{ill|Hitachiōta|ja|常陸太田市}}, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県}} Miyamoto-chō 2344 |coordinates={{coord2|36|32|34.8|N|140|31|14.6|E|type:landmark_region:JP-08|display=inline,title|name=Wakamiya Hachimangū}} |enshrined_deities=[[Ōsasagi no Mikoto]] ({{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}})
[[Ukanomitama no Mikoto]] |established={{ill|Ōei era|ja|応永}} (circa 1400) |shrine_rank=Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |annual_festival=See [[#Festivals]] |main_hall= |map={{location map|Japan Ibaraki|label=Wakamiya Hachimangū|coordinates= {{coord2|36|32|34.8|N|140|31|14.6|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|32|34.8|N|140|31|14.6|E}}}} }} [[File:Torii of Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|thumb|right|245px|Torii]] [[File:Shinmon of Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|thumb|right|245px|Shinmon (gate)]] '''Wakamiya Hachimangū''' (若宮八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Hitachiōta|ja|常陸太田市}}, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県}}, Japan. The shrine's rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度}} was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == History == The shrine was established during the {{ill|Ōei era|ja|応永}} (circa 1400) by {{ill|Yoshihito Satake|ja|佐竹義人}}, who invited its deity from {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|ja|鶴岡八幡宮}} in {{ill|Kamakura|ja|鎌倉市}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}. It was originally enshrined within {{ill|Ōta Castle (Hitachi Province)|ja|太田城 (常陸国)}} as a guardian deity and served as the prayer site for the {{ill|Satake clan|ja|佐竹氏}}. Research suggests that the Satake clan initially revered a {{ill|Hachiman shrine|ja|八幡社}} derived from {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮}}, which was the guardian shrine of the Minamoto clan in Hitachi. However, Yoshihito Satake, who had ties with the {{ill|Uesugi clan|ja|上杉氏}}, instead adopted the enshrinement of Tsurugaoka Hachimangū, possibly against opposition from the Satake family.Teiko Tsutsumi, "Satake Clan and Hachiman Worship," {{ill|Ibaraki Prefectural History Museum Bulletin|ja|茨城県立歴史館報}} No. 28, 2001. Compiled in {{ill|Research Series on Medieval Kanto Warriors Vol. 30: Hitachi Satake Clan|ja|シリーズ・中世関東武士の研究 第三十巻 常陸佐竹氏}} (Kōshōshō Publishing, 2021), pp. 192–227. Although the Satake clan was later relocated to {{ill|Akita|ja|秋田}} after the {{ill|Battle of Sekigahara|ja|関ヶ原の戦い}}, they took the shrine's deity with them to {{ill|Ōdate Castle|ja|大館城}} as its guardian spirit. The deity was subsequently enshrined at {{ill|Ōdate Hachiman Shrine|ja|大館八幡神社}}, continuing its role as a protector.{{Cite web|title=八幡神社 |url=http://akita-jinjacho.sakura.ne.jp/tatsujin_etc/kennsaku/oodate/67_hachiman_hachiman.html |publisher=Akita Prefecture Shrine Association |accessdate=2015-10-12}} In {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} 14 (1609), the first {{ill|daimyō|ja|大名}} of the {{ill|Mito Domain|ja|水戸藩}}, {{ill|Tokugawa Yorifusa|ja|徳川頼房}}, was healed of an illness after praying at the shrine. Thereafter, the {{ill|Mito Tokugawa clan|ja|水戸徳川家}} held the shrine in high reverence. In {{ill|Genroku|ja|元禄}} 5 (1692), the second daimyō, {{ill|Tokugawa Mitsukuni|ja|徳川光圀}} (popularly known as Mito Kōmon), designated the shrine as a guardian of the Ōta district. In {{ill|Hōei|ja|宝永}} 5 (1708), the shrine was relocated to its current location alongside the {{ill|Ōta Inari Shrine|ja|太田稲荷神社}}, which had been worshiped together with it. In 1940 (Showa 15), based on the tradition that a woman named Tsuruko accompanied the deity’s transfer from Tsurugaoka Hachimangū and oversaw its rituals, the shrine's parishioner priestesses revived the "Tsuruko Dance." Recently, this {{ill|kagura|ja|神楽}} offering has been expanded to include performances such as {{ill|Urayasu no Mai|ja|浦安の舞}}, {{ill|Toyo no Sakai no Mai|ja|豊栄の舞}}, and {{ill|Yaotome no Mai|ja|八乙女舞}}, which are performed with grace during major festivals. [[File:Kaguraden of Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|thumb|right|245px|Kaguraden]] == Enshrined Deities == * '''[[Ōsasagi no Mikoto]]''' (Emperor Nintoku) * '''[[Ukanomitama no Mikoto]]''' == Festivals == * January: {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|元旦祭|Saitan-sai=Saitan-sai|lt=Saitan-sai (New Year's Festival)}} * February: {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分追儺祭|lt=Setsubun Tsuina-sai}} * May: festival * June: {{ill|Nagoshi no Harae|ja|夏越の祓}} * September: Autumn Festival * December: {{ill|Ōharae|ja|大祓}} == Cultural Properties == * Large {{ill|zelkova|ja|ケヤキ}} tree (designated a natural monument of Ibaraki Prefecture) * Hanging scroll of Hachiman as a monk * Stone sword artifacts * Military fan (attributed to {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|ja|源義光}}) == Auxiliary Shrines == * {{ill|Kitano Tenjin Shrine|ja|北野天神社}} (Plum Tenjin) – Established in {{ill|Kōhei 6|ja|康平}} (1062) by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|ja|源義家}} to pray for victory in the {{ill|Earlier Nine Years' War|ja|前九年の役}}. * {{ill|Ichihime Shrine|ja|市比売神社}} * {{ill|Ashio Shrine|ja|足尾神社}} * {{ill|Atago Shrine|ja|愛宕神社}} * {{ill|Wakazumi Shrine|ja|別雷神社}} * {{ill|Fuji Shrine|ja|富士神社}} * {{ill|Awashima Shrine|ja|淡島神社}} File:Ota-tenmangu in Wakamiya-hachimanhu (Hitachiota).jpg|Kitano Tenjin Shrine File:Keidaisha1 in Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|Ichihime Shrine, Wakazumi Shrine, Atago Shrine File:Ashio-jinja in Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|Ashio Shrine File:Fuji-jinja in Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|Fuji Shrine == Access == The shrine is approximately 1.5 km north of {{ill|Hitachi-Ōta Station|ja|常陸太田駅}} on the {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|ja|東日本旅客鉄道}} (JR). It is a 20-minute walk. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Shinto shrines|ja|神社一覧}} * {{ill|List of Japanese deities|ja|日本の神の一覧}} == External Links == * {{Instagram|wakamiyah}} * {{Twitter|wakamiya_h}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Ibaraki Prefecture Shrine Index}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Former Gōsha]] [[Category:Satake clan]] [[Category:Architecture in Hitachiōta]] [[Category:History of Hitachiōta]] [[Category:Emperor Nintoku]] [[zh:若宮八幡宮]] [[ja:%E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E5%AE%AE_(%E5%B8%B8%E9%99%B8%E5%A4%AA%E7%94%B0%E5%B8%82)]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)}} [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:New uncategorized original categories]] [[Category:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]][[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)}} [[Category:New uncategorized original categories]] [[Category:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]][[Category:qq]][[en:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]][[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:if these are dates then move them to completion tier 5]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]][[Category:if these are dates then move them to completion tier 5]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]][[Category:qq]]

{{Cite sources|date=2019-03-12}} {{Shrine |name=Wakamiya Hachimangū (若宮八幡宮) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Wakamiyahachimaｎgu.JPG|250px]]
Main hall (September 2005) |location={{ill|Hitachiōta|ja|常陸太田市}}, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県}} Miyamoto-chō 2344 |coordinates={{coord2|36|32|34.8|N|140|31|14.6|E|type:landmark_region:JP-08|display=inline,title|name=Wakamiya Hachimangū}} |enshrined_deities=[[Ōsasagi no Mikoto]] ({{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}})
[[Ukanomitama no Mikoto]] |established={{ill|Ōei era|ja|応永}} (circa 1400) |shrine_rank=Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |annual_festival=See [[#Festivals]] |main_hall= |map={{location map|Japan Ibaraki|label=Wakamiya Hachimangū|coordinates= {{coord2|36|32|34.8|N|140|31|14.6|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|32|34.8|N|140|31|14.6|E}}}} }} [[File:Torii of Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|thumb|right|245px|Torii]] [[File:Shinmon of Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|thumb|right|245px|Shinmon (gate)]] '''Wakamiya Hachimangū''' (若宮八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Hitachiōta|ja|常陸太田市}}, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県}}, Japan. The shrine's rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度}} was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == History == The shrine was established during the {{ill|Ōei era|ja|応永}} (circa 1400) by {{ill|Yoshihito Satake|ja|佐竹義人}}, who invited its deity from {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|ja|鶴岡八幡宮}} in {{ill|Kamakura|ja|鎌倉市}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}. It was originally enshrined within {{ill|Ōta Castle (Hitachi Province)|ja|太田城 (常陸国)}} as a guardian deity and served as the prayer site for the {{ill|Satake clan|ja|佐竹氏}}. Research suggests that the Satake clan initially revered a {{ill|Hachiman shrine|ja|八幡社}} derived from {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮}}, which was the guardian shrine of the Minamoto clan in Hitachi. However, Yoshihito Satake, who had ties with the {{ill|Uesugi clan|ja|上杉氏}}, instead adopted the enshrinement of Tsurugaoka Hachimangū, possibly against opposition from the Satake family.Teiko Tsutsumi, "Satake Clan and Hachiman Worship," {{ill|Ibaraki Prefectural History Museum Bulletin|ja|茨城県立歴史館報}} No. 28, 2001. Compiled in {{ill|Research Series on Medieval Kanto Warriors Vol. 30: Hitachi Satake Clan|ja|シリーズ・中世関東武士の研究 第三十巻 常陸佐竹氏}} (Kōshōshō Publishing, 2021), pp. 192–227. Although the Satake clan was later relocated to {{ill|Akita|ja|秋田}} after the {{ill|Battle of Sekigahara|ja|関ヶ原の戦い}}, they took the shrine's deity with them to {{ill|Ōdate Castle|ja|大館城}} as its guardian spirit. The deity was subsequently enshrined at {{ill|Ōdate Hachiman Shrine|ja|大館八幡神社}}, continuing its role as a protector.{{Cite web|title=八幡神社 |url=http://akita-jinjacho.sakura.ne.jp/tatsujin_etc/kennsaku/oodate/67_hachiman_hachiman.html |publisher=Akita Prefecture Shrine Association |accessdate=2015-10-12}} In {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} 14 (1609), the first {{ill|daimyō|ja|大名}} of the {{ill|Mito Domain|ja|水戸藩}}, {{ill|Tokugawa Yorifusa|ja|徳川頼房}}, was healed of an illness after praying at the shrine. Thereafter, the {{ill|Mito Tokugawa clan|ja|水戸徳川家}} held the shrine in high reverence. In {{ill|Genroku|ja|元禄}} 5 (1692), the second daimyō, {{ill|Tokugawa Mitsukuni|ja|徳川光圀}} (popularly known as Mito Kōmon), designated the shrine as a guardian of the Ōta district. In {{ill|Hōei|ja|宝永}} 5 (1708), the shrine was relocated to its current location alongside the {{ill|Ōta Inari Shrine|ja|太田稲荷神社}}, which had been worshiped together with it. In 1940 (Showa 15), based on the tradition that a woman named Tsuruko accompanied the deity’s transfer from Tsurugaoka Hachimangū and oversaw its rituals, the shrine's parishioner priestesses revived the "Tsuruko Dance." Recently, this {{ill|kagura|ja|神楽}} offering has been expanded to include performances such as {{ill|Urayasu no Mai|ja|浦安の舞}}, {{ill|Toyo no Sakai no Mai|ja|豊栄の舞}}, and {{ill|Yaotome no Mai|ja|八乙女舞}}, which are performed with grace during major festivals. [[File:Kaguraden of Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|thumb|right|245px|Kaguraden]] == Enshrined Deities == * '''[[Ōsasagi no Mikoto]]''' (Emperor Nintoku) * '''[[Ukanomitama no Mikoto]]''' == Festivals == * January: {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|元旦祭|Saitan-sai=Saitan-sai|lt=Saitan-sai (New Year's Festival)}} * February: {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分追儺祭|lt=Setsubun Tsuina-sai}} * May: festival * June: {{ill|Nagoshi no Harae|ja|夏越の祓}} * September: Autumn Festival * December: {{ill|Ōharae|ja|大祓}} == Cultural Properties == * Large {{ill|zelkova|ja|ケヤキ}} tree (designated a natural monument of Ibaraki Prefecture) * Hanging scroll of Hachiman as a monk * Stone sword artifacts * Military fan (attributed to {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|ja|源義光}}) == Auxiliary Shrines == * {{ill|Kitano Tenjin Shrine|ja|北野天神社}} (Plum Tenjin) – Established in {{ill|Kōhei 6|ja|康平}} (1062) by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|ja|源義家}} to pray for victory in the {{ill|Earlier Nine Years' War|ja|前九年の役}}. * {{ill|Ichihime Shrine|ja|市比売神社}} * {{ill|Ashio Shrine|ja|足尾神社}} * {{ill|Atago Shrine|ja|愛宕神社}} * {{ill|Wakazumi Shrine|ja|別雷神社}} * {{ill|Fuji Shrine|ja|富士神社}} * {{ill|Awashima Shrine|ja|淡島神社}} File:Ota-tenmangu in Wakamiya-hachimanhu (Hitachiota).jpg|Kitano Tenjin Shrine File:Keidaisha1 in Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|Ichihime Shrine, Wakazumi Shrine, Atago Shrine File:Ashio-jinja in Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|Ashio Shrine File:Fuji-jinja in Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|Fuji Shrine == Access == The shrine is approximately 1.5 km north of {{ill|Hitachi-Ōta Station|ja|常陸太田駅}} on the {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|ja|東日本旅客鉄道}} (JR). It is a 20-minute walk. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Shinto shrines|ja|神社一覧}} * {{ill|List of Japanese deities|ja|日本の神の一覧}} == External Links == * {{Instagram|wakamiyah}} * {{Twitter|wakamiya_h}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Ibaraki Prefecture Shrine Index}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Former Gōsha]] [[Category:Satake clan]] [[Category:Architecture in Hitachiōta]] [[Category:History of Hitachiōta]] [[Category:Emperor Nintoku]] [[zh:若宮八幡宮]] [[ja:%E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E5%AE%AE_(%E5%B8%B8%E9%99%B8%E5%A4%AA%E7%94%B0%E5%B8%82)]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)}} [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]][[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)}} [[Category:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]][[Category:qq]][[en:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]][[Category:qqq]] [[Category:if these are dates then move them to completion tier 5]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]][[Category:if these are dates then move them to completion tier 5]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]][[Category:qq]]
