[[File:Kaguraden of Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|thumb|right|245px|Kaguraden]] == Enshrined Deities == * '''[[Ōsasagi no Mikoto]]''' (Emperor Nintoku) * '''[[Ukanomitama no Mikoto]]''' == Festivals == * January: {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|元旦祭|Saitan-sai=Saitan-sai|lt=Saitan-sai (New Year's Festival)|12=draft}} * February: {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分追儺祭|lt=Setsubun Tsuina-sai|12=draft}} * May: festival * June: {{ill|Nagoshi no Harae|ja|夏越の祓|12=draft}} * September: Autumn Festival * December: {{ill|Ōharae|ja|大祓|12=draft}} == Cultural Properties == * Large {{ill|zelkova|ja|ケヤキ|12=draft}} tree (designated a natural monument of Ibaraki Prefecture) * Hanging scroll of Hachiman as a monk * Stone sword artifacts * Military fan (attributed to {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|ja|源義光|12=draft}}) == Auxiliary Shrines == * {{ill|Kitano Tenjin Shrine|ja|北野天神社|12=draft}} (Plum Tenjin) – Established in {{ill|Kōhei 6|ja|康平|12=draft}} (1062) by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|ja|源義家|12=draft}} to pray for victory in the {{ill|Earlier Nine Years' War|ja|前九年の役|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Ichihime Shrine|ja|市比売神社|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ashio Shrine|ja|足尾神社|12=draft}} * {{ill|Atago Shrine|ja|愛宕神社|12=draft}} * {{ill|Wakazumi Shrine|ja|別雷神社|12=draft}} * {{ill|Fuji Shrine|ja|富士神社|12=draft}} * {{ill|Awashima Shrine|ja|淡島神社|12=draft}} File:Ota-tenmangu in Wakamiya-hachimanhu (Hitachiota).jpg|Kitano Tenjin Shrine File:Keidaisha1 in Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|Ichihime Shrine, Wakazumi Shrine, Atago Shrine File:Ashio-jinja in Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|Ashio Shrine File:Fuji-jinja in Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|Fuji Shrine == Access == The shrine is approximately 1.5 km north of {{ill|Hitachi-Ōta Station|ja|常陸太田駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|ja|東日本旅客鉄道|12=draft}} (JR). It is a 20-minute walk. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Shinto shrines|ja|神社一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Japanese deities|ja|日本の神の一覧|12=draft}} == External Links == * {{Instagram|wakamiyah}} * {{Twitter|wakamiya_h}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Ibaraki Prefecture Shrine Index}} {{citation needed|Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)}} {{citation needed|Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:Satake clan]] [[Category:Architecture in Hitachiōta]] [[Category:History of Hitachiōta]] [[Category:Emperor Nintoku]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:March 25th orphaned pages]] [[Category:March 28 orphaned pages]] [[Category:retagging the orphans]] [[Category:ophans as of March 31]] }} [[zh:若宮八幡宮]] [[ja:%E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E5%AE%AE_(%E5%B8%B8%E9%99%B8%E5%A4%AA%E7%94%B0%E5%B8%82)]] [[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]] [[en:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]] [[Category:orphaned pages as of April 8]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]][[Category:qq]]

{{Cite sources|date=2019-03-12}} {{Shrine |name=Wakamiya Hachimangū (若宮八幡宮) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Wakamiyahachimaｎgu.JPG|250px]]
Main hall (September 2005) |location={{ill|Hitachiōta|ja|常陸太田市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県|12=draft}} Miyamoto-chō 2344 |coordinates={{coord2|36|32|34.8|N|140|31|14.6|E|type:landmark_region:JP-08|display=inline,title|name=Wakamiya Hachimangū}} |enshrined_deities=[[Ōsasagi no Mikoto]] ({{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇|12=draft}})
[[Ukanomitama no Mikoto]] |established={{ill|Ōei era|ja|応永|12=draft}} (circa 1400) |shrine_rank=Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft}} |annual_festival=See [[#Festivals]] |main_hall= |map={{location map|Japan Ibaraki|label=Wakamiya Hachimangū|coordinates= {{coord2|36|32|34.8|N|140|31|14.6|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|32|34.8|N|140|31|14.6|E}}}} }} [[File:Torii of Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|thumb|right|245px|Torii]] [[File:Shinmon of Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|thumb|right|245px|Shinmon (gate)]] '''Wakamiya Hachimangū''' (若宮八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Hitachiōta|ja|常陸太田市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県|12=draft}}, Japan. The shrine's rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=draft}} was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft}}. == History == The shrine was established during the {{ill|Ōei era|ja|応永|12=draft}} (circa 1400) by {{ill|Yoshihito Satake|ja|佐竹義人|12=draft}}, who invited its deity from {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|ja|鶴岡八幡宮|12=draft}} in {{ill|Kamakura|ja|鎌倉市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県|12=draft}}. It was originally enshrined within {{ill|Ōta Castle (Hitachi Province)|ja|太田城 (常陸国)|12=draft}} as a guardian deity and served as the prayer site for the {{ill|Satake clan|ja|佐竹氏|12=draft}}. Research suggests that the Satake clan initially revered a {{ill|Hachiman shrine|ja|八幡社|12=draft}} derived from {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮|12=draft}}, which was the guardian shrine of the Minamoto clan in Hitachi. However, Yoshihito Satake, who had ties with the {{ill|Uesugi clan|ja|上杉氏|12=draft}}, instead adopted the enshrinement of Tsurugaoka Hachimangū, possibly against opposition from the Satake family.Teiko Tsutsumi, "Satake Clan and Hachiman Worship," {{ill|Ibaraki Prefectural History Museum Bulletin|ja|茨城県立歴史館報|12=draft}} No. 28, 2001. Compiled in {{ill|Research Series on Medieval Kanto Warriors Vol. 30: Hitachi Satake Clan|ja|シリーズ・中世関東武士の研究 第三十巻 常陸佐竹氏|12=draft}} (Kōshōshō Publishing, 2021), pp. 192–227. Although the Satake clan was later relocated to {{ill|Akita|ja|秋田|12=draft}} after the {{ill|Battle of Sekigahara|ja|関ヶ原の戦い|12=draft}}, they took the shrine's deity with them to {{ill|Ōdate Castle|ja|大館城|12=draft}} as its guardian spirit. The deity was subsequently enshrined at {{ill|Ōdate Hachiman Shrine|ja|大館八幡神社|12=draft}}, continuing its role as a protector.{{Cite web|title=八幡神社 |url=http://akita-jinjacho.sakura.ne.jp/tatsujin_etc/kennsaku/oodate/67_hachiman_hachiman.html |publisher=Akita Prefecture Shrine Association |accessdate=2015-10-12}} In {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長|12=draft}} 14 (1609), the first {{ill|daimyō|ja|大名|12=draft}} of the {{ill|Mito Domain|ja|水戸藩|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tokugawa Yorifusa|ja|徳川頼房|12=draft}}, was healed of an illness after praying at the shrine. Thereafter, the {{ill|Mito Tokugawa clan|ja|水戸徳川家|12=draft}} held the shrine in high reverence. In {{ill|Genroku|ja|元禄|12=draft}} 5 (1692), the second daimyō, {{ill|Tokugawa Mitsukuni|ja|徳川光圀|12=draft}} (popularly known as Mito Kōmon), designated the shrine as a guardian of the Ōta district. In {{ill|Hōei|ja|宝永|12=draft}} 5 (1708), the shrine was relocated to its current location alongside the {{ill|Ōta Inari Shrine|ja|太田稲荷神社|12=draft}}, which had been worshiped together with it. In 1940 (Showa 15), based on the tradition that a woman named Tsuruko accompanied the deity’s transfer from Tsurugaoka Hachimangū and oversaw its rituals, the shrine's parishioner priestesses revived the "Tsuruko Dance." Recently, this {{ill|kagura|ja|神楽|12=draft}} offering has been expanded to include performances such as {{ill|Urayasu no Mai|ja|浦安の舞|12=draft}}, {{ill|Toyo no Sakai no Mai|ja|豊栄の舞|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Yaotome no Mai|ja|八乙女舞|12=draft}}, which are performed with grace during major festivals. [[File:Kaguraden of Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|thumb|right|245px|Kaguraden]] == Enshrined Deities == * '''[[Ōsasagi no Mikoto]]''' (Emperor Nintoku) * '''[[Ukanomitama no Mikoto]]''' == Festivals == * January: {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|元旦祭|Saitan-sai=Saitan-sai|lt=Saitan-sai (New Year's Festival)|12=draft}} * February: {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分追儺祭|lt=Setsubun Tsuina-sai|12=draft}} * May: festival * June: {{ill|Nagoshi no Harae|ja|夏越の祓|12=draft}} * September: Autumn Festival * December: {{ill|Ōharae|ja|大祓|12=draft}} == Cultural Properties == * Large {{ill|zelkova|ja|ケヤキ|12=draft}} tree (designated a natural monument of Ibaraki Prefecture) * Hanging scroll of Hachiman as a monk * Stone sword artifacts * Military fan (attributed to {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|ja|源義光|12=draft}}) == Auxiliary Shrines == * {{ill|Kitano Tenjin Shrine|ja|北野天神社|12=draft}} (Plum Tenjin) – Established in {{ill|Kōhei 6|ja|康平|12=draft}} (1062) by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|ja|源義家|12=draft}} to pray for victory in the {{ill|Earlier Nine Years' War|ja|前九年の役|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Ichihime Shrine|ja|市比売神社|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ashio Shrine|ja|足尾神社|12=draft}} * {{ill|Atago Shrine|ja|愛宕神社|12=draft}} * {{ill|Wakazumi Shrine|ja|別雷神社|12=draft}} * {{ill|Fuji Shrine|ja|富士神社|12=draft}} * {{ill|Awashima Shrine|ja|淡島神社|12=draft}} File:Ota-tenmangu in Wakamiya-hachimanhu (Hitachiota).jpg|Kitano Tenjin Shrine File:Keidaisha1 in Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|Ichihime Shrine, Wakazumi Shrine, Atago Shrine File:Ashio-jinja in Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|Ashio Shrine File:Fuji-jinja in Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|Fuji Shrine == Access == The shrine is approximately 1.5 km north of {{ill|Hitachi-Ōta Station|ja|常陸太田駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|ja|東日本旅客鉄道|12=draft}} (JR). It is a 20-minute walk. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Shinto shrines|ja|神社一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Japanese deities|ja|日本の神の一覧|12=draft}} == External Links == * {{Instagram|wakamiyah}} * {{Twitter|wakamiya_h}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Ibaraki Prefecture Shrine Index}} {{citation needed|Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)}} {{citation needed|Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:Satake clan]] [[Category:Architecture in Hitachiōta]] [[Category:History of Hitachiōta]] [[Category:Emperor Nintoku]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:March 25th orphaned pages]] [[Category:March 28 orphaned pages]] [[Category:retagging the orphans]] [[Category:ophans as of March 31]] }} [[zh:若宮八幡宮]] [[ja:%E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E5%AE%AE_(%E5%B8%B8%E9%99%B8%E5%A4%AA%E7%94%B0%E5%B8%82)]] [[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]] [[en:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]] [[Category:orphaned pages as of April 8]] [[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]]

{{Cite sources|date=2019-03-12}} {{Shrine |name=Wakamiya Hachimangū (若宮八幡宮) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Wakamiyahachimaｎgu.JPG|250px]]
Main hall (September 2005) |location={{ill|Hitachiōta|ja|常陸太田市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県|12=draft}} Miyamoto-chō 2344 |coordinates={{coord2|36|32|34.8|N|140|31|14.6|E|type:landmark_region:JP-08|display=inline,title|name=Wakamiya Hachimangū}} |enshrined_deities=[[Ōsasagi no Mikoto]] ({{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇|12=draft}})
[[Ukanomitama no Mikoto]] |established={{ill|Ōei era|ja|応永|12=draft}} (circa 1400) |shrine_rank=Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft}} |annual_festival=See [[#Festivals]] |main_hall= |map={{location map|Japan Ibaraki|label=Wakamiya Hachimangū|coordinates= {{coord2|36|32|34.8|N|140|31|14.6|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|32|34.8|N|140|31|14.6|E}}}} }} [[File:Torii of Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|thumb|right|245px|Torii]] [[File:Shinmon of Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|thumb|right|245px|Shinmon (gate)]] '''Wakamiya Hachimangū''' (若宮八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Hitachiōta|ja|常陸太田市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県|12=draft}}, Japan. The shrine's rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=draft}} was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft}}. == History == The shrine was established during the {{ill|Ōei era|ja|応永|12=draft}} (circa 1400) by {{ill|Yoshihito Satake|ja|佐竹義人|12=draft}}, who invited its deity from {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|ja|鶴岡八幡宮|12=draft}} in {{ill|Kamakura|ja|鎌倉市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県|12=draft}}. It was originally enshrined within {{ill|Ōta Castle (Hitachi Province)|ja|太田城 (常陸国)|12=draft}} as a guardian deity and served as the prayer site for the {{ill|Satake clan|ja|佐竹氏|12=draft}}. Research suggests that the Satake clan initially revered a {{ill|Hachiman shrine|ja|八幡社|12=draft}} derived from {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮|12=draft}}, which was the guardian shrine of the Minamoto clan in Hitachi. However, Yoshihito Satake, who had ties with the {{ill|Uesugi clan|ja|上杉氏|12=draft}}, instead adopted the enshrinement of Tsurugaoka Hachimangū, possibly against opposition from the Satake family.Teiko Tsutsumi, "Satake Clan and Hachiman Worship," {{ill|Ibaraki Prefectural History Museum Bulletin|ja|茨城県立歴史館報|12=draft}} No. 28, 2001. Compiled in {{ill|Research Series on Medieval Kanto Warriors Vol. 30: Hitachi Satake Clan|ja|シリーズ・中世関東武士の研究 第三十巻 常陸佐竹氏|12=draft}} (Kōshōshō Publishing, 2021), pp. 192–227. Although the Satake clan was later relocated to {{ill|Akita|ja|秋田|12=draft}} after the {{ill|Battle of Sekigahara|ja|関ヶ原の戦い|12=draft}}, they took the shrine's deity with them to {{ill|Ōdate Castle|ja|大館城|12=draft}} as its guardian spirit. The deity was subsequently enshrined at {{ill|Ōdate Hachiman Shrine|ja|大館八幡神社|12=draft}}, continuing its role as a protector.{{Cite web|title=八幡神社 |url=http://akita-jinjacho.sakura.ne.jp/tatsujin_etc/kennsaku/oodate/67_hachiman_hachiman.html |publisher=Akita Prefecture Shrine Association |accessdate=2015-10-12}} In {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長|12=draft}} 14 (1609), the first {{ill|daimyō|ja|大名|12=draft}} of the {{ill|Mito Domain|ja|水戸藩|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tokugawa Yorifusa|ja|徳川頼房|12=draft}}, was healed of an illness after praying at the shrine. Thereafter, the {{ill|Mito Tokugawa clan|ja|水戸徳川家|12=draft}} held the shrine in high reverence. In {{ill|Genroku|ja|元禄|12=draft}} 5 (1692), the second daimyō, {{ill|Tokugawa Mitsukuni|ja|徳川光圀|12=draft}} (popularly known as Mito Kōmon), designated the shrine as a guardian of the Ōta district. In {{ill|Hōei|ja|宝永|12=draft}} 5 (1708), the shrine was relocated to its current location alongside the {{ill|Ōta Inari Shrine|ja|太田稲荷神社|12=draft}}, which had been worshiped together with it. In 1940 (Showa 15), based on the tradition that a woman named Tsuruko accompanied the deity’s transfer from Tsurugaoka Hachimangū and oversaw its rituals, the shrine's parishioner priestesses revived the "Tsuruko Dance." Recently, this {{ill|kagura|ja|神楽|12=draft}} offering has been expanded to include performances such as {{ill|Urayasu no Mai|ja|浦安の舞|12=draft}}, {{ill|Toyo no Sakai no Mai|ja|豊栄の舞|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Yaotome no Mai|ja|八乙女舞|12=draft}}, which are performed with grace during major festivals. [[File:Kaguraden of Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|thumb|right|245px|Kaguraden]] == Enshrined Deities == * '''[[Ōsasagi no Mikoto]]''' (Emperor Nintoku) * '''[[Ukanomitama no Mikoto]]''' == Festivals == * January: {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|元旦祭|Saitan-sai=Saitan-sai|lt=Saitan-sai (New Year's Festival)|12=draft}} * February: {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分追儺祭|lt=Setsubun Tsuina-sai|12=draft}} * May: festival * June: {{ill|Nagoshi no Harae|ja|夏越の祓|12=draft}} * September: Autumn Festival * December: {{ill|Ōharae|ja|大祓|12=draft}} == Cultural Properties == * Large {{ill|zelkova|ja|ケヤキ|12=draft}} tree (designated a natural monument of Ibaraki Prefecture) * Hanging scroll of Hachiman as a monk * Stone sword artifacts * Military fan (attributed to {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|ja|源義光|12=draft}}) == Auxiliary Shrines == * {{ill|Kitano Tenjin Shrine|ja|北野天神社|12=draft}} (Plum Tenjin) – Established in {{ill|Kōhei 6|ja|康平|12=draft}} (1062) by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|ja|源義家|12=draft}} to pray for victory in the {{ill|Earlier Nine Years' War|ja|前九年の役|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Ichihime Shrine|ja|市比売神社|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ashio Shrine|ja|足尾神社|12=draft}} * {{ill|Atago Shrine|ja|愛宕神社|12=draft}} * {{ill|Wakazumi Shrine|ja|別雷神社|12=draft}} * {{ill|Fuji Shrine|ja|富士神社|12=draft}} * {{ill|Awashima Shrine|ja|淡島神社|12=draft}} File:Ota-tenmangu in Wakamiya-hachimanhu (Hitachiota).jpg|Kitano Tenjin Shrine File:Keidaisha1 in Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|Ichihime Shrine, Wakazumi Shrine, Atago Shrine File:Ashio-jinja in Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|Ashio Shrine File:Fuji-jinja in Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|Fuji Shrine == Access == The shrine is approximately 1.5 km north of {{ill|Hitachi-Ōta Station|ja|常陸太田駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|ja|東日本旅客鉄道|12=draft}} (JR). It is a 20-minute walk. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Shinto shrines|ja|神社一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Japanese deities|ja|日本の神の一覧|12=draft}} == External Links == * {{Instagram|wakamiyah}} * {{Twitter|wakamiya_h}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Ibaraki Prefecture Shrine Index}} {{citation needed|Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)}} {{citation needed|Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:Satake clan]] [[Category:Architecture in Hitachiōta]] [[Category:History of Hitachiōta]] [[Category:Emperor Nintoku]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:March 25th orphaned pages]] [[Category:March 28 orphaned pages]] [[Category:ophans as of March 31]] }} [[zh:若宮八幡宮]] [[ja:%E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E5%AE%AE_(%E5%B8%B8%E9%99%B8%E5%A4%AA%E7%94%B0%E5%B8%82)]] [[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]] [[en:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]] [[Category:orphaned pages as of April 8]] [[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]] [[Category:Orphans as of April 16]]

[[File:Kaguraden of Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|thumb|right|245px|Kaguraden]] == Enshrined Deities == * '''[[Ōsasagi no Mikoto]]''' (Emperor Nintoku) * '''[[Ukanomitama no Mikoto]]''' == Festivals == * January: {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|元旦祭|Saitan-sai=Saitan-sai|lt=Saitan-sai (New Year's Festival)|12=draft}} * February: {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分追儺祭|lt=Setsubun Tsuina-sai|12=draft}} * May: festival * June: {{ill|Nagoshi no Harae|ja|夏越の祓|12=draft}} * September: Autumn Festival * December: {{ill|Ōharae|ja|大祓|12=draft}} == Cultural Properties == * Large {{ill|zelkova|ja|ケヤキ|12=draft}} tree (designated a natural monument of Ibaraki Prefecture) * Hanging scroll of Hachiman as a monk * Stone sword artifacts * Military fan (attributed to {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|ja|源義光|12=draft}}) == Auxiliary Shrines == * {{ill|Kitano Tenjin Shrine|ja|北野天神社|12=draft}} (Plum Tenjin) – Established in {{ill|Kōhei 6|ja|康平|12=draft}} (1062) by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|ja|源義家|12=draft}} to pray for victory in the {{ill|Earlier Nine Years' War|ja|前九年の役|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Ichihime Shrine|ja|市比売神社|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ashio Shrine|ja|足尾神社|12=draft}} * {{ill|Atago Shrine|ja|愛宕神社|12=draft}} * {{ill|Wakazumi Shrine|ja|別雷神社|12=draft}} * {{ill|Fuji Shrine|ja|富士神社|12=draft}} * {{ill|Awashima Shrine|ja|淡島神社|12=draft}} File:Ota-tenmangu in Wakamiya-hachimanhu (Hitachiota).jpg|Kitano Tenjin Shrine File:Keidaisha1 in Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|Ichihime Shrine, Wakazumi Shrine, Atago Shrine File:Ashio-jinja in Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|Ashio Shrine File:Fuji-jinja in Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|Fuji Shrine == Access == The shrine is approximately 1.5 km north of {{ill|Hitachi-Ōta Station|ja|常陸太田駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|ja|東日本旅客鉄道|12=draft}} (JR). It is a 20-minute walk. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Shinto shrines|ja|神社一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Japanese deities|ja|日本の神の一覧|12=draft}} == External Links == * {{Instagram|wakamiyah}} * {{Twitter|wakamiya_h}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Ibaraki Prefecture Shrine Index}} {{citation needed|Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)}} {{citation needed|Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:Satake clan]] [[Category:Architecture in Hitachiōta]] [[Category:History of Hitachiōta]] [[Category:Emperor Nintoku]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:March 25th orphaned pages]] [[Category:ophans as of March 31]] }} [[zh:若宮八幡宮]] [[ja:%E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E5%AE%AE_(%E5%B8%B8%E9%99%B8%E5%A4%AA%E7%94%B0%E5%B8%82)]] [[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]] [[en:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]] [[Category:orphaned pages as of April 8]] [[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]] [[Category:Orphans as of April 16]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]][[Category:qq]]

revision.text:
{{Cite sources|date=2019-03-12}} {{Shrine |name=Wakamiya Hachimangū (若宮八幡宮) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Wakamiyahachimaｎgu.JPG|250px]]
Main hall (September 2005) |location={{ill|Hitachiōta|ja|常陸太田市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県|12=draft}} Miyamoto-chō 2344 |coordinates={{coord2|36|32|34.8|N|140|31|14.6|E|type:landmark_region:JP-08|display=inline,title|name=Wakamiya Hachimangū}} |enshrined_deities=[[Ōsasagi no Mikoto]] ({{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇|12=draft}})
[[Ukanomitama no Mikoto]] |established={{ill|Ōei era|ja|応永|12=draft}} (circa 1400) |shrine_rank=Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft}} |annual_festival=See [[#Festivals]] |main_hall= |map={{location map|Japan Ibaraki|label=Wakamiya Hachimangū|coordinates= {{coord2|36|32|34.8|N|140|31|14.6|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|32|34.8|N|140|31|14.6|E}}}} }} [[File:Torii of Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|thumb|right|245px|Torii]] [[File:Shinmon of Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|thumb|right|245px|Shinmon (gate)]] '''Wakamiya Hachimangū''' (若宮八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Hitachiōta|ja|常陸太田市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県|12=draft}}, Japan. The shrine's rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=draft}} was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft}}. == History == The shrine was established during the {{ill|Ōei era|ja|応永|12=draft}} (circa 1400) by {{ill|Yoshihito Satake|ja|佐竹義人|12=draft}}, who invited its deity from {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|ja|鶴岡八幡宮|12=draft}} in {{ill|Kamakura|ja|鎌倉市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県|12=draft}}. It was originally enshrined within {{ill|Ōta Castle (Hitachi Province)|ja|太田城 (常陸国)|12=draft}} as a guardian deity and served as the prayer site for the {{ill|Satake clan|ja|佐竹氏|12=draft}}. Research suggests that the Satake clan initially revered a {{ill|Hachiman shrine|ja|八幡社|12=draft}} derived from {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮|12=draft}}, which was the guardian shrine of the Minamoto clan in Hitachi. However, Yoshihito Satake, who had ties with the {{ill|Uesugi clan|ja|上杉氏|12=draft}}, instead adopted the enshrinement of Tsurugaoka Hachimangū, possibly against opposition from the Satake family.Teiko Tsutsumi, "Satake Clan and Hachiman Worship," {{ill|Ibaraki Prefectural History Museum Bulletin|ja|茨城県立歴史館報|12=draft}} No. 28, 2001. Compiled in {{ill|Research Series on Medieval Kanto Warriors Vol. 30: Hitachi Satake Clan|ja|シリーズ・中世関東武士の研究 第三十巻 常陸佐竹氏|12=draft}} (Kōshōshō Publishing, 2021), pp. 192–227. Although the Satake clan was later relocated to {{ill|Akita|ja|秋田|12=draft}} after the {{ill|Battle of Sekigahara|ja|関ヶ原の戦い|12=draft}}, they took the shrine's deity with them to {{ill|Ōdate Castle|ja|大館城|12=draft}} as its guardian spirit. The deity was subsequently enshrined at {{ill|Ōdate Hachiman Shrine|ja|大館八幡神社|12=draft}}, continuing its role as a protector.{{Cite web|title=八幡神社 |url=http://akita-jinjacho.sakura.ne.jp/tatsujin_etc/kennsaku/oodate/67_hachiman_hachiman.html |publisher=Akita Prefecture Shrine Association |accessdate=2015-10-12}} In {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長|12=draft}} 14 (1609), the first {{ill|daimyō|ja|大名|12=draft}} of the {{ill|Mito Domain|ja|水戸藩|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tokugawa Yorifusa|ja|徳川頼房|12=draft}}, was healed of an illness after praying at the shrine. Thereafter, the {{ill|Mito Tokugawa clan|ja|水戸徳川家|12=draft}} held the shrine in high reverence. In {{ill|Genroku|ja|元禄|12=draft}} 5 (1692), the second daimyō, {{ill|Tokugawa Mitsukuni|ja|徳川光圀|12=draft}} (popularly known as Mito Kōmon), designated the shrine as a guardian of the Ōta district. In {{ill|Hōei|ja|宝永|12=draft}} 5 (1708), the shrine was relocated to its current location alongside the {{ill|Ōta Inari Shrine|ja|太田稲荷神社|12=draft}}, which had been worshiped together with it. In 1940 (Showa 15), based on the tradition that a woman named Tsuruko accompanied the deity’s transfer from Tsurugaoka Hachimangū and oversaw its rituals, the shrine's parishioner priestesses revived the "Tsuruko Dance." Recently, this {{ill|kagura|ja|神楽|12=draft}} offering has been expanded to include performances such as {{ill|Urayasu no Mai|ja|浦安の舞|12=draft}}, {{ill|Toyo no Sakai no Mai|ja|豊栄の舞|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Yaotome no Mai|ja|八乙女舞|12=draft}}, which are performed with grace during major festivals. [[File:Kaguraden of Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|thumb|right|245px|Kaguraden]] == Enshrined Deities == * '''[[Ōsasagi no Mikoto]]''' (Emperor Nintoku) * '''[[Ukanomitama no Mikoto]]''' == Festivals == * January: {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|元旦祭|Saitan-sai=Saitan-sai|lt=Saitan-sai (New Year's Festival)|12=draft}} * February: {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分追儺祭|lt=Setsubun Tsuina-sai|12=draft}} * May: festival * June: {{ill|Nagoshi no Harae|ja|夏越の祓|12=draft}} * September: Autumn Festival * December: {{ill|Ōharae|ja|大祓|12=draft}} == Cultural Properties == * Large {{ill|zelkova|ja|ケヤキ|12=draft}} tree (designated a natural monument of Ibaraki Prefecture) * Hanging scroll of Hachiman as a monk * Stone sword artifacts * Military fan (attributed to {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|ja|源義光|12=draft}}) == Auxiliary Shrines == * {{ill|Kitano Tenjin Shrine|ja|北野天神社|12=draft}} (Plum Tenjin) – Established in {{ill|Kōhei 6|ja|康平|12=draft}} (1062) by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|ja|源義家|12=draft}} to pray for victory in the {{ill|Earlier Nine Years' War|ja|前九年の役|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Ichihime Shrine|ja|市比売神社|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ashio Shrine|ja|足尾神社|12=draft}} * {{ill|Atago Shrine|ja|愛宕神社|12=draft}} * {{ill|Wakazumi Shrine|ja|別雷神社|12=draft}} * {{ill|Fuji Shrine|ja|富士神社|12=draft}} * {{ill|Awashima Shrine|ja|淡島神社|12=draft}} File:Ota-tenmangu in Wakamiya-hachimanhu (Hitachiota).jpg|Kitano Tenjin Shrine File:Keidaisha1 in Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|Ichihime Shrine, Wakazumi Shrine, Atago Shrine File:Ashio-jinja in Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|Ashio Shrine File:Fuji-jinja in Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|Fuji Shrine == Access == The shrine is approximately 1.5 km north of {{ill|Hitachi-Ōta Station|ja|常陸太田駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|ja|東日本旅客鉄道|12=draft}} (JR). It is a 20-minute walk. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Shinto shrines|ja|神社一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Japanese deities|ja|日本の神の一覧|12=draft}} == External Links == * {{Instagram|wakamiyah}} * {{Twitter|wakamiya_h}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Ibaraki Prefecture Shrine Index}} {{citation needed|Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)}} {{citation needed|Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:Satake clan]] [[Category:Architecture in Hitachiōta]] [[Category:History of Hitachiōta]] [[Category:Emperor Nintoku]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:March 25th orphaned pages]] }} [[zh:若宮八幡宮]] [[ja:%E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E5%AE%AE_(%E5%B8%B8%E9%99%B8%E5%A4%AA%E7%94%B0%E5%B8%82)]] [[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]] [[en:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]] [[Category:orphaned pages as of April 8]] [[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]] [[Category:Orphans as of April 16]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]][[Category:qq]]

revision.text:
{{出典の明記|date=2019-03-12}} {{神社 |名称=若宮八幡宮 |画像=[[画像:Wakamiyahachimaｎgu.JPG|250px]]
拝殿（2005年9月） |所在地=[[茨城県]][[常陸太田市]]宮本町2344 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|32|34.8|N|140|31|14.6|E|type:landmark_region:JP-08|display=inline,title|name=若宮八幡宮}} |祭神=[[大鷦鷯尊]]（[[仁徳天皇]]）
[[倉稲魂命]] |創建=[[応永]]年間（[[1400年]]頃） |社格=旧[[郷社]] |例祭=[[#祭事]]を参照 |本殿= |地図={{Location map|Japan Ibaraki|label=若宮八幡宮|coordinates= {{coord2|36|32|34.8|N|140|31|14.6|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|32|34.8|N|140|31|14.6|E}}}} }} [[File:Torii of Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|thumb|right|245px|鳥居]] [[File:Shinmon of Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|thumb|right|245px|神門]] '''若宮八幡宮'''（わかみや はちまんぐう）は、[[茨城県]][[常陸太田市]]にある[[神社]]。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[郷社]]。 == 歴史 == [[応永]]年間（1400年頃）、[[佐竹義人|佐竹義仁]]が[[鶴岡八幡宮]]（[[神奈川県]][[鎌倉市]]）より勧請して[[太田城 (常陸国)|太田城]]内に祀り、守護神としたのが始まりであり、それ以来、[[佐竹氏]]の祈願所となった。なお、研究によれば、佐竹氏は本来は常陸源氏の守護神として[[石清水八幡宮]]から勧請して八幡社を建立していたが、佐竹一族の反対を押し切る形で藤原氏系の[[上杉氏]]からの養子に入った義仁は上杉氏ともつながりが深い鶴岡八幡宮から勧請を行ったとみられている堤禎子「佐竹氏と八幡信仰」『茨城県立歴史館報』28号、2001年。佐々木倫朗 編著『シリーズ・中世関東武士の研究 第三十巻 常陸佐竹氏』（戒光祥出版、2021年）P192-227.所収。 佐竹氏は[[関ヶ原の戦い]]の後に秋田に転封となるが、その際に[[大館城]]の守護神として当神社の神霊を城中に奉持し、のちに城の東の[[大館八幡神社]]に祀り、転封の後でも守護神としていた{{Cite web|和書|url=http://akita-jinjacho.sakura.ne.jp/tatsujin_etc/kennsaku/oodate/67_hachiman_hachiman.html |title=八幡神社 |publisher=秋田県神社庁 |accessdate=2015-10-12}}。 [[慶長]]14年（[[1609年]]）、[[水戸藩]]初代藩主[[徳川頼房]]が7歳の時、病気平癒を祈願して全快したとされる。それ以来、[[水戸徳川家]]の崇敬厚く、[[元禄]]5年（[[1692年]]）二代藩主[[徳川光圀]]（[[水戸黄門]]）が太田郷の鎮守とした。 [[宝永]]5年（[[1708年]]）、並祀された[[太田稲荷神社]]と共に、太田城内から現在の地に社殿を遷した。 「鶴岡より勧請奉斎された際、鶴子なる女性が供奉して来り、祭祀を司った」という由緒に基づき、[[昭和]]15年（[[1940年]]）に氏子の巫女たちによって「鶴子舞」が奉納された。近年、この[[巫女舞]]奉納が復活し、例大祭などで「[[浦安の舞]]」「[[近代に作られた神楽|豊栄の舞]]」「[[八乙女舞|八乙女の舞]]」が厳かな雰囲気のなかに愛らしさをもって奉納されている。 [[File:Kaguraden of Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|thumb|right|245px|神楽殿]] == 祭神 == * '''[[大鷦鷯尊]]'''（おおささぎのみこと、[[仁徳天皇]]） * '''[[倉稲魂命]]'''（うがのみたまのみこと） == 祭事 == * 1月 - [[元旦]]祭 * 2月 - [[節分]]追儺祭 * 5月 - 例大祭 * 6月 - [[夏越の祓|夏越大祓]] * 9月 - 秋季例祭 * 12月 - [[大祓]] == 文化財 == * 大[[ケヤキ]]（茨城県指定[[天然記念物]]） * 絹本着色僧形八幡画像 * 石剣形宝物 * 軍扇（伝[[源義光|新羅三郎義光]]所用） == 境内社 == * 北野天神社（梅天神） - [[康平]]6年（[[1062年]]）に[[源義家]]が、[[菅原道真]]自筆の天満像を掲げて[[前九年の役]]の勝利を祈願したことによる。 * 市比売神社 * 足尾神社 * 愛宕神社 * 別雷神社 * 富士神社 * 淡島神社（[[淡島神]]） File:Ota-tenmangu in Wakamiya-hachimanhu (Hitachiota).jpg|北野天神社 File:Keidaisha1 in Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|市比売神社・別雷神社・愛宕神社 File:Ashio-jinja in Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|足尾神社 File:Fuji-jinja in Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|富士神社 == アクセス == [[東日本旅客鉄道|JR]][[常陸太田駅]]から北へ約1.5km。徒歩約20分。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} == 関連項目 == * [[神社一覧]] * [[日本の神の一覧]] == 外部リンク == * {{Instagram|wakamiyah}} * {{Twitter|wakamiya_h}} {{神道 横}} {{茨城県の神社一覧目次}} {{DEFAULTSORT:わかみやはちまんくう ひたちおおた}} [[Category:茨城県の神社]] [[Category:八幡宮]] [[Category:茨城県の旧郷社]] [[Category:佐竹右京大夫家|社わかみやはちまんくう]] [[Category:常陸太田市の建築物]] [[Category:常陸太田市の歴史]] [[Category:仁徳天皇]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[若宮八幡宮 (常陸太田市)]] to [[Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import

revision.text:
{{Cite sources|date=2019-03-12}} {{Shrine |name=Wakamiya Hachimangū (若宮八幡宮) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Wakamiyahachimaｎgu.JPG|250px]]
Main hall (September 2005) |location={{ill|Hitachiōta|ja|常陸太田市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Miyamoto-chō 2344 |coordinates={{coord2|36|32|34.8|N|140|31|14.6|E|type:landmark_region:JP-08|display=inline,title|name=Wakamiya Hachimangū}} |enshrined_deities=[[Ōsasagi no Mikoto]] ({{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}})
[[Ukanomitama no Mikoto]] |established={{ill|Ōei era|ja|応永|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (circa 1400) |shrine_Rank=Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |annual_festival=See [[#Festivals]] |main_hall= |map={{location map|Japan Ibaraki|label=Wakamiya Hachimangū|coordinates= {{coord2|36|32|34.8|N|140|31|14.6|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|32|34.8|N|140|31|14.6|E}}}} }} [[File:Torii of Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|thumb|right|245px|Torii]] [[File:Shinmon of Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|thumb|right|245px|Shinmon (gate)]] '''Wakamiya Hachimangū''' (若宮八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Hitachiōta|ja|常陸太田市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Japan. The shrine's Rank under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == History == The shrine was established during the {{ill|Ōei era|ja|応永|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (circa 1400) by {{ill|Yoshihito Satake|ja|佐竹義人|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, who invited its deity from {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|ja|鶴岡八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Kamakura|ja|鎌倉市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It was originally enshrined within {{ill|Ōta Castle (Hitachi Province)|ja|太田城 (常陸国)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as a guardian deity and served as the prayer site for the {{ill|Satake clan|ja|佐竹氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Research suggests that the Satake clan initially revered a {{ill|Hachiman shrine|ja|八幡社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} derived from {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, which was the guardian shrine of the Minamoto clan in Hitachi. However, Yoshihito Satake, who had ties with the {{ill|Uesugi clan|ja|上杉氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, instead adopted the enshrinement of Tsurugaoka Hachimangū, possibly against opposition from the Satake family.Teiko Tsutsumi, "Satake Clan and Hachiman Worship," {{ill|Ibaraki Prefectural History Museum Bulletin|ja|茨城県立歴史館報|qq=draft|qq=draft}} No. 28, 2001. Compiled in {{ill|Research Series on Medieval Kanto Warriors Vol. 30: Hitachi Satake Clan|ja|シリーズ・中世関東武士の研究 第三十巻 常陸佐竹氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Kōshōshō Publishing, 2021), pp. 192–227. Although the Satake clan was later relocated to {{ill|Akita|ja|秋田|qq=draft|qq=draft}} after the {{ill|Battle of Sekigahara|ja|関ヶ原の戦い|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, they took the shrine's deity with them to {{ill|Ōdate Castle|ja|大館城|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as its guardian spirit. The deity was subsequently enshrined at {{ill|Ōdate Hachiman Shrine|ja|大館八幡神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, continuing its role as a protector.{{Cite web|title=八幡神社 |url=http://akita-jinjacho.sakura.ne.jp/tatsujin_etc/kennsaku/oodate/67_hachiman_hachiman.html |publisher=Akita Prefecture Shrine Association |accessdate=2015-10-12}} In {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 14 (1609), the first {{ill|daimyō|ja|大名|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of the {{ill|Mito Domain|ja|水戸藩|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Tokugawa Yorifusa|ja|徳川頼房|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, was healed of an illness after praying at the shrine. Thereafter, the {{ill|Mito Tokugawa clan|ja|水戸徳川家|qq=draft|qq=draft}} held the shrine in high reverence. In {{ill|Genroku|ja|元禄|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 5 (1692), the second daimyō, {{ill|Tokugawa Mitsukuni|ja|徳川光圀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (popularly known as Mito Kōmon), designated the shrine as a guardian of the Ōta district. In {{ill|Hōei|ja|宝永|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 5 (1708), the shrine was relocated to its current location alongside the {{ill|Ōta Inari Shrine|ja|太田稲荷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, which had been worshiped together with it. In 1940 (Showa 15), based on the tradition that a woman named Tsuruko accompanied the deity’s transfer from Tsurugaoka Hachimangū and oversaw its rituals, the shrine's parishioner priestesses revived the "Tsuruko Dance." Recently, this {{ill|kagura|ja|神楽|qq=draft|qq=draft}} offering has been expanded to include performances such as {{ill|Urayasu no Mai|ja|浦安の舞|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Toyo no Sakai no Mai|ja|豊栄の舞|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Yaotome no Mai|ja|八乙女舞|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, which are performed with grace during major festivals. [[File:Kaguraden of Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|thumb|right|245px|Kaguraden]] == Enshrined Deities == * '''[[Ōsasagi no Mikoto]]''' (Emperor Nintoku) * '''[[Ukanomitama no Mikoto]]''' == Festivals == * January: {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|元旦祭|Saitan-sai=Saitan-sai|lt=Saitan-sai (New Year's Festival)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * February: {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分追儺祭|lt=Setsubun Tsuina-sai|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * May: festival * June: {{ill|Nagoshi no Harae|ja|夏越の祓|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * September: Autumn Festival * December: {{ill|Ōharae|ja|大祓|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == Cultural Properties == * Large {{ill|zelkova|ja|ケヤキ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} tree (designated a natural monument of Ibaraki Prefecture) * Hanging scroll of Hachiman as a monk * Stone sword artifacts * Military fan (attributed to {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|ja|源義光|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) == Auxiliary Shrines == * {{ill|Kitano Tenjin Shrine|ja|北野天神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Plum Tenjin) – Established in {{ill|Kōhei 6|ja|康平|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (1062) by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|ja|源義家|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to pray for victory in the {{ill|Earlier Nine Years' War|ja|前九年の役|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * {{ill|Ichihime Shrine|ja|市比売神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Ashio Shrine|ja|足尾神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Atago Shrine|ja|愛宕神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Wakazumi Shrine|ja|別雷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Fuji Shrine|ja|富士神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Awashima Shrine|ja|淡島神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} File:Ota-tenmangu in Wakamiya-hachimanhu (Hitachiota).jpg|Kitano Tenjin Shrine File:Keidaisha1 in Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|Ichihime Shrine, Wakazumi Shrine, Atago Shrine File:Ashio-jinja in Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|Ashio Shrine File:Fuji-jinja in Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|Fuji Shrine == Access == The shrine is approximately 1.5 km north of {{ill|Hitachi-Ōta Station|ja|常陸太田駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|ja|東日本旅客鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (JR). It is a 20-minute walk. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Shinto shrines|ja|神社一覧|1=List of Shinto shrines in Japan|2=ja|3=神社一覧|4=zh|5=日本神社列表|6=uk|7=Список синтоїстських святилищ|8=es|9=Anexo:Santuarios sintoístas|10=fr|11=Liste de sanctuaires shinto japonais|12=it|13=Lista di santuari shintoisti|14=simple|15=List of Shinto shrines|16=mr|17=जपानमधील शिंतो तीर्थस्थानांची यादी|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|List of Japanese deities|ja|日本の神の一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == * {{Instagram|wakamiyah}} * {{Twitter|wakamiya_h}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Ibaraki Prefecture Shrine Index}} {{translated page|ja|若宮八幡宮 (常陸太田市)|version=101968922|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:Satake clan]] [[Category:Architecture in Hitachiōta]] [[Category:History of Hitachiōta]] [[Category:Emperor Nintoku]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:orphaned pages as of April 8]] [[Category:Orphans as of April 16]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[zh:若宮八幡宮]] [[ja:%E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E5%AE%AE_(%E5%B8%B8%E9%99%B8%E5%A4%AA%E7%94%B0%E5%B8%82)]] [[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]] [[en:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]]

{{Cite sources|date=2019-03-12}} {{Shrine |name=Wakamiya Hachimangū (若宮八幡宮) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Wakamiyahachimaｎgu.JPG|250px]]
Main hall (September 2005) |location={{ill|Hitachiōta|ja|常陸太田市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Miyamoto-chō 2344 |coordinates={{coord2|36|32|34.8|N|140|31|14.6|E|type:landmark_region:JP-08|display=inline,title|name=Wakamiya Hachimangū}} |enshrined_deities=[[Ōsasagi no Mikoto]] ({{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}})
[[Ukanomitama no Mikoto]] |established={{ill|Ōei era|ja|応永|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (circa 1400) |shrine_Rank=Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |annual_festival=See [[#Festivals]] |main_hall= |map={{location map|Japan Ibaraki|label=Wakamiya Hachimangū|coordinates= {{coord2|36|32|34.8|N|140|31|14.6|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|32|34.8|N|140|31|14.6|E}}}} }} [[File:Torii of Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|thumb|right|245px|Torii]] [[File:Shinmon of Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|thumb|right|245px|Shinmon (gate)]] '''Wakamiya Hachimangū''' (若宮八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Hitachiōta|ja|常陸太田市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Japan. The shrine's Rank under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == History == The shrine was established during the {{ill|Ōei era|ja|応永|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (circa 1400) by {{ill|Yoshihito Satake|ja|佐竹義人|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, who invited its deity from {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|ja|鶴岡八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Kamakura|ja|鎌倉市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It was originally enshrined within {{ill|Ōta Castle (Hitachi Province)|ja|太田城 (常陸国)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as a guardian deity and served as the prayer site for the {{ill|Satake clan|ja|佐竹氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Research suggests that the Satake clan initially revered a {{ill|Hachiman shrine|ja|八幡社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} derived from {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, which was the guardian shrine of the Minamoto clan in Hitachi. However, Yoshihito Satake, who had ties with the {{ill|Uesugi clan|ja|上杉氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, instead adopted the enshrinement of Tsurugaoka Hachimangū, possibly against opposition from the Satake family.Teiko Tsutsumi, "Satake Clan and Hachiman Worship," {{ill|Ibaraki Prefectural History Museum Bulletin|ja|茨城県立歴史館報|qq=draft|qq=draft}} No. 28, 2001. Compiled in {{ill|Research Series on Medieval Kanto Warriors Vol. 30: Hitachi Satake Clan|ja|シリーズ・中世関東武士の研究 第三十巻 常陸佐竹氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Kōshōshō Publishing, 2021), pp. 192–227. Although the Satake clan was later relocated to {{ill|Akita|ja|秋田|qq=draft|qq=draft}} after the {{ill|Battle of Sekigahara|ja|関ヶ原の戦い|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, they took the shrine's deity with them to {{ill|Ōdate Castle|ja|大館城|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as its guardian spirit. The deity was subsequently enshrined at {{ill|Ōdate Hachiman Shrine|ja|大館八幡神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, continuing its role as a protector.{{Cite web|title=八幡神社 |url=http://akita-jinjacho.sakura.ne.jp/tatsujin_etc/kennsaku/oodate/67_hachiman_hachiman.html |publisher=Akita Prefecture Shrine Association |accessdate=2015-10-12}} In {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 14 (1609), the first {{ill|daimyō|ja|大名|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of the {{ill|Mito Domain|ja|水戸藩|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Tokugawa Yorifusa|ja|徳川頼房|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, was healed of an illness after praying at the shrine. Thereafter, the {{ill|Mito Tokugawa clan|ja|水戸徳川家|qq=draft|qq=draft}} held the shrine in high reverence. In {{ill|Genroku|ja|元禄|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 5 (1692), the second daimyō, {{ill|Tokugawa Mitsukuni|ja|徳川光圀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (popularly known as Mito Kōmon), designated the shrine as a guardian of the Ōta district. In {{ill|Hōei|ja|宝永|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 5 (1708), the shrine was relocated to its current location alongside the {{ill|Ōta Inari Shrine|ja|太田稲荷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, which had been worshiped together with it. In 1940 (Showa 15), based on the tradition that a woman named Tsuruko accompanied the deity’s transfer from Tsurugaoka Hachimangū and oversaw its rituals, the shrine's parishioner priestesses revived the "Tsuruko Dance." Recently, this {{ill|kagura|ja|神楽|qq=draft|qq=draft}} offering has been expanded to include performances such as {{ill|Urayasu no Mai|ja|浦安の舞|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Toyo no Sakai no Mai|ja|豊栄の舞|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Yaotome no Mai|ja|八乙女舞|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, which are performed with grace during major festivals. It is a 20-minute walk. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Shinto shrines|ja|神社一覧|1=List of Shinto shrines in Japan|2=ja|3=神社一覧|4=zh|5=日本神社列表|6=uk|7=Список синтоїстських святилищ|8=es|9=Anexo:Santuarios sintoístas|10=fr|11=Liste de sanctuaires shinto japonais|12=it|13=Lista di santuari shintoisti|14=simple|15=List of Shinto shrines|16=mr|17=जपानमधील शिंतो तीर्थस्थानांची यादी|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|List of Japanese deities|ja|日本の神の一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == * {{Instagram|wakamiyah}} * {{Twitter|wakamiya_h}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Ibaraki Prefecture Shrine Index}} {{translated page|ja|若宮八幡宮 (常陸太田市)|version=101968922|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:Satake clan]] [[Category:Architecture in Hitachiōta]] [[Category:History of Hitachiōta]] [[Category:Emperor Nintoku]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:orphaned pages as of April 8]] [[Category:Orphans as of April 16]] }} [[zh:若宮八幡宮]] [[ja:%E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E5%AE%AE_(%E5%B8%B8%E9%99%B8%E5%A4%AA%E7%94%B0%E5%B8%82)]] [[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]] [[en:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]]

{{Unreferenced|date=2019-03-12}} {{Shrine |name=Wakamiya Hachimangū (若宮八幡宮) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Wakamiyahachimaｎgu.JPG|250px]]
Main hall (September 2005) |location=[[:en:Hitachiōta, Ibaraki|Hitachiōta]], [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]] Miyamoto-chō 2344 |coordinates={{coord2|36|32|34.8|N|140|31|14.6|E|type:landmark_region:JP-08|display=inline,title|name=Wakamiya Hachimangū}} |enshrined_deities=[[Ōsasagi no Mikoto]] ([[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]])
[[Ukanomitama no Mikoto]] |established=[[:en:Ōei|Ōei era]] (circa 1400) |shrine_Rank=Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} |annual_festival=See [[#Festivals]] |main_hall= |map={{location map|Japan Ibaraki|label=Wakamiya Hachimangū|coordinates= {{coord2|36|32|34.8|N|140|31|14.6|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|32|34.8|N|140|31|14.6|E}}}} }} [[File:Torii of Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|thumb|right|245px|Torii]] [[File:Shinmon of Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|thumb|right|245px|Shinmon (gate)]] '''Wakamiya Hachimangū''' (若宮八幡宮) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Hitachiōta, Ibaraki|Hitachiōta]], [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]], Japan. The shrine's Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}}. == History == The shrine was established during the [[:en:Ōei|Ōei era]] (circa 1400) by {{ill|Yoshihito Satake|ja|佐竹義人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshihito Satake|qq=}}, who invited its deity from [[:en:Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū]] in [[:en:Kamakura|Kamakura]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It was originally enshrined within {{ill|Ōta Castle (Hitachi Province)|ja|太田城 (常陸国)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta Castle (Hitachi Province)|qq=}} as a guardian deity and served as the prayer site for the [[:en:Satake clan|Satake clan]]. Research suggests that the Satake clan initially revered a {{ill|Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman shrine|qq=}} derived from [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Iwashimizu Hachimangū]], which was the guardian shrine of the Minamoto clan in Hitachi. However, Yoshihito Satake, who had ties with the [[:en:Uesugi clan|Uesugi clan]], instead adopted the enshrinement of Tsurugaoka Hachimangū, possibly against opposition from the Satake family.Teiko Tsutsumi, "Satake Clan and Hachiman Worship," {{ill|Ibaraki Prefectural History Museum Bulletin|ja|茨城県立歴史館報|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibaraki Prefectural History Museum Bulletin|qq=}} No. 28, 2001. Compiled in {{ill|Research Series on Medieval Kanto Warriors Vol. 30: Hitachi Satake Clan|ja|シリーズ・中世関東武士の研究 第三十巻 常陸佐竹氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Research Series on Medieval Kanto Warriors Vol. 30: Hitachi Satake Clan|qq=}} (Kōshōshō Publishing, 2021), pp. 192–227. Although the Satake clan was later relocated to {{ill|Akita|ja|秋田|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akita|qq=}} after the [[:en:Battle of Sekigahara|Battle of Sekigahara]], they took the shrine's deity with them to {{ill|Ōdate Castle|ja|大館城|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōdate Castle|qq=}} as its guardian spirit. The deity was subsequently enshrined at {{ill|Ōdate Hachiman Shrine|ja|大館八幡神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōdate Hachiman Shrine|qq=}}, continuing its role as a protector.{{Cite web|title=八幡神社 |url=http://akita-jinjacho.sakura.ne.jp/tatsujin_etc/kennsaku/oodate/67_hachiman_hachiman.html |publisher=Akita Prefecture Shrine Association |accessdate=2015-10-12}} In [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 14 (1609), the first [[:en:Daimyo|daimyō]] of the [[:en:Mito Domain|Mito Domain]], [[:en:Tokugawa Yorifusa|Tokugawa Yorifusa]], was healed of an illness after praying at the shrine. Thereafter, the [[:en:Mito Tokugawa family|Mito Tokugawa clan]] held the shrine in high reverence. In [[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] 5 (1692), the second daimyō, [[:en:Tokugawa Mitsukuni|Tokugawa Mitsukuni]] (popularly known as Mito Kōmon), designated the shrine as a guardian of the Ōta district. In [[:en:Hōei|Hōei]] 5 (1708), the shrine was relocated to its current location alongside the {{ill|Ōta Inari Shrine|ja|太田稲荷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta Inari Shrine|qq=}}, which had been worshiped together with it. In 1940 (Showa 15), based on the tradition that a woman named Tsuruko accompanied the deity’s transfer from Tsurugaoka Hachimangū and oversaw its rituals, the shrine's parishioner priestesses revived the "Tsuruko Dance." Recently, this [[:en:Kagura|kagura]] offering has been expanded to include performances such as {{ill|Urayasu no Mai|ja|浦安の舞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urayasu no Mai|qq=}}, {{ill|Toyo no Sakai no Mai|ja|豊栄の舞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyo no Sakai no Mai|qq=}}, and {{ill|Yaotome no Mai|ja|八乙女舞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yaotome no Mai|qq=}}, which are performed with grace during major festivals. [[File:Kaguraden of Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|thumb|right|245px|Kaguraden]] == Enshrined Deities == * '''[[Ōsasagi no Mikoto]]''' (Emperor Nintoku) * '''[[Ukanomitama no Mikoto]]''' == Festivals == * January: {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|元旦祭|Saitan-sai=Saitan-sai|lt=Saitan-sai (New Year's Festival)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}} * February: {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分追儺祭|lt=Setsubun Tsuina-sai|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setsubun|qq=}} * May: festival * June: {{ill|Nagoshi no Harae|ja|夏越の祓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoshi no Harae|qq=}} * September: Autumn Festival * December: [[:en:Ōharae-shiki|Ōharae]] == Cultural Properties == * Large [[:en:Zelkova serrata|zelkova]] tree (designated a natural monument of Ibaraki Prefecture) * Hanging scroll of Hachiman as a monk * Stone sword artifacts * Military fan (attributed to [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu]]) == Auxiliary Shrines == * {{ill|Kitano Tenjin Shrine|ja|北野天神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitano Tenjin Shrine|qq=}} (Plum Tenjin) – Established in [[:en:Kōhei|Kōhei 6]] (1062) by [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshiie|Minamoto no Yoshiie]] to pray for victory in the [[:en:Former Nine Years' War|Earlier Nine Years' War]]. * {{ill|Ichihime Shrine|ja|市比売神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichihime Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Ashio Shrine|ja|足尾神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashio Shrine|qq=}} * [[:en:Atago Shrine (Kyoto)|Atago Shrine]] * {{ill|Wakazumi Shrine|ja|別雷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakazumi Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Fuji Shrine|ja|富士神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuji Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Awashima Shrine (Wakayama)|ja|淡島神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awashima Shrine|qq=}} File:Ota-tenmangu in Wakamiya-hachimanhu (Hitachiota).jpg|Kitano Tenjin Shrine File:Keidaisha1 in Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|Ichihime Shrine, Wakazumi Shrine, Atago Shrine File:Ashio-jinja in Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|Ashio Shrine File:Fuji-jinja in Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|Fuji Shrine == Access == The shrine is approximately 1.5 km north of [[:en:Hitachi-Ōta Station|Hitachi-Ōta Station]] on the [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] (JR). It is a 20-minute walk. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:List of Shinto shrines in Japan|List of Shinto shrines in Japan]] * [[:en:List of Japanese deities|List of Japanese deities]] == External Links == * {{Instagram|wakamiyah}} * {{Twitter|wakamiya_h}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Ibaraki Prefecture Shrine Index}} {{translated page|ja|若宮八幡宮 (常陸太田市)|version=101968922|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:Satake clan]] [[Category:Architecture in Hitachiōta]] [[Category:History of Hitachiōta]] [[Category:Emperor Nintoku]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:orphaned pages as of April 8]] [[Category:Orphans as of April 16]] }} [[zh:若宮八幡宮]] [[ja:%E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E5%AE%AE_(%E5%B8%B8%E9%99%B8%E5%A4%AA%E7%94%B0%E5%B8%82)]] [[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]] [[en:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]]

in Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|Ashio Shrine File:Fuji-jinja in Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|Fuji Shrine == Access == The shrine is approximately 1.5 km north of [[:en:Hitachi-Ōta Station|Hitachi-Ōta Station]] on the [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] (JR). It is a 20-minute walk. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:List of Shinto shrines in Japan|List of Shinto shrines in Japan]] * [[:en:List of Japanese deities|List of Japanese deities]] == External Links == * {{Instagram|wakamiyah}} * {{Twitter|wakamiya_h}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Ibaraki Prefecture Shrine Index}} {{translated page|ja|若宮八幡宮 (常陸太田市)|version=101968922|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:Satake clan]] [[Category:Architecture in Hitachiōta]] [[Category:History of Hitachiōta]] [[Category:Emperor Nintoku]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:orphaned pages as of April 8]] [[Category:Orphans as of April 16]] }} [[zh:若宮八幡宮]] [[ja:%E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E5%AE%AE_(%E5%B8%B8%E9%99%B8%E5%A4%AA%E7%94%B0%E5%B8%82)]] [[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]] [[en:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]] [[Category:仁徳天皇]]

revision.text:
{{Unreferenced|date=2019-03-12}} {{Shrine |name=Wakamiya Hachimangū (若宮八幡宮) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Wakamiyahachimaｎgu.JPG|250px]]
Main hall (September 2005) |location=[[:en:Hitachiōta, Ibaraki|Hitachiōta]], [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]] Miyamoto-chō 2344 |coordinates={{coord2|36|32|34.8|N|140|31|14.6|E|type:landmark_region:JP-08|display=inline,title|name=Wakamiya Hachimangū}} |enshrined_deities=[[Ōsasagi no Mikoto]] ([[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]])
[[Ukanomitama no Mikoto]] |established=[[:en:Ōei|Ōei era]] (circa 1400) |shrine_Rank=Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} |annual_festival=See [[#Festivals]] |main_hall= |map={{location map|Japan Ibaraki|label=Wakamiya Hachimangū|coordinates= {{coord2|36|32|34.8|N|140|31|14.6|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|32|34.8|N|140|31|14.6|E}}}} }} [[File:Torii of Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|thumb|right|245px|Torii]] [[File:Shinmon of Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|thumb|right|245px|Shinmon (gate)]] '''Wakamiya Hachimangū''' (若宮八幡宮) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Hitachiōta, Ibaraki|Hitachiōta]], [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]], Japan. The shrine's Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}}. == History == The shrine was established during the [[:en:Ōei|Ōei era]] (circa 1400) by {{ill|Yoshihito Satake|ja|佐竹義人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshihito Satake|qq=}}, who invited its deity from [[:en:Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū]] in [[:en:Kamakura|Kamakura]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It was originally enshrined within {{ill|Ōta Castle (Hitachi Province)|ja|太田城 (常陸国)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta Castle (Hitachi Province)|qq=}} as a guardian deity and served as the prayer site for the [[:en:Satake clan|Satake clan]]. Research suggests that the Satake clan initially revered a {{ill|Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman shrine|qq=}} derived from [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Iwashimizu Hachimangū]], which was the guardian shrine of the Minamoto clan in Hitachi. However, Yoshihito Satake, who had ties with the [[:en:Uesugi clan|Uesugi clan]], instead adopted the enshrinement of Tsurugaoka Hachimangū, possibly against opposition from the Satake family.Teiko Tsutsumi, "Satake Clan and Hachiman Worship," {{ill|Ibaraki Prefectural History Museum Bulletin|ja|茨城県立歴史館報|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibaraki Prefectural History Museum Bulletin|qq=}} No. 28, 2001. Compiled in {{ill|Research Series on Medieval Kanto Warriors Vol. 30: Hitachi Satake Clan|ja|シリーズ・中世関東武士の研究 第三十巻 常陸佐竹氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Research Series on Medieval Kanto Warriors Vol. 30: Hitachi Satake Clan|qq=}} (Kōshōshō Publishing, 2021), pp. 192–227. Although the Satake clan was later relocated to {{ill|Akita|ja|秋田|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akita|qq=}} after the [[:en:Battle of Sekigahara|Battle of Sekigahara]], they took the shrine's deity with them to {{ill|Ōdate Castle|ja|大館城|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōdate Castle|qq=}} as its guardian spirit. The deity was subsequently enshrined at {{ill|Ōdate Hachiman Shrine|ja|大館八幡神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōdate Hachiman Shrine|qq=}}, continuing its role as a protector.{{Cite web|title=八幡神社 |url=http://akita-jinjacho.sakura.ne.jp/tatsujin_etc/kennsaku/oodate/67_hachiman_hachiman.html |publisher=Akita Prefecture Shrine Association |accessdate=2015-10-12}} In [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 14 (1609), the first [[:en:Daimyo|daimyō]] of the [[:en:Mito Domain|Mito Domain]], [[:en:Tokugawa Yorifusa|Tokugawa Yorifusa]], was healed of an illness after praying at the shrine. Thereafter, the [[:en:Mito Tokugawa family|Mito Tokugawa clan]] held the shrine in high reverence. In [[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] 5 (1692), the second daimyō, [[:en:Tokugawa Mitsukuni|Tokugawa Mitsukuni]] (popularly known as Mito Kōmon), designated the shrine as a guardian of the Ōta district. In [[:en:Hōei|Hōei]] 5 (1708), the shrine was relocated to its current location alongside the {{ill|Ōta Inari Shrine|ja|太田稲荷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta Inari Shrine|qq=}}, which had been worshiped together with it. In 1940 (Showa 15), based on the tradition that a woman named Tsuruko accompanied the deity’s transfer from Tsurugaoka Hachimangū and oversaw its rituals, the shrine's parishioner priestesses revived the "Tsuruko Dance." Recently, this [[:en:Kagura|kagura]] offering has been expanded to include performances such as {{ill|Urayasu no Mai|ja|浦安の舞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urayasu no Mai|qq=}}, {{ill|Toyo no Sakai no Mai|ja|豊栄の舞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyo no Sakai no Mai|qq=}}, and {{ill|Yaotome no Mai|ja|八乙女舞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yaotome no Mai|qq=}}, which are performed with grace during major festivals. [[File:Kaguraden of Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|thumb|right|245px|Kaguraden]] == Enshrined Deities == * '''[[Ōsasagi no Mikoto]]''' (Emperor Nintoku) * '''[[Ukanomitama no Mikoto]]''' == Festivals == * January: {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|元旦祭|Saitan-sai=Saitan-sai|lt=Saitan-sai (New Year's Festival)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}} * February: {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分追儺祭|lt=Setsubun Tsuina-sai|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setsubun|qq=}} * May: festival * June: {{ill|Nagoshi no Harae|ja|夏越の祓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoshi no Harae|qq=}} * September: Autumn Festival * December: [[:en:Ōharae-shiki|Ōharae]] == Cultural Properties == * Large [[:en:Zelkova serrata|zelkova]] tree (designated a natural monument of Ibaraki Prefecture) * Hanging scroll of Hachiman as a monk * Stone sword artifacts * Military fan (attributed to [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu]]) == Auxiliary Shrines == * {{ill|Kitano Tenjin Shrine|ja|北野天神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitano Tenjin Shrine|qq=}} (Plum Tenjin) – Established in [[:en:Kōhei|Kōhei 6]] (1062) by [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshiie|Minamoto no Yoshiie]] to pray for victory in the [[:en:Former Nine Years' War|Earlier Nine Years' War]]. * {{ill|Ichihime Shrine|ja|市比売神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichihime Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Ashio Shrine|ja|足尾神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashio Shrine|qq=}} * [[:en:Atago Shrine (Kyoto)|Atago Shrine]] * {{ill|Wakazumi Shrine|ja|別雷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakazumi Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Fuji Shrine|ja|富士神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuji Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Awashima Shrine (Wakayama)|ja|淡島神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awashima Shrine|qq=}} File:Ota-tenmangu in Wakamiya-hachimanhu (Hitachiota).jpg|Kitano Tenjin Shrine File:Keidaisha1 in Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|Ichihime Shrine, Wakazumi Shrine, Atago Shrine File:Ashio-jinja in Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|Ashio Shrine File:Fuji-jinja in Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|Fuji Shrine == Access == The shrine is approximately 1.5 km north of [[:en:Hitachi-Ōta Station|Hitachi-Ōta Station]] on the [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] (JR). It is a 20-minute walk. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:List of Shinto shrines in Japan|List of Shinto shrines in Japan]] * [[:en:List of Japanese deities|List of Japanese deities]] == External Links == * {{Instagram|wakamiyah}} * {{Twitter|wakamiya_h}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Ibaraki Prefecture Shrine Index}} {{translated page|ja|若宮八幡宮 (常陸太田市)|version=101968922|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:Satake clan]] [[Category:Architecture in Hitachiōta]] [[Category:History of Hitachiōta]] [[Category:Emperor Nintoku]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:orphaned pages as of April 8]] [[Category:Orphans as of April 16]] }} [[zh:若宮八幡宮]] [[ja:%E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E5%AE%AE_(%E5%B8%B8%E9%99%B8%E5%A4%AA%E7%94%B0%E5%B8%82)]] [[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]] [[en:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]] [[Category:仁徳天皇]] [[Category:佐竹右京大夫家]]

{{Unreferenced|date=2019-03-12}} {{Shrine |name=Wakamiya Hachimangū (若宮八幡宮) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Wakamiyahachimaｎgu.JPG|250px]]
Main hall (September 2005) |location=[[:en:Hitachiōta, Ibaraki|Hitachiōta]], [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]] Miyamoto-chō 2344 |coordinates={{coord2|36|32|34.8|N|140|31|14.6|E|type:landmark_region:JP-08|display=inline,title|name=Wakamiya Hachimangū}} |enshrined_deities=[[Ōsasagi no Mikoto]] ([[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]])
[[Ukanomitama no Mikoto]] |established=[[:en:Ōei|Ōei era]] (circa 1400) |shrine_Rank=Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} |annual_festival=See [[#Festivals]] |main_hall= |map={{location map|Japan Ibaraki|label=Wakamiya Hachimangū|coordinates= {{coord2|36|32|34.8|N|140|31|14.6|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|32|34.8|N|140|31|14.6|E}}}} }} [[File:Torii of Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|thumb|right|245px|Torii]] [[File:Shinmon of Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|thumb|right|245px|Shinmon (gate)]] '''Wakamiya Hachimangū''' (若宮八幡宮) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Hitachiōta, Ibaraki|Hitachiōta]], [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]], Japan. The shrine's Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}}. == History == The shrine was established during the [[:en:Ōei|Ōei era]] (circa 1400) by {{ill|Yoshihito Satake|ja|佐竹義人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshihito Satake|qq=}}, who invited its deity from [[:en:Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū]] in [[:en:Kamakura|Kamakura]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It was originally enshrined within {{ill|Ōta Castle (Hitachi Province)|ja|太田城 (常陸国)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta Castle (Hitachi Province)|qq=}} as a guardian deity and served as the prayer site for the [[:en:Satake clan|Satake clan]]. Research suggests that the Satake clan initially revered a {{ill|Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman shrine|qq=}} derived from [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Iwashimizu Hachimangū]], which was the guardian shrine of the Minamoto clan in Hitachi. However, Yoshihito Satake, who had ties with the [[:en:Uesugi clan|Uesugi clan]], instead adopted the enshrinement of Tsurugaoka Hachimangū, possibly against opposition from the Satake family.Teiko Tsutsumi, "Satake Clan and Hachiman Worship," {{ill|Ibaraki Prefectural History Museum Bulletin|ja|茨城県立歴史館報|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibaraki Prefectural History Museum Bulletin|qq=}} No. 28, 2001. Compiled in {{ill|Research Series on Medieval Kanto Warriors Vol. 30: Hitachi Satake Clan|ja|シリーズ・中世関東武士の研究 第三十巻 常陸佐竹氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Research Series on Medieval Kanto Warriors Vol. 30: Hitachi Satake Clan|qq=}} (Kōshōshō Publishing, 2021), pp. 192–227. Although the Satake clan was later relocated to {{ill|Akita|ja|秋田|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akita|qq=}} after the [[:en:Battle of Sekigahara|Battle of Sekigahara]], they took the shrine's deity with them to {{ill|Ōdate Castle|ja|大館城|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōdate Castle|qq=}} as its guardian spirit. The deity was subsequently enshrined at {{ill|Ōdate Hachiman Shrine|ja|大館八幡神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōdate Hachiman Shrine|qq=}}, continuing its role as a protector.{{Cite web|title=八幡神社 |url=http://akita-jinjacho.sakura.ne.jp/tatsujin_etc/kennsaku/oodate/67_hachiman_hachiman.html |publisher=Akita Prefecture Shrine Association |accessdate=2015-10-12}} In [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 14 (1609), the first [[:en:Daimyo|daimyō]] of the [[:en:Mito Domain|Mito Domain]], [[:en:Tokugawa Yorifusa|Tokugawa Yorifusa]], was healed of an illness after praying at the shrine. Thereafter, the [[:en:Mito Tokugawa family|Mito Tokugawa clan]] held the shrine in high reverence. In [[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] 5 (1692), the second daimyō, [[:en:Tokugawa Mitsukuni|Tokugawa Mitsukuni]] (popularly known as Mito Kōmon), designated the shrine as a guardian of the Ōta district. In [[:en:Hōei|Hōei]] 5 (1708), the shrine was relocated to its current location alongside the {{ill|Ōta Inari Shrine|ja|太田稲荷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta Inari Shrine|qq=}}, which had been worshiped together with it. In 1940 (Showa 15), based on the tradition that a woman named Tsuruko accompanied the deity’s transfer from Tsurugaoka Hachimangū and oversaw its rituals, the shrine's parishioner priestesses revived the "Tsuruko Dance." Recently, this [[:en:Kagura|kagura]] offering has been expanded to include performances such as {{ill|Urayasu no Mai|ja|浦安の舞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urayasu no Mai|qq=}}, {{ill|Toyo no Sakai no Mai|ja|豊栄の舞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyo no Sakai no Mai|qq=}}, and {{ill|Yaotome no Mai|ja|八乙女舞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yaotome no Mai|qq=}}, which are performed with grace during major festivals. [[File:Kaguraden of Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|thumb|right|245px|Kaguraden]] == Enshrined Deities == * '''[[Ōsasagi no Mikoto]]''' (Emperor Nintoku) * '''[[Ukanomitama no Mikoto]]''' == Festivals == * January: {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|元旦祭|Saitan-sai=Saitan-sai|lt=Saitan-sai (New Year's Festival)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}} * February: {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分追儺祭|lt=Setsubun Tsuina-sai|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setsubun|qq=}} * May: festival * June: {{ill|Nagoshi no Harae|ja|夏越の祓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoshi no Harae|qq=}} * September: Autumn Festival * December: [[:en:Ōharae-shiki|Ōharae]] == Cultural Properties == * Large [[:en:Zelkova serrata|zelkova]] tree (designated a natural monument of Ibaraki Prefecture) * Hanging scroll of Hachiman as a monk * Stone sword artifacts * Military fan (attributed to [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu]]) == Auxiliary Shrines == * {{ill|Kitano Tenjin Shrine|ja|北野天神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitano Tenjin Shrine|qq=}} (Plum Tenjin) – Established in [[:en:Kōhei|Kōhei 6]] (1062) by [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshiie|Minamoto no Yoshiie]] to pray for victory in the [[:en:Former Nine Years' War|Earlier Nine Years' War]]. * {{ill|Ichihime Shrine|ja|市比売神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichihime Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Ashio Shrine|ja|足尾神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashio Shrine|qq=}} * [[:en:Atago Shrine (Kyoto)|Atago Shrine]] * {{ill|Wakazumi Shrine|ja|別雷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakazumi Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Fuji Shrine|ja|富士神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuji Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Awashima Shrine (Wakayama)|ja|淡島神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awashima Shrine|qq=}} File:Ota-tenmangu in Wakamiya-hachimanhu (Hitachiota).jpg|Kitano Tenjin Shrine File:Keidaisha1 in Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|Ichihime Shrine, Wakazumi Shrine, Atago Shrine File:Ashio-jinja in Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|Ashio Shrine File:Fuji-jinja in Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|Fuji Shrine == Access == The shrine is approximately 1.5 km north of [[:en:Hitachi-Ōta Station|Hitachi-Ōta Station]] on the [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] (JR). It is a 20-minute walk. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:List of Shinto shrines in Japan|List of Shinto shrines in Japan]] * [[:en:List of Japanese deities|List of Japanese deities]] == External Links == * {{Instagram|wakamiyah}} * {{Twitter|wakamiya_h}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Ibaraki Prefecture Shrine Index}} {{translated page|ja|若宮八幡宮 (常陸太田市)|version=101968922|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:Satake clan]] [[Category:Architecture in Hitachiōta]] [[Category:History of Hitachiōta]] [[Category:Emperor Nintoku]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:orphaned pages as of April 8]] [[Category:Orphans as of April 16]] }} [[zh:若宮八幡宮]] [[ja:%E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E5%AE%AE_(%E5%B8%B8%E9%99%B8%E5%A4%AA%E7%94%B0%E5%B8%82)]] [[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]] [[en:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]] [[Category:仁徳天皇]] [[Category:佐竹右京大夫家]] [[Category:常陸太田市の建築物]] [[Category:常陸太田市の歴史]]

It was originally enshrined within {{ill|Ōta Castle (Hitachi Province)|ja|太田城 (常陸国)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta Castle (Hitachi Province)|qq=}} as a guardian deity and served as the prayer site for the [[:en:Satake clan|Satake clan]].
