However, Yoshihito Satake, who had ties with the [[:en:Uesugi clan|Uesugi clan]], instead adopted the enshrinement of Tsurugaoka Hachimangū, possibly against opposition from the Satake family.Teiko Tsutsumi, "Satake Clan and Hachiman Worship," {{ill|Ibaraki Prefectural History Museum Bulletin|ja|茨城県立歴史館報|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibaraki Prefectural History Museum Bulletin|qq=}} No. 28, 2001. Compiled in {{ill|Research Series on Medieval Kanto Warriors Vol. 30: Hitachi Satake Clan|ja|シリーズ・中世関東武士の研究 第三十巻 常陸佐竹氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Research Series on Medieval Kanto Warriors Vol. 30: Hitachi Satake Clan|qq=}} (Kōshōshō Publishing, 2021), pp. 192–227. Although the Satake clan was later relocated to {{ill|Akita|ja|秋田|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akita|qq=}} after the [[:en:Battle of Sekigahara|Battle of Sekigahara]], they took the shrine's deity with them to {{ill|Ōdate Castle|ja|大館城|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōdate Castle|qq=}} as its guardian spirit. The deity was subsequently enshrined at {{ill|Ōdate Hachiman Shrine|ja|大館八幡神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōdate Hachiman Shrine|qq=}}, continuing its role as a protector.{{Cite web|title=八幡神社 |url=http://akita-jinjacho.sakura.ne.jp/tatsujin_etc/kennsaku/oodate/67_hachiman_hachiman.html |publisher=Akita Prefecture Shrine Association |accessdate=2015-10-12}} In [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 14 (1609), the first [[:en:Daimyo|daimyō]] of the [[:en:Mito Domain|Mito Domain]], [[:en:Tokugawa Yorifusa|Tokugawa Yorifusa]], was healed of an illness after praying at the shrine. Thereafter, the [[:en:Mito Tokugawa family|Mito Tokugawa clan]] held the shrine in high reverence. In [[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] 5 (1692), the second daimyō, [[:en:Tokugawa Mitsukuni|Tokugawa Mitsukuni]] (popularly known as Mito Kōmon), designated the shrine as a guardian of the Ōta district. In [[:en:Hōei|Hōei]] 5 (1708), the shrine was relocated to its current location alongside the {{ill|Ōta Inari Shrine|ja|太田稲荷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta Inari Shrine|qq=}}, which had been worshiped together with it. In 1940 (Showa 15), based on the tradition that a woman named Tsuruko accompanied the deity’s transfer from Tsurugaoka Hachimangū and oversaw its rituals, the shrine's parishioner priestesses revived the "Tsuruko Dance." Recently, this [[:en:Kagura|kagura]] offering has been expanded to include performances such as {{ill|Urayasu no Mai|ja|浦安の舞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urayasu no Mai|qq=}}, {{ill|Toyo no Sakai no Mai|ja|豊栄の舞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyo no Sakai no Mai|qq=}}, and {{ill|Yaotome no Mai|ja|八乙女舞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yaotome no Mai|qq=}}, which are performed with grace during major festivals. [[File:Kaguraden of Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|thumb|right|245px|Kaguraden]] == Enshrined Deities == * '''[[Ōsasagi no Mikoto]]''' (Emperor Nintoku) * '''[[Ukanomitama no Mikoto]]''' == Festivals == * January: {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|元旦祭|Saitan-sai=Saitan-sai|lt=Saitan-sai (New Year's Festival)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}} * February: {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分追儺祭|lt=Setsubun Tsuina-sai|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setsubun|qq=}} * May: festival * June: {{ill|Nagoshi no Harae|ja|夏越の祓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoshi no Harae|qq=}} * September: Autumn Festival * December: [[:en:Ōharae-shiki|Ōharae]] == Cultural Properties == * Large [[:en:Zelkova serrata|zelkova]] tree (designated a natural monument of Ibaraki Prefecture) * Hanging scroll of Hachiman as a monk * Stone sword artifacts * Military fan (attributed to [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu]]) == Auxiliary Shrines == * {{ill|Kitano Tenjin Shrine|ja|北野天神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitano Tenjin Shrine|qq=}} (Plum Tenjin) – Established in [[:en:Kōhei|Kōhei 6]] (1062) by [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshiie|Minamoto no Yoshiie]] to pray for victory in the [[:en:Former Nine Years' War|Earlier Nine Years' War]]. * {{ill|Ichihime Shrine|ja|市比売神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichihime Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Ashio Shrine|ja|足尾神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashio Shrine|qq=}} * [[:en:Atago Shrine (Kyoto)|Atago Shrine]] * {{ill|Wakazumi Shrine|ja|別雷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakazumi Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Fuji Shrine|ja|富士神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuji Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Awashima Shrine (Wakayama)|ja|淡島神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awashima Shrine|qq=}} File:Ota-tenmangu in Wakamiya-hachimanhu (Hitachiota).jpg|Kitano Tenjin Shrine File:Keidaisha1 in Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|Ichihime Shrine, Wakazumi Shrine, Atago Shrine File:Ashio-jinja in Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|Ashio Shrine File:Fuji-jinja in Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|Fuji Shrine == Access == The shrine is approximately 1.5 km north of [[:en:Hitachi-Ōta Station|Hitachi-Ōta Station]] on the [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] (JR). It is a 20-minute walk. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:List of Shinto shrines in Japan|List of Shinto shrines in Japan]] * [[:en:List of Japanese deities|List of Japanese deities]] == External Links == * {{Instagram|wakamiyah}} * {{Twitter|wakamiya_h}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Ibaraki Prefecture Shrine Index}} {{translated page|ja|若宮八幡宮 (常陸太田市)|version=101968922|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:Satake clan]] [[Category:Architecture in Hitachiōta]] [[Category:History of Hitachiōta]] [[Category:Emperor Nintoku]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:orphaned pages as of April 8]] [[Category:Orphans as of April 16]] }} [[zh:若宮八幡宮]] [[ja:%E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E5%AE%AE_(%E5%B8%B8%E9%99%B8%E5%A4%AA%E7%94%B0%E5%B8%82)]] [[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]] [[en:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]] [[Category:仁徳天皇]] [[Category:佐竹右京大夫家]] [[Category:常陸太田市の建築物]] [[Category:常陸太田市の歴史]] [[Category:茨城県の旧郷社]] {{Commons category|Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota)}} [[ja:若宮八幡宮 (常陸太田市)]]

The shrine's Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}}. == History == The shrine was established during the [[:en:Ōei|Ōei era]] (circa 1400) by {{ill|Yoshihito Satake|ja|佐竹義人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshihito Satake|qq=}}, who invited its deity from [[:en:Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū]] in [[:en:Kamakura|Kamakura]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It was originally enshrined within {{ill|Ōta Castle (Hitachi Province)|ja|太田城 (常陸国)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta Castle (Hitachi Province)|qq=}} as a guardian deity and served as the prayer site for the [[:en:Satake clan|Satake clan]]. Although the Satake clan was later relocated to {{ill|Akita|ja|秋田|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akita|qq=}} after the [[:en:Battle of Sekigahara|Battle of Sekigahara]], they took the shrine's deity with them to {{ill|Ōdate Castle|ja|大館城|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōdate Castle|qq=}} as its guardian spirit. Thereafter, the [[:en:Mito Tokugawa family|Mito Tokugawa clan]] held the shrine in high reverence. In [[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] 5 (1692), the second daimyō, [[:en:Tokugawa Mitsukuni|Tokugawa Mitsukuni]] (popularly known as Mito Kōmon), designated the shrine as a guardian of the Ōta district. In [[:en:Hōei|Hōei]] 5 (1708), the shrine was relocated to its current location alongside the {{ill|Ōta Inari Shrine|ja|太田稲荷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta Inari Shrine|qq=}}, which had been worshiped together with it. In 1940 (Showa 15), based on the tradition that a woman named Tsuruko accompanied the deity’s transfer from Tsurugaoka Hachimangū and oversaw its rituals, the shrine's parishioner priestesses revived the "Tsuruko Dance." Recently, this [[:en:Kagura|kagura]] offering has been expanded to include performances such as {{ill|Urayasu no Mai|ja|浦安の舞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urayasu no Mai|qq=}}, {{ill|Toyo no Sakai no Mai|ja|豊栄の舞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyo no Sakai no Mai|qq=}}, and {{ill|Yaotome no Mai|ja|八乙女舞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yaotome no Mai|qq=}}, which are performed with grace during major festivals. In [[:en:Hōei|Hōei]] 5 (1708), the shrine was relocated to its current location alongside the {{ill|Ōta Inari Shrine|ja|太田稲荷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta Inari Shrine|qq=}}, which had been worshiped together with it. In 1940 (Showa 15), based on the tradition that a woman named Tsuruko accompanied the deity’s transfer from Tsurugaoka Hachimangū and oversaw its rituals, the shrine's parishioner priestesses revived the "Tsuruko Dance." Recently, this [[:en:Kagura|kagura]] offering has been expanded to include performances such as {{ill|Urayasu no Mai|ja|浦安の舞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urayasu no Mai|qq=}}, {{ill|Toyo no Sakai no Mai|ja|豊栄の舞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyo no Sakai no Mai|qq=}}, and {{ill|Yaotome no Mai|ja|八乙女舞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yaotome no Mai|qq=}}, which are performed with grace during major festivals. [[File:Kaguraden of Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|thumb|right|245px|Kaguraden]] == Enshrined Deities == * '''[[Ōsasagi no Mikoto]]''' (Emperor Nintoku) * '''[[Ukanomitama no Mikoto]]''' == Festivals == * January: {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|元旦祭|Saitan-sai=Saitan-sai|lt=Saitan-sai (New Year's Festival)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}} * February: {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分追儺祭|lt=Setsubun Tsuina-sai|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setsubun|qq=}} * May: festival * June: {{ill|Nagoshi no Harae|ja|夏越の祓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoshi no Harae|qq=}} * September: Autumn Festival * December: [[:en:Ōharae-shiki|Ōharae]] == Cultural Properties == * Large [[:en:Zelkova serrata|zelkova]] tree (designated a natural monument of Ibaraki Prefecture) * Hanging scroll of Hachiman as a monk * Stone sword artifacts * Military fan (attributed to [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu]]) == Auxiliary Shrines == * {{ill|Kitano Tenjin Shrine|ja|北野天神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitano Tenjin Shrine|qq=}} (Plum Tenjin) – Established in [[:en:Kōhei|Kōhei 6]] (1062) by [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshiie|Minamoto no Yoshiie]] to pray for victory in the [[:en:Former Nine Years' War|Earlier Nine Years' War]]. * {{ill|Ichihime Shrine|ja|市比売神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichihime Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Ashio Shrine|ja|足尾神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashio Shrine|qq=}} * [[:en:Atago Shrine (Kyoto)|Atago Shrine]] * {{ill|Wakazumi Shrine|ja|別雷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakazumi Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Fuji Shrine|ja|富士神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuji Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Awashima Shrine (Wakayama)|ja|淡島神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awashima Shrine|qq=}} File:Ota-tenmangu in Wakamiya-hachimanhu (Hitachiota).jpg|Kitano Tenjin Shrine File:Keidaisha1 in Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|Ichihime Shrine, Wakazumi Shrine, Atago Shrine File:Ashio-jinja in Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|Ashio Shrine File:Fuji-jinja in Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|Fuji Shrine == Access == The shrine is approximately 1.5 km north of [[:en:Hitachi-Ōta Station|Hitachi-Ōta Station]] on the [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] (JR). It is a 20-minute walk. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:List of Shinto shrines in Japan|List of Shinto shrines in Japan]] * [[:en:List of Japanese deities|List of Japanese deities]] == External Links == * {{Instagram|wakamiyah}} * {{Twitter|wakamiya_h}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Ibaraki Prefecture Shrine Index}} {{translated page|ja|若宮八幡宮 (常陸太田市)|version=101968922|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:Satake clan]] [[Category:Architecture in Hitachiōta]] [[Category:History of Hitachiōta]] [[Category:Emperor Nintoku]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:orphaned pages as of April 8]] [[Category:Orphans as of April 16]] }} [[zh:若宮八幡宮]] [[ja:%E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E5%AE%AE_(%E5%B8%B8%E9%99%B8%E5%A4%AA%E7%94%B0%E5%B8%82)]] [[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]] [[Category:Emperor Nintoku�]] [[Category:佐竹右京大夫家]] [[Category:常陸太田市の建築物]] [[Category:常陸太田市の歴史]] [[Category:茨城県の旧郷社]] {{Commons category|Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota)}} [[ja:若宮八幡宮 (常陸太田市)]]

{{Unreferenced|date=2019-03-12}} {{Shrine |name=Wakamiya Hachimangū (若宮八幡宮) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Wakamiyahachimaｎgu.JPG|250px]]
Main hall (September 2005) |location=[[:en:Hitachiōta, Ibaraki|Hitachiōta]], [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]] Miyamoto-chō 2344 |coordinates={{coord2|36|32|34.8|N|140|31|14.6|E|type:landmark_region:JP-08|display=inline,title|name=Wakamiya Hachimangū}} |enshrined_deities=[[Ōsasagi no Mikoto]] ([[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]])
[[Ukanomitama no Mikoto]] |established=[[:en:Ōei|Ōei era]] (circa 1400) |shrine_Rank=Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} |annual_festival=See [[#Festivals]] |main_hall= |map={{location map|Japan Ibaraki|label=Wakamiya Hachimangū|coordinates= {{coord2|36|32|34.8|N|140|31|14.6|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|32|34.8|N|140|31|14.6|E}}}} }} [[File:Torii of Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|thumb|right|245px|Torii]] [[File:Shinmon of Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|thumb|right|245px|Shinmon (gate)]] '''Wakamiya Hachimangū''' (若宮八幡宮) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Hitachiōta, Ibaraki|Hitachiōta]], [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]], Japan. The shrine's Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}}. == History == The shrine was established during the [[:en:Ōei|Ōei era]] (circa 1400) by {{ill|Yoshihito Satake|ja|佐竹義人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshihito Satake|qq=}}, who invited its deity from [[:en:Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū]] in [[:en:Kamakura|Kamakura]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It was originally enshrined within {{ill|Ōta Castle (Hitachi Province)|ja|太田城 (常陸国)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta Castle (Hitachi Province)|qq=}} as a guardian deity and served as the prayer site for the [[:en:Satake clan|Satake clan]]. Research suggests that the Satake clan initially revered a {{ill|Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman shrine|qq=}} derived from [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Iwashimizu Hachimangū]], which was the guardian shrine of the Minamoto clan in Hitachi. However, Yoshihito Satake, who had ties with the [[:en:Uesugi clan|Uesugi clan]], instead adopted the enshrinement of Tsurugaoka Hachimangū, possibly against opposition from the Satake family.Teiko Tsutsumi, "Satake Clan and Hachiman Worship," {{ill|Ibaraki Prefectural History Museum Bulletin|ja|茨城県立歴史館報|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibaraki Prefectural History Museum Bulletin|qq=}} No. 28, 2001. Compiled in {{ill|Research Series on Medieval Kanto Warriors Vol. 30: Hitachi Satake Clan|ja|シリーズ・中世関東武士の研究 第三十巻 常陸佐竹氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Research Series on Medieval Kanto Warriors Vol. 30: Hitachi Satake Clan|qq=}} (Kōshōshō Publishing, 2021), pp. 192–227. Although the Satake clan was later relocated to {{ill|Akita|ja|秋田|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akita|qq=}} after the [[:en:Battle of Sekigahara|Battle of Sekigahara]], they took the shrine's deity with them to {{ill|Ōdate Castle|ja|大館城|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōdate Castle|qq=}} as its guardian spirit. The deity was subsequently enshrined at {{ill|Ōdate Hachiman Shrine|ja|大館八幡神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōdate Hachiman Shrine|qq=}}, continuing its role as a protector.{{Cite web|title=八幡神社 |url=http://akita-jinjacho.sakura.ne.jp/tatsujin_etc/kennsaku/oodate/67_hachiman_hachiman.html |publisher=Akita Prefecture Shrine Association |accessdate=2015-10-12}} In [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 14 (1609), the first [[:en:Daimyo|daimyō]] of the [[:en:Mito Domain|Mito Domain]], [[:en:Tokugawa Yorifusa|Tokugawa Yorifusa]], was healed of an illness after praying at the shrine. Thereafter, the [[:en:Mito Tokugawa family|Mito Tokugawa clan]] held the shrine in high reverence. In [[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] 5 (1692), the second daimyō, [[:en:Tokugawa Mitsukuni|Tokugawa Mitsukuni]] (popularly known as Mito Kōmon), designated the shrine as a guardian of the Ōta district. In [[:en:Hōei|Hōei]] 5 (1708), the shrine was relocated to its current location alongside the {{ill|Ōta Inari Shrine|ja|太田稲荷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta Inari Shrine|qq=}}, which had been worshiped together with it. In 1940 (Showa 15), based on the tradition that a woman named Tsuruko accompanied the deity’s transfer from Tsurugaoka Hachimangū and oversaw its rituals, the shrine's parishioner priestesses revived the "Tsuruko Dance." Recently, this [[:en:Kagura|kagura]] offering has been expanded to include performances such as {{ill|Urayasu no Mai|ja|浦安の舞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urayasu no Mai|qq=}}, {{ill|Toyo no Sakai no Mai|ja|豊栄の舞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyo no Sakai no Mai|qq=}}, and {{ill|Yaotome no Mai|ja|八乙女舞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yaotome no Mai|qq=}}, which are performed with grace during major festivals. [[File:Kaguraden of Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|thumb|right|245px|Kaguraden]] == Enshrined Deities == * '''[[Ōsasagi no Mikoto]]''' (Emperor Nintoku) * '''[[Ukanomitama no Mikoto]]''' == {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分追儺祭|lt=Setsubun Tsuina-sai|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setsubun|qq=}} * May: festival * June: {{ill|Nagoshi no {{Unreferenced|date=2019-03-12}} {{Shrine |name=Wakamiya Hachimangū (若宮八幡宮) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Wakamiyahachimaｎgu.JPG|250px]]
Main hall (September 2005) |location=[[:en:Hitachiōta, Ibaraki|Hitachiōta]], [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]] Miyamoto-chō 2344 |coordinates={{coord2|36|32|34.8|N|140|31|14.6|E|type:landmark_region:JP-08|display=inline,title|name=Wakamiya Hachimangū}} |enshrined_deities=[[Ōsasagi no Mikoto]] ([[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]])
[[Ukanomitama no Mikoto]] |established=[[:en:Ōei|Ōei era]] (circa 1400) |shrine_Rank=Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} |annual_festival=See [[#Festivals]] |main_hall= |map={{location map|Japan Ibaraki|label=Wakamiya Hachimangū|coordinates= {{coord2|36|32|34.8|N|140|31|14.6|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|32|34.8|N|140|31|14.6|E}}}} }} [[File:Torii of Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|thumb|right|245px|Torii]] [[File:Shinmon of Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|thumb|right|245px|Shinmon (gate)]] '''Wakamiya Hachimangū''' (若宮八幡宮) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Hitachiōta, Ibaraki|Hitachiōta]], [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]], Japan. The shrine's Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. == History == The shrine was established during the [[:en:Ōei|Ōei era]] (circa 1400) by {{ill|Yoshihito Satake|ja|佐竹義人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshihito Satake|qq=}}, who invited its deity from [[:en:Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū]] in [[:en:Kamakura|Kamakura]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It was originally enshrined within {{ill|Ōta Castle (Hitachi Province)|ja|太田城 (常陸国)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta Castle (Hitachi Province)|qq=}} as a guardian deity and served as the prayer site for the [[:en:Satake clan|Satake clan]]. Research suggests that the Satake clan initially revered a {{ill|Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡宮}} derived from [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Iwashimizu Hachimangū]], which was the guardian shrine of the Minamoto clan in Hitachi. However, Yoshihito Satake, who had ties with the [[:en:Uesugi clan|Uesugi clan]], instead adopted the enshrinement of Tsurugaoka Hachimangū, possibly against opposition from the Satake family.Teiko Tsutsumi, "Satake Clan and Hachiman Worship," {{ill|Ibaraki Prefectural History Museum Bulletin|ja|茨城県立歴史館報|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibaraki Prefectural History Museum Bulletin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} No. 28, 2001. Compiled in {{ill|Research Series on Medieval Kanto Warriors Vol. 30: Hitachi Satake Clan|ja|シリーズ・中世関東武士の研究 第三十巻 常陸佐竹氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Research Series on Medieval Kanto Warriors Vol. 30: Hitachi Satake Clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Kōshōshō Publishing, 2021), pp. 192–227. Although the Satake clan was later relocated to {{ill|Akita|ja|秋田|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akita|qq=}} after the [[:en:Battle of Sekigahara|Battle of Sekigahara]], they took the shrine's deity with them to {{ill|Ōdate Castle|ja|大館城|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōdate Castle|qq=}} as its guardian spirit. The deity was subsequently enshrined at {{ill|Ōdate Hachiman Shrine|ja|大館八幡神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōdate Hachiman Shrine|qq=}}, continuing its role as a protector.{{Cite web|title=八幡神社 |url=http://akita-jinjacho.sakura.ne.jp/tatsujin_etc/kennsaku/oodate/67_hachiman_hachiman.html |publisher=Akita Prefecture Shrine Association |accessdate=2015-10-12}} In [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 14 (1609), the first [[:en:Daimyo|daimyō]] of the [[:en:Mito Domain|Mito Domain]], [[:en:Tokugawa Yorifusa|Tokugawa Yorifusa]], was healed of an illness after praying at the shrine. Thereafter, the [[:en:Mito Tokugawa family|Mito Tokugawa clan]] held the shrine in high reverence. In [[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] 5 (1692), the second daimyō, [[:en:Tokugawa Mitsukuni|Tokugawa Mitsukuni]] (popularly known as Mito Kōmon), designated the shrine as a guardian of the Ōta district. In [[:en:Hōei|Hōei]] 5 (1708), the shrine was relocated to its current location alongside the {{ill|Ōta Inari Shrine|ja|太田稲荷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta Inari Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which had been worshiped together with it. In 1940 (Showa 15), based on the tradition that a woman named Tsuruko accompanied the deity’s transfer from Tsurugaoka Hachimangū and oversaw its rituals, the shrine's parishioner priestesses revived the "Tsuruko Dance." Recently, this [[:en:Kagura|kagura]] offering has been expanded to include performances such as {{ill|Urayasu no Mai|ja|浦安の舞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urayasu no Mai|qq=}}, {{ill|Toyo no Sakai no Mai|ja|豊栄の舞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyo no Sakai no Mai|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Yaotome no Mai|ja|八乙女舞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yaotome no Mai|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 巫女舞}}, which are performed with grace during major festivals. 歳旦祭}} * February: {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分追儺祭|lt=Setsubun Tsuina-sai|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setsubun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * May: festival * June: {{ill|Nagoshi no Harae|ja|夏越の祓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoshi no Harae|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大祓}} * September: Autumn Festival * December: [[:en:Ōharae-shiki|Ōharae]] == Cultural Properties == * Large [[:en:Zelkova serrata|zelkova]] tree (designated a natural monument of Ibaraki Prefecture) * Hanging scroll of Hachiman as a monk * Stone sword artifacts * Military fan (attributed to [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu]]) == Auxiliary Shrines == * {{ill|Kitano Tenjin Shrine|ja|北野天神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitano Tenjin War]]. * {{ill|Ichihime Shrine|ja|市比売神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichihime Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Ashio It is a 20-minute walk. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:List of Shinto shrines in Japan|List of Shinto shrines in Japan]] * [[:en:List of Japanese deities|List of Japanese deities]] == External Links == * {{Instagram|wakamiyah}} * {{Twitter|wakamiya_h}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Ibaraki Prefecture Shrine Index}} {{translated page|ja|若宮八幡宮 (常陸太田市)|version=101968922|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Satake clan]] [[Category:History of Hitachiōta]] [[Category:Emperor Nintoku]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:orphaned pages as of April 8]] [[Category:Orphans as of April 16]] }} [[zh:若宮八幡宮]] [[ja:%E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E5%AE%AE_(%E5%B8%B8%E9%99%B8%E5%A4%AA%E7%94%B0%E5%B8%82)]] [[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]] [[Category:Emperor Nintoku]] [[Category:佐竹右京大夫家]] [[Category:常陸太田市の建築物]] [[Category:常陸太田市の歴史]] [[Category:茨城県の旧郷社]] {{Commons category|Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota)}} [[ja:若宮八幡宮 (常陸太田市)]]

{{Unreferenced|date=2019-03-12}} {{Shrine |name=Wakamiya Hachimangū (若宮八幡宮) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Wakamiyahachimaｎgu.JPG|250px]]
Main hall (September 2005) |location=[[:en:Hitachiōta, Ibaraki|Hitachiōta]], [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]] Miyamoto-chō 2344 |coordinates={{coord2|36|32|34.8|N|140|31|14.6|E|type:landmark_region:JP-08|display=inline,title|name=Wakamiya Hachimangū}} |enshrined_deities=[[Ōsasagi no Mikoto]] ([[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]])
[[Ukanomitama no Mikoto]] |established=[[:en:Ōei|Ōei era]] (circa 1400) |shrine_Rank=Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} |annual_festival=See [[#Festivals]] |main_hall= |map={{location map|Japan Ibaraki|label=Wakamiya Hachimangū|coordinates= {{coord2|36|32|34.8|N|140|31|14.6|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|32|34.8|N|140|31|14.6|E}}}} }} [[File:Torii of Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|thumb|right|245px|Torii]] [[File:Shinmon of Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|thumb|right|245px|Shinmon (gate)]] '''Wakamiya Hachimangū''' (若宮八幡宮) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Hitachiōta, Ibaraki|Hitachiōta]], [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]], Japan. The shrine's Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. == History == The shrine was established during the [[:en:Ōei|Ōei era]] (circa 1400) by {{ill|Yoshihito Satake|ja|佐竹義人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshihito Satake|qq=}}, who invited its deity from [[:en:Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū]] in [[:en:Kamakura|Kamakura]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It was originally enshrined within {{ill|Ōta Castle (Hitachi Province)|ja|太田城 (常陸国)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta Castle (Hitachi Province)|qq=}} as a guardian deity and served as the prayer site for the [[:en:Satake clan|Satake clan]]. Research suggests that the Satake clan initially revered a {{ill|Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡宮}} derived from [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Iwashimizu Hachimangū]], which was the guardian shrine of the Minamoto clan in Hitachi. However, Yoshihito Satake, who had ties with the [[:en:Uesugi clan|Uesugi clan]], instead adopted the enshrinement of Tsurugaoka Hachimangū, possibly against opposition from the Satake family.Teiko Tsutsumi, "Satake Clan and Hachiman Worship," {{ill|Ibaraki Prefectural History Museum Bulletin|ja|茨城県立歴史館報|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibaraki Prefectural History Museum Bulletin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} No. 28, 2001. Compiled in {{ill|Research Series on Medieval Kanto Warriors Vol. 30: Hitachi Satake Clan|ja|シリーズ・中世関東武士の研究 第三十巻 常陸佐竹氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Research Series on Medieval Kanto Warriors Vol. 30: Hitachi Satake Clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Kōshōshō Publishing, 2021), pp. 192–227. Although the Satake clan was later relocated to {{ill|Akita|ja|秋田|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akita|qq=}} after the [[:en:Battle of Sekigahara|Battle of Sekigahara]], they took the shrine's deity with them to {{ill|Ōdate Castle|ja|大館城|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōdate Castle|qq=}} as its guardian spirit. The deity was subsequently enshrined at {{ill|Ōdate Hachiman Shrine|ja|大館八幡神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōdate Hachiman Shrine|qq=}}, continuing its role as a protector.{{Cite web|title=八幡神社 |url=http://akita-jinjacho.sakura.ne.jp/tatsujin_etc/kennsaku/oodate/67_hachiman_hachiman.html |publisher=Akita Prefecture Shrine Association |accessdate=2015-10-12}} In [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 14 (1609), the first [[:en:Daimyo|daimyō]] of the [[:en:Mito Domain|Mito Domain]], [[:en:Tokugawa Yorifusa|Tokugawa Yorifusa]], was healed of an illness after praying at the shrine. Thereafter, the [[:en:Mito Tokugawa family|Mito Tokugawa clan]] held the shrine in high reverence. In [[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] 5 (1692), the second daimyō, [[:en:Tokugawa Mitsukuni|Tokugawa Mitsukuni]] (popularly known as Mito Kōmon), designated the shrine as a guardian of the Ōta district. In [[:en:Hōei|Hōei]] 5 (1708), the shrine was relocated to its current location alongside the {{ill|Ōta Inari Shrine|ja|太田稲荷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta Inari Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which had been worshiped together with it. In 1940 (Showa 15), based on the tradition that a woman named Tsuruko accompanied the deity’s transfer from Tsurugaoka Hachimangū and oversaw its rituals, the shrine's parishioner priestesses revived the "Tsuruko Dance." Recently, this [[:en:Kagura|kagura]] offering has been expanded to include performances such as {{ill|Urayasu no Mai|ja|浦安の舞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urayasu no Mai|qq=}}, {{ill|Toyo no Sakai no Mai|ja|豊栄の舞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyo no Sakai no Mai|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Yaotome no Mai|ja|八乙女舞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yaotome no Mai|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 巫女舞}}, which are performed with grace during major festivals. [[File:Kaguraden of Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|thumb|right|245px|Kaguraden]] == Enshrined Deities == * '''[[Ōsasagi no Mikoto]]''' (Emperor Nintoku) * '''[[Ukanomitama no Mikoto]]''' == Festivals == * January: {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|元旦祭|Saitan-sai=Saitan-sai|lt=Saitan-sai (New Year's Festival)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歳旦祭}} * February: {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分追儺祭|lt=Setsubun Tsuina-sai|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setsubun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * May: festival * June: {{ill|Nagoshi no Harae|ja|夏越の祓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoshi no Harae|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大祓}} * September: Autumn Festival * December: [[:en:Ōharae-shiki|Ōharae]] == Cultural Properties == * Large [[:en:Zelkova serrata|zelkova]] tree (designated a natural monument of Ibaraki Prefecture) * Hanging scroll of Hachiman as a monk * Stone sword artifacts * Military fan (attributed to [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu]]) == Auxiliary Shrines == * {{ill|Kitano Tenjin Shrine|ja|北野天神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitano Tenjin Shrine|qq=}} (Plum Tenjin) – Established in [[:en:Kōhei|Kōhei 6]] (1062) by [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshiie|Minamoto no Yoshiie]] to pray for victory in the [[:en:Former Nine Years' War|Earlier Nine Years' War]]. * {{ill|Ichihime Shrine|ja|市比売神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichihime Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Ashio Shrine|ja|足尾神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashio Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[:en:Atago Shrine (Kyoto)|Atago Shrine]] * {{ill|Wakazumi Shrine|ja|別雷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakazumi Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Fuji Shrine|ja|富士神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuji Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Awashima Shrine (Wakayama)|ja|淡島神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awashima Shrine|qq=}} File:Ota-tenmangu in Wakamiya-hachimanhu (Hitachiota).jpg|Kitano Tenjin Shrine File:Keidaisha1 in Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|Ichihime Shrine, Wakazumi Shrine, Atago Shrine File:Ashio-jinja in Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|Ashio Shrine File:Fuji-jinja in Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|Fuji Shrine == Access == The shrine is approximately 1.5 km north of [[:en:Hitachi-Ōta Station|Hitachi-Ōta Station]] on the [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] (JR). It is a 20-minute walk. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:List of Shinto shrines in Japan|List of Shinto shrines in Japan]] * [[:en:List of Japanese deities|List of Japanese deities]] == External Links == * {{Instagram|wakamiyah}} * {{Twitter|wakamiya_h}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Ibaraki Prefecture Shrine Index}} {{translated page|ja|若宮八幡宮 (常陸太田市)|version=101968922|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Satake clan]] [[Category:Emperor Nintoku]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:orphaned pages as of April 8]] [[Category:Orphans as of April 16]] }} [[zh:若宮八幡宮]] [[ja:%E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E5%AE%AE_(%E5%B8%B8%E9%99%B8%E5%A4%AA%E7%94%B0%E5%B8%82)]] [[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]] [[Category:Emperor Nintoku]] [[Category:佐竹右京大夫家]] [[Category:常陸太田市の建築物]] [[Category:常陸太田市の歴史]] [[Category:茨城県の旧郷社]] {{Commons category|Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota)}} [[ja:若宮八幡宮 (常陸太田市)]]

{{Unreferenced|date=2019-03-12}} {{Shrine |name=Wakamiya Hachimangū (若宮八幡宮) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Wakamiyahachimaｎgu.JPG|250px]]
Main hall (September 2005) |location=[[:en:Hitachiōta, Ibaraki|Hitachiōta]], [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]] Miyamoto-chō 2344 |coordinates={{coord2|36|32|34.8|N|140|31|14.6|E|type:landmark_region:JP-08|display=inline,title|name=Wakamiya Hachimangū}} |enshrined_deities=[[Ōsasagi no Mikoto]] ([[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]])
[[Ukanomitama no Mikoto]] |established=[[:en:Ōei|Ōei era]] (circa 1400) |shrine_Rank=Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} |annual_festival=See [[#Festivals]] |main_hall= |map={{location map|Japan Ibaraki|label=Wakamiya Hachimangū|coordinates= {{coord2|36|32|34.8|N|140|31|14.6|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|32|34.8|N|140|31|14.6|E}}}} }} [[File:Torii of Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|thumb|right|245px|Torii]] [[File:Shinmon of Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|thumb|right|245px|Shinmon (gate)]] '''Wakamiya Hachimangū''' (若宮八幡宮) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Hitachiōta, Ibaraki|Hitachiōta]], [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]], Japan. The shrine's Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. == History == The shrine was established during the [[:en:Ōei|Ōei era]] (circa 1400) by {{ill|Yoshihito Satake|ja|佐竹義人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshihito Satake|qq=}}, who invited its deity from [[:en:Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū]] in [[:en:Kamakura|Kamakura]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It was originally enshrined within {{ill|Ōta Castle (Hitachi Province)|ja|太田城 (常陸国)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta Castle (Hitachi Province)|qq=}} as a guardian deity and served as the prayer site for the [[:en:Satake clan|Satake clan]]. Research suggests that the Satake clan initially revered a {{ill|Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡宮}} derived from [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Iwashimizu Hachimangū]], which was the guardian shrine of the Minamoto clan in Hitachi. However, Yoshihito Satake, who had ties with the [[:en:Uesugi clan|Uesugi clan]], instead adopted the enshrinement of Tsurugaoka Hachimangū, possibly against opposition from the Satake family.Teiko Tsutsumi, "Satake Clan and Hachiman Worship," {{ill|Ibaraki Prefectural History Museum Bulletin|ja|茨城県立歴史館報|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibaraki Prefectural History Museum Bulletin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} No. 28, 2001. Compiled in {{ill|Research Series on Medieval Kanto Warriors Vol. 30: Hitachi Satake Clan|ja|シリーズ・中世関東武士の研究 第三十巻 常陸佐竹氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Research Series on Medieval Kanto Warriors Vol. 30: Hitachi Satake Clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Kōshōshō Publishing, 2021), pp. 192–227. Although the Satake clan was later relocated to {{ill|Akita|ja|秋田|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akita|qq=}} after the [[:en:Battle of Sekigahara|Battle of Sekigahara]], they took the shrine's deity with them to {{ill|Ōdate Castle|ja|大館城|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōdate Castle|qq=}} as its guardian spirit. The deity was subsequently enshrined at {{ill|Ōdate Hachiman Shrine|ja|大館八幡神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōdate Hachiman Shrine|qq=}}, continuing its role as a protector.{{Cite web|title=八幡神社 |url=http://akita-jinjacho.sakura.ne.jp/tatsujin_etc/kennsaku/oodate/67_hachiman_hachiman.html |publisher=Akita Prefecture Shrine Association |accessdate=2015-10-12}} In [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 14 (1609), the first [[:en:Daimyo|daimyō]] of the [[:en:Mito Domain|Mito Domain]], [[:en:Tokugawa Yorifusa|Tokugawa Yorifusa]], was healed of an illness after praying at the shrine. Thereafter, the [[:en:Mito Tokugawa family|Mito Tokugawa clan]] held the shrine in high reverence. In [[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] 5 (1692), the second daimyō, [[:en:Tokugawa Mitsukuni|Tokugawa Mitsukuni]] (popularly known as Mito Kōmon), designated the shrine as a guardian of the Ōta district. In [[:en:Hōei|Hōei]] 5 (1708), the shrine was relocated to its current location alongside the {{ill|Ōta Inari Shrine|ja|太田稲荷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta Inari Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which had been worshiped together with it. In 1940 (Showa 15), based on the tradition that a woman named Tsuruko accompanied the deity’s transfer from Tsurugaoka Hachimangū and oversaw its rituals, the shrine's parishioner priestesses revived the "Tsuruko Dance." Recently, this [[:en:Kagura|kagura]] offering has been expanded to include performances such as {{ill|Urayasu no Mai|ja|浦安の舞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urayasu no Mai|qq=}}, {{ill|Toyo no Sakai no Mai|ja|豊栄の舞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyo no Sakai no Mai|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Yaotome no Mai|ja|八乙女舞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yaotome no Mai|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 巫女舞}}, which are performed with grace during major festivals. [[File:Kaguraden of Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|thumb|right|245px|Kaguraden]] == Enshrined Deities == * '''[[Ōsasagi no Mikoto]]''' (Emperor Nintoku) * '''[[Ukanomitama no Mikoto]]''' == Festivals == * January: {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|元旦祭|Saitan-sai=Saitan-sai|lt=Saitan-sai (New Year's Festival)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歳旦祭}} * February: {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分追儺祭|lt=Setsubun Tsuina-sai|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setsubun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * May: festival * June: {{ill|Nagoshi no Harae|ja|夏越の祓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoshi no Harae|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大祓}} * September: Autumn Festival * December: [[:en:Ōharae-shiki|Ōharae]] == Cultural Properties == * Large [[:en:Zelkova serrata|zelkova]] tree (designated a natural monument of Ibaraki Prefecture) * Hanging scroll of Hachiman as a monk * Stone sword artifacts * Military fan (attributed to [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu]]) == Auxiliary Shrines == * {{ill|Kitano Tenjin Shrine|ja|北野天神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitano Tenjin Shrine|qq=}} (Plum Tenjin) – Established in [[:en:Kōhei|Kōhei 6]] (1062) by [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshiie|Minamoto no Yoshiie]] to pray for victory in the [[:en:Former Nine Years' War|Earlier Nine Years' War]]. * {{ill|Ichihime Shrine|ja|市比売神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichihime Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Ashio Shrine|ja|足尾神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashio Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[:en:Atago Shrine (Kyoto)|Atago Shrine]] * {{ill|Wakazumi Shrine|ja|別雷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakazumi Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Fuji Shrine|ja|富士神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuji Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Awashima Shrine (Wakayama)|ja|淡島神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awashima Shrine|qq=}} File:Ota-tenmangu in Wakamiya-hachimanhu (Hitachiota).jpg|Kitano Tenjin Shrine File:Keidaisha1 in Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|Ichihime Shrine, Wakazumi Shrine, Atago Shrine File:Ashio-jinja in Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|Ashio Shrine File:Fuji-jinja in Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|Fuji Shrine == Access == The shrine is approximately 1.5 km north of [[:en:Hitachi-Ōta Station|Hitachi-Ōta Station]] on the [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] (JR). It is a 20-minute walk. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:List of Shinto shrines in Japan|List of Shinto shrines in Japan]] * [[:en:List of Japanese deities|List of Japanese deities]] == External Links == * {{Instagram|wakamiyah}} * {{Twitter|wakamiya_h}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Ibaraki Prefecture Shrine Index}} {{translated page|ja|若宮八幡宮 (常陸太田市)|version=101968922|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Satake clan]] [[Category:Emperor Nintoku]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[zh:若宮八幡宮]] [[ja:%E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E5%AE%AE_(%E5%B8%B8%E9%99%B8%E5%A4%AA%E7%94%B0%E5%B8%82)]] [[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]] [[Category:Emperor Nintoku]] [[Category:佐竹右京大夫家]] [[Category:常陸太田市の建築物]] [[Category:常陸太田市の歴史]] [[Category:茨城県の旧郷社]] {{Commons category|Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota)}} [[ja:若宮八幡宮 (常陸太田市)]]

Main hall (September 2005) |location=[[:en:Hitachiōta, Ibaraki|Hitachiōta]], [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]] Miyamoto-chō 2344 |coordinates={{coord2|36|32|34.8|N|140|31|14.6|E|type:landmark_region:JP-08|display=inline,title|name=Wakamiya Hachimangū}} |enshrined_deities=[[Ōsasagi no Mikoto]] ([[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]])
[[Ukanomitama no Mikoto]] |established=[[:en:Ōei|Ōei era]] (circa 1400) |shrine_Rank=Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} |annual_festival=See [[#Festivals]] |main_hall= |map={{location map|Japan Ibaraki|label=Wakamiya Hachimangū|coordinates= {{coord2|36|32|34.8|N|140|31|14.6|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|32|34.8|N|140|31|14.6|E}}}} }} [[File:Torii of Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|thumb|right|245px|Torii]] [[File:Shinmon of Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|thumb|right|245px|Shinmon (gate)]] '''Wakamiya Hachimangū''' (若宮八幡宮) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Hitachiōta, Ibaraki|Hitachiōta]], [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]], Japan. The shrine's Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. == History == The shrine was established during the [[:en:Ōei|Ōei era]] (circa 1400) by {{ill|Yoshihito Satake|ja|佐竹義人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshihito Satake|qq=}}, who invited its deity from [[:en:Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū]] in [[:en:Kamakura|Kamakura]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It was originally enshrined within {{ill|Ōta Castle (Hitachi Province)|ja|太田城 (常陸国)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta Castle (Hitachi Province)|qq=}} as a guardian deity and served as the prayer site for the [[:en:Satake clan|Satake clan]]. Research suggests that the Satake clan initially revered a {{ill|Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡宮}} derived from [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Iwashimizu Hachimangū]], which was the guardian shrine of the Minamoto clan in Hitachi. However, Yoshihito Satake, who had ties with the [[:en:Uesugi clan|Uesugi clan]], instead adopted the enshrinement of Tsurugaoka Hachimangū, possibly against opposition from the Satake family.Teiko Tsutsumi, "Satake Clan and Hachiman Worship," {{ill|Ibaraki Prefectural History Museum Bulletin|ja|茨城県立歴史館報|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibaraki Prefectural History Museum Bulletin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} No. 28, 2001. Compiled in {{ill|Research Series on Medieval Kanto Warriors Vol. 30: Hitachi Satake Clan|ja|シリーズ・中世関東武士の研究 第三十巻 常陸佐竹氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Research Series on Medieval Kanto Warriors Vol. 30: Hitachi Satake Clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Kōshōshō Publishing, 2021), pp. 192–227. Although the Satake clan was later relocated to {{ill|Akita|ja|秋田|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akita|qq=}} after the [[:en:Battle of Sekigahara|Battle of Sekigahara]], they took the shrine's deity with them to {{ill|Ōdate Castle|ja|大館城|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōdate Castle|qq=}} as its guardian spirit. The deity was subsequently enshrined at {{ill|Ōdate Hachiman Shrine|ja|大館八幡神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōdate Hachiman Shrine|qq=}}, continuing its role as a protector.{{Cite web|title=八幡神社 |url=http://akita-jinjacho.sakura.ne.jp/tatsujin_etc/kennsaku/oodate/67_hachiman_hachiman.html |publisher=Akita Prefecture Shrine Association |accessdate=2015-10-12}} In [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 14 (1609), the first [[:en:Daimyo|daimyō]] of the [[:en:Mito Domain|Mito Domain]], [[:en:Tokugawa Yorifusa|Tokugawa Yorifusa]], was healed of an illness after praying at the shrine. Thereafter, the [[:en:Mito Tokugawa family|Mito Tokugawa clan]] held the shrine in high reverence. In [[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] 5 (1692), the second daimyō, [[:en:Tokugawa Mitsukuni|Tokugawa Mitsukuni]] (popularly known as Mito Kōmon), designated the shrine as a guardian of the Ōta district. In [[:en:Hōei|Hōei]] 5 (1708), the shrine was relocated to its current location alongside the {{ill|Ōta Inari Shrine|ja|太田稲荷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta Inari Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which had been worshiped together with it. In 1940 (Showa 15), based on the tradition that a woman named Tsuruko accompanied the deity’s transfer from Tsurugaoka Hachimangū and oversaw its rituals, the shrine's parishioner priestesses revived the "Tsuruko Dance." Recently, this [[:en:Kagura|kagura]] offering has been expanded to include performances such as {{ill|Urayasu no Mai|ja|浦安の舞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urayasu no Mai|qq=}}, {{ill|Toyo no Sakai no Mai|ja|豊栄の舞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyo no Sakai no Mai|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Yaotome no Mai|ja|八乙女舞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yaotome no Mai|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 巫女舞}}, which are performed with grace during major festivals. [[File:Kaguraden of Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|thumb|right|245px|Kaguraden]] == Enshrined Deities == * '''[[Ōsasagi no Mikoto]]''' (Emperor Nintoku) * '''[[Ukanomitama no Mikoto]]''' == Festivals == * January: {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|元旦祭|Saitan-sai=Saitan-sai|lt=Saitan-sai (New Year's Festival)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歳旦祭}} * February: {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分追儺祭|lt=Setsubun Tsuina-sai|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setsubun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * May: festival * June: {{ill|Nagoshi no Harae|ja|夏越の祓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoshi no Harae|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大祓}} * September: Autumn Festival * December: [[:en:Ōharae-shiki|Ōharae]] == Cultural Properties == * Large [[:en:Zelkova serrata|zelkova]] tree (designated a natural monument of Ibaraki Prefecture) * Hanging scroll of Hachiman as a monk * Stone sword artifacts * Military fan (attributed to [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu]]) == Auxiliary Shrines == * {{ill|Kitano Tenjin Shrine|ja|北野天神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitano Tenjin Shrine|qq=}} (Plum Tenjin) – Established in [[:en:Kōhei|Kōhei 6]] (1062) by [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshiie|Minamoto no Yoshiie]] to pray for victory in the [[:en:Former Nine Years' War|Earlier Nine Years' War]]. * {{ill|Ichihime Shrine|ja|市比売神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichihime Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Ashio Shrine|ja|足尾神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashio Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[:en:Atago Shrine (Kyoto)|Atago Shrine]] * {{ill|Wakazumi Shrine|ja|別雷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakazumi Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Fuji Shrine|ja|富士神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuji Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Awashima Shrine (Wakayama)|ja|淡島神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awashima Shrine|qq=}} File:Ota-tenmangu in Wakamiya-hachimanhu (Hitachiota).jpg|Kitano Tenjin Shrine File:Keidaisha1 in Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|Ichihime Shrine, Wakazumi Shrine, Atago Shrine File:Ashio-jinja in Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|Ashio Shrine File:Fuji-jinja in Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|Fuji Shrine == Access == The shrine is approximately 1.5 km north of [[:en:Hitachi-Ōta Station|Hitachi-Ōta Station]] on the [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] (JR). It is a 20-minute walk. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:List of Shinto shrines in Japan|List of Shinto shrines in Japan]] * [[:en:List of Japanese deities|List of Japanese deities]] == External Links == * {{Instagram|wakamiyah}} * {{Twitter|wakamiya_h}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Ibaraki Prefecture Shrine Index}} {{translated page|ja|若宮八幡宮 (常陸太田市)|version=101968922|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Satake clan]] [[Category:Emperor Nintoku]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[zh:若宮八幡宮]] [[ja:%E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E5%AE%AE_(%E5%B8%B8%E9%99%B8%E5%A4%AA%E7%94%B0%E5%B8%82)]] [[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]] [[Category:Emperor Nintoku]] [[Category:佐竹右京大夫家]] [[Category:常陸太田市の建築物]] [[Category:常陸太田市の歴史]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Ibaraki Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota)}} [[ja:若宮八幡宮 (常陸太田市)]]

{{Unreferenced|date=2019-03-12}} {{Shrine |name=Wakamiya Hachimangū (若宮八幡宮) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Wakamiyahachimaｎgu.JPG|250px]]
Main hall (September 2005) |location=[[:en:Hitachiōta, Ibaraki|Hitachiōta]], [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]] Miyamoto-chō 2344 |coordinates={{coord2|36|32|34.8|N|140|31|14.6|E|type:landmark_region:JP-08|display=inline,title|name=Wakamiya Hachimangū}} |enshrined_deities=[[Ōsasagi no Mikoto]] ([[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]])
[[Ukanomitama no Mikoto]] |established=[[:en:Ōei|Ōei era]] (circa 1400) |shrine_Rank=Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} |annual_festival=See [[#Festivals]] |main_hall= |map={{location map|Japan Ibaraki|label=Wakamiya Hachimangū|coordinates= {{coord2|36|32|34.8|N|140|31|14.6|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|32|34.8|N|140|31|14.6|E}}}} }} [[File:Torii of Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|thumb|right|245px|Torii]] [[File:Shinmon of Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|thumb|right|245px|Shinmon (gate)]] '''Wakamiya Hachimangū''' (若宮八幡宮) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Hitachiōta, Ibaraki|Hitachiōta]], [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]], Japan. The shrine's Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. == History == The shrine was established during the [[:en:Ōei|Ōei era]] (circa 1400) by {{ill|Yoshihito Satake|ja|佐竹義人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshihito Satake|qq=}}, who invited its deity from [[:en:Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū]] in [[:en:Kamakura|Kamakura]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It was originally enshrined within {{ill|Ōta Castle (Hitachi Province)|ja|太田城 (常陸国)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta Castle (Hitachi Province)|qq=}} as a guardian deity and served as the prayer site for the [[:en:Satake clan|Satake clan]]. Research suggests that the Satake clan initially revered a {{ill|Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡宮}} derived from [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Iwashimizu Hachimangū]], which was the guardian shrine of the Minamoto clan in Hitachi. However, Yoshihito Satake, who had ties with the [[:en:Uesugi clan|Uesugi clan]], instead adopted the enshrinement of Tsurugaoka Hachimangū, possibly against opposition from the Satake family.Teiko Tsutsumi, "Satake Clan and Hachiman Worship," {{ill|Ibaraki Prefectural History Museum Bulletin|ja|茨城県立歴史館報|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibaraki Prefectural History Museum Bulletin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} No. 28, 2001. Compiled in {{ill|Research Series on Medieval Kanto Warriors Vol. 30: Hitachi Satake Clan|ja|シリーズ・中世関東武士の研究 第三十巻 常陸佐竹氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Research Series on Medieval Kanto Warriors Vol. 30: Hitachi Satake Clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Kōshōshō Publishing, 2021), pp. 192–227. Although the Satake clan was later relocated to {{ill|Akita|ja|秋田|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akita|qq=}} after the [[:en:Battle of Sekigahara|Battle of Sekigahara]], they took the shrine's deity with them to {{ill|Ōdate Castle|ja|大館城|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōdate Castle|qq=}} as its guardian spirit. The deity was subsequently enshrined at {{ill|Ōdate Hachiman Shrine|ja|大館八幡神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōdate Hachiman Shrine|qq=}}, continuing its role as a protector.{{Cite web|title=八幡神社 |url=http://akita-jinjacho.sakura.ne.jp/tatsujin_etc/kennsaku/oodate/67_hachiman_hachiman.html |publisher=Akita Prefecture Shrine Association |accessdate=2015-10-12}} In [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 14 (1609), the first [[:en:Daimyo|daimyō]] of the [[:en:Mito Domain|Mito Domain]], [[:en:Tokugawa Yorifusa|Tokugawa Yorifusa]], was healed of an illness after praying at the shrine. Thereafter, the [[:en:Mito Tokugawa family|Mito Tokugawa clan]] held the shrine in high reverence. In [[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] 5 (1692), the second daimyō, [[:en:Tokugawa Mitsukuni|Tokugawa Mitsukuni]] (popularly known as Mito Kōmon), designated the shrine as a guardian of the Ōta district. In [[:en:Hōei|Hōei]] 5 (1708), the shrine was relocated to its current location alongside the {{ill|Ōta Inari Shrine|ja|太田稲荷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta Inari Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which had been worshiped together with it. In 1940 (Showa 15), based on the tradition that a woman named Tsuruko accompanied the deity’s transfer from Tsurugaoka Hachimangū and oversaw its rituals, the shrine's parishioner priestesses revived the "Tsuruko Dance." Recently, this [[:en:Kagura|kagura]] offering has been expanded to include performances such as {{ill|Urayasu no Mai|ja|浦安の舞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urayasu no Mai|qq=}}, {{ill|Toyo no Sakai no Mai|ja|豊栄の舞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyo no Sakai no Mai|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Yaotome no Mai|ja|八乙女舞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yaotome no Mai|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 巫女舞}}, which are performed with grace during major festivals. [[File:Kaguraden of Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|thumb|right|245px|Kaguraden]] == Enshrined Deities == * '''[[Ōsasagi no Mikoto]]''' (Emperor Nintoku) * '''[[Ukanomitama no Mikoto]]''' == Festivals == * January: {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|元旦祭|Saitan-sai=Saitan-sai|lt=Saitan-sai (New Year's Festival)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歳旦祭}} * February: {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分追儺祭|lt=Setsubun Tsuina-sai|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setsubun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * May: festival * June: {{ill|Nagoshi no Harae|ja|夏越の祓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoshi no Harae|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大祓}} * September: Autumn Festival * December: [[:en:Ōharae-shiki|Ōharae]] == Cultural Properties == * Large [[:en:Zelkova serrata|zelkova]] tree (designated a natural monument of Ibaraki Prefecture) * Hanging scroll of Hachiman as a monk * Stone sword artifacts * Military fan (attributed to [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu]]) == Auxiliary Shrines == * {{ill|Kitano Tenjin Shrine|ja|北野天神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitano Tenjin Shrine|qq=}} (Plum Tenjin) – Established in [[:en:Kōhei|Kōhei 6]] (1062) by [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshiie|Minamoto no Yoshiie]] to pray for victory in the [[:en:Former Nine Years' War|Earlier Nine Years' War]]. * {{ill|Ichihime Shrine|ja|市比売神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichihime Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Ashio Shrine|ja|足尾神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashio Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[:en:Atago Shrine (Kyoto)|Atago Shrine]] * {{ill|Wakazumi Shrine|ja|別雷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakazumi Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Fuji Shrine|ja|富士神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuji Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Awashima Shrine (Wakayama)|ja|淡島神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awashima Shrine|qq=}} File:Ota-tenmangu in Wakamiya-hachimanhu (Hitachiota).jpg|Kitano Tenjin Shrine File:Keidaisha1 in Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|Ichihime Shrine, Wakazumi Shrine, Atago Shrine File:Ashio-jinja in Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|Ashio Shrine File:Fuji-jinja in Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|Fuji Shrine == Access == The shrine is approximately 1.5 km north of [[:en:Hitachi-Ōta Station|Hitachi-Ōta Station]] on the [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] (JR). It is a 20-minute walk. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:List of Shinto shrines in Japan|List of Shinto shrines in Japan]] * [[:en:List of Japanese deities|List of Japanese deities]] == External Links == * {{Instagram|wakamiyah}} * {{Twitter|wakamiya_h}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Ibaraki Prefecture Shrine Index}} {{translated page|ja|若宮八幡宮 (常陸太田市)|version=101968922|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Satake clan]] [[Category:Emperor Nintoku]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[zh:若宮八幡宮]] [[ja:%E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E5%AE%AE_(%E5%B8%B8%E9%99%B8%E5%A4%AA%E7%94%B0%E5%B8%82)]] [[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]] [[Category:Emperor Nintoku]] [[Category:Satake Ukyō-no-Daibu Family]] [[Category:常陸太田市の建築物]] [[Category:常陸太田の Municipal History]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Ibaraki Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota)}} [[ja:若宮八幡宮 (常陸太田市)]]

{{Unreferenced|date=2019-03-12}} {{Shrine |name=Wakamiya Hachimangū (若宮八幡宮) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Wakamiyahachimaｎgu.JPG|250px]]
Main hall (September 2005) |location=[[:en:Hitachiōta, Ibaraki|Hitachiōta]], [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]] Miyamoto-chō 2344 |coordinates={{coord2|36|32|34.8|N|140|31|14.6|E|type:landmark_region:JP-08|display=inline,title|name=Wakamiya Hachimangū}} |enshrined_deities=[[Ōsasagi no Mikoto]] ([[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]])
[[Ukanomitama no Mikoto]] |established=[[:en:Ōei|Ōei era]] (circa 1400) |shrine_Rank=Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} |annual_festival=See [[#Festivals]] |main_hall= |map={{location map|Japan Ibaraki|label=Wakamiya Hachimangū|coordinates= {{coord2|36|32|34.8|N|140|31|14.6|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|32|34.8|N|140|31|14.6|E}}}} }} [[File:Torii of Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|thumb|right|245px|Torii]] [[File:Shinmon of Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|thumb|right|245px|Shinmon (gate)]] '''Wakamiya Hachimangū''' (若宮八幡宮) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Hitachiōta, Ibaraki|Hitachiōta]], [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]], Japan. The shrine's Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. == History == The shrine was established during the [[:en:Ōei|Ōei era]] (circa 1400) by {{ill|Yoshihito Satake|ja|佐竹義人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshihito Satake|qq=}}, who invited its deity from [[:en:Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū]] in [[:en:Kamakura|Kamakura]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It was originally enshrined within {{ill|Ōta Castle (Hitachi Province)|ja|太田城 (常陸国)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta Castle (Hitachi Province)|qq=}} as a guardian deity and served as the prayer site for the [[:en:Satake clan|Satake clan]]. Research suggests that the Satake clan initially revered a {{ill|Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡宮}} derived from [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Iwashimizu Hachimangū]], which was the guardian shrine of the Minamoto clan in Hitachi. However, Yoshihito Satake, who had ties with the [[:en:Uesugi clan|Uesugi clan]], instead adopted the enshrinement of Tsurugaoka Hachimangū, possibly against opposition from the Satake family.Teiko Tsutsumi, "Satake Clan and Hachiman Worship," {{ill|Ibaraki Prefectural History Museum Bulletin|ja|茨城県立歴史館報|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibaraki Prefectural History Museum Bulletin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} No. 28, 2001. Compiled in {{ill|Research Series on Medieval Kanto Warriors Vol. 30: Hitachi Satake Clan|ja|シリーズ・中世関東武士の研究 第三十巻 常陸佐竹氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Research Series on Medieval Kanto Warriors Vol. 30: Hitachi Satake Clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Kōshōshō Publishing, 2021), pp. 192–227. Although the Satake clan was later relocated to {{ill|Akita|ja|秋田|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akita|qq=}} after the [[:en:Battle of Sekigahara|Battle of Sekigahara]], they took the shrine's deity with them to {{ill|Ōdate Castle|ja|大館城|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōdate Castle|qq=}} as its guardian spirit. The deity was subsequently enshrined at {{ill|Ōdate Hachiman Shrine|ja|大館八幡神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōdate Hachiman Shrine|qq=}}, continuing its role as a protector.{{Cite web|title=八幡神社 |url=http://akita-jinjacho.sakura.ne.jp/tatsujin_etc/kennsaku/oodate/67_hachiman_hachiman.html |publisher=Akita Prefecture Shrine Association |accessdate=2015-10-12}} In [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 14 (1609), the first [[:en:Daimyo|daimyō]] of the [[:en:Mito Domain|Mito Domain]], [[:en:Tokugawa Yorifusa|Tokugawa Yorifusa]], was healed of an illness after praying at the shrine. Thereafter, the [[:en:Mito Tokugawa family|Mito Tokugawa clan]] held the shrine in high reverence. In [[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] 5 (1692), the second daimyō, [[:en:Tokugawa Mitsukuni|Tokugawa Mitsukuni]] (popularly known as Mito Kōmon), designated the shrine as a guardian of the Ōta district. In [[:en:Hōei|Hōei]] 5 (1708), the shrine was relocated to its current location alongside the {{ill|Ōta Inari Shrine|ja|太田稲荷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta Inari Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which had been worshiped together with it. In 1940 (Showa 15), based on the tradition that a woman named Tsuruko accompanied the deity’s transfer from Tsurugaoka Hachimangū and oversaw its rituals, the shrine's parishioner priestesses revived the "Tsuruko Dance." Recently, this [[:en:Kagura|kagura]] offering has been expanded to include performances such as {{ill|Urayasu no Mai|ja|浦安の舞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urayasu no Mai|qq=}}, {{ill|Toyo no Sakai no Mai|ja|豊栄の舞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyo no Sakai no Mai|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Yaotome no Mai|ja|八乙女舞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yaotome no Mai|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 巫女舞}}, which are performed with grace during major festivals. [[File:Kaguraden of Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|thumb|right|245px|Kaguraden]] == Enshrined Deities == * '''[[Ōsasagi no Mikoto]]''' (Emperor Nintoku) * '''[[Ukanomitama no Mikoto]]''' == Festivals == * January: {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|元旦祭|Saitan-sai=Saitan-sai|lt=Saitan-sai (New Year's Festival)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歳旦祭}} * February: {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分追儺祭|lt=Setsubun Tsuina-sai|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setsubun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * May: festival * June: {{ill|Nagoshi no Harae|ja|夏越の祓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoshi no Harae|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大祓}} * September: Autumn Festival * December: [[:en:Ōharae-shiki|Ōharae]] == Cultural Properties == * Large [[:en:Zelkova serrata|zelkova]] tree (designated a natural monument of Ibaraki Prefecture) * Hanging scroll of Hachiman as a monk * Stone sword artifacts * Military fan (attributed to [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu]]) == Auxiliary Shrines == * {{ill|Kitano Tenjin Shrine|ja|北野天神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitano Tenjin Shrine|qq=}} (Plum Tenjin) – Established in [[:en:Kōhei|Kōhei 6]] (1062) by [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshiie|Minamoto no Yoshiie]] to pray for victory in the [[:en:Former Nine Years' War|Earlier Nine Years' War]]. * {{ill|Ichihime Shrine|ja|市比売神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichihime Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Ashio Shrine|ja|足尾神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashio Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[:en:Atago Shrine (Kyoto)|Atago Shrine]] * {{ill|Wakazumi Shrine|ja|別雷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakazumi Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Fuji Shrine|ja|富士神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuji Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Awashima Shrine (Wakayama)|ja|淡島神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awashima Shrine|qq=}} File:Ota-tenmangu in Wakamiya-hachimanhu (Hitachiota).jpg|Kitano Tenjin Shrine File:Keidaisha1 in Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|Ichihime Shrine, Wakazumi Shrine, Atago Shrine File:Ashio-jinja in Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|Ashio Shrine File:Fuji-jinja in Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota).jpg|Fuji Shrine == Access == The shrine is approximately 1.5 km north of [[:en:Hitachi-Ōta Station|Hitachi-Ōta Station]] on the [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] (JR). It is a 20-minute walk. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:List of Shinto shrines in Japan|List of Shinto shrines in Japan]] * [[:en:List of Japanese deities|List of Japanese deities]] == External Links == * {{Instagram|wakamiyah}} * {{Twitter|wakamiya_h}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Ibaraki Prefecture Shrine Index}} {{translated page|ja|若宮八幡宮 (常陸太田市)|version=101968922|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Satake clan]] [[Category:Emperor Nintoku]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[zh:若宮八幡宮]] [[ja:%E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E5%AE%AE_(%E5%B8%B8%E9%99%B8%E5%A4%AA%E7%94%B0%E5%B8%82)]] [[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Hitachiōta)]] [[Category:Emperor Nintoku]] [[Category:Satake Ukyō-no-Daibu Family]] [[Category:Architecture of Hitachiōta City]] [[Category:Municipal History of Hitachiota]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Ibaraki Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Wakamiya-hachimangu (Hitachiota)}} [[ja:若宮八幡宮 (常陸太田市)]]
